review_title: Easy to navigate, cheap and convenient.
review_text: The BTS SkyTrain system operates two main lines which are the Sukhumvit line and Silom line. Although the lines cover a vast number of stations, they don't really go directly to a majority of the main tourist attractions like Wat Pho or The Grand Palace. I come to Bangkok often for work. For me, the BTS SkyTrain is useful to travel short distances mainly between Sukhumvit which is where I usually stay and Siam. Very cheap. The trains are air conditioned. The trains are often crowded and seats are limited, so you will most likely need to stand. Cheap to use. My main negative is that they need to incorporate contactless payment cards into the system so you can easily tap in and out. This will thus eliminate the need to handle cash and buy tickets. The lines can be long especially during peak hours if you want to purchase a ticket. Also, some of the ticket machines only take coins to purchase a ticket which is annoying. If you don't have the right number of coins, then you have to wait in line at the counter to purchase a ticket. Otherwise, the BTS SkyTrain is easy to navigate. Trains are frequent. Useful and convenient way to get around the city.
Use this communication. Simple and good. Easy and buy tickets and so on. Coming trains all the time so hardly any waiting time

If you buy a Rabbit card, you can save yourself a lot of time buying tickets, which is much more convenient.

review_title: Worst train system in the world
review_text:
This is the worst train system anywhere in the world, and a good reason for tourists to avoid Bangkok. There are a number of reasons....

1. You cannot use a credit card to top up a travel card, and they won't give you coins for the ticket machines, and there are no ATMs located nearby. They also require a passport every time you top up.
2. They discriminate against non-Thais by not giving them the senior citizen and student discounts. 
3. The trains are so packed in the mornings that you can't even get on most of the time, and they do nothing to try to ease the congestion.

Ironically, the other train line in Bangkok - the MRT - is one of the best in the world.  Everything is mentioned above is the opposite in the MRT. Try to take the MRT instead of BTS anytime you can.
The fastest means of communication is no inconvenience for foreigners to use and, above all, clean facilities are good. Except for the taxi, this is the only way.

review_title: Concerns Regarding Lack of Support and Communication
review_text:
I am very disappointed with the customer service experience regarding Rabbit Card through BTS. When attempting to call their customer service, the line keeps you waiting for an extended period and then disconnects without providing any assistance. This is extremely frustrating and unhelpful, especially for passengers who require immediate support.

I also tried reaching out via LINE regarding a lost BTS card. While the service claims to be an “automatic response system,” it is not actually automated in a way that provides clear next steps. Instead, it leads to repetitive and unclear responses, leaving customers without proper guidance.

For a service that is so essential to daily transportation in Bangkok, the lack of reliable customer support is unacceptable. Clear communication, consistent follow-up, and an effective channel to resolve urgent issues should be basic standards. I strongly urge BTS and Rabbit Card management to review their customer service processes and provide a more professional and supportive experience for passengers.

Bangkok is home for me but the BTS is ALWAYS NOT ON SCHEDULE every Saturday & Sunday. Most of the time I need to wait for almost 30 minutes to get into the train when I’m connecting my trip in Siam. It’s very disappointed with the service.

review_text: Overlooking the city, we discovered breathtaking views of skyscrapers and busy streets. It was a nice way to navigate the city while sharing crazy laughs. The comfort and speed of the train allowed us to enjoy every moment, making our exploration even more memorable.

BTS skytrain is fantastic. Built above the roads it allows for very quick and easy transport throughout the city - helping avoid the traffic jams that the taxis will experience. The trains are clean, cheap and not that crowded. Buying tickets from the ticket machines is easy - though having 100, 50 or 20 Baht notes will help. We didn’t seem to be able to use a credit card.

We bought day passes which saved us both money and time. Clean, efficient, and wonderfully cool - it's the best way to explore Bangkok. The signs are in English, making it super easy to navigate. Definitely beat taking taxis during rush hour.

My complaint is crime specific, or a scam would be more appropriate. I’ve been living in Bangkok since September of 2023 and use the BTS daily. 
Three times since September the cashier has ripped me off by shortchanging me when I had her 1k THB. All three times it’s happened at the Asok Station and all three times it’s been on a Saturday morning. This last time I gave her my only 1k Baht Bill and she shortchanged me 500 Baht.i told her of her intentional “mistake” and she acted confused, and I walked over to the Security Guard and told him what happened and he directed me to a kid in the booth working next to the girl I bought the ticket from. They checked the Security footage which is way too grainy to determine that’s it’s a 1000 bill, but the guy left nervously saying it was a 500 bill. My vision is great and there’s no way you can determine anything other than it’s me at the window and you can only tell that because of my clothing. 
They are definitely running a scam and as a White Guy in Thailand, you are a target for criminals and scams. My career involved Security Frsud and I worked in the Banking Industry for 35 years in America and was a Fortune 100 Company’s Expert Fraud Witness in Court, so I have experience investigating and knowing Fraud. 
Next time I need to bring a hidden Go Pro and record their criminal activity and bring it to the attention of Management! 
Stay vigilant when exchanging money at establishments in Bangkok!

The first step in traveling is to understand transportation. When traveling to a place for the first time, it always takes a little time to understand it. Nowadays, the popular mobile phone programs are often a good travel tool, at least in terms of arranging itineraries. Quickly plan where you want to go and how to get there.

In terms of traffic complexity, Bangkok's transportation system is surprisingly easy and simple. Airbus (BTS) is the most common transportation system for many friends who come to Bangkok. The second (BTS) route is also the most common transportation system for many tourists. Popular routes used, it can be said that sightseeing has a great advantage in these two routes.

I don’t know which airport everyone comes to Bangkok through. Now most of my friends are like me. They take the Airport Express and then transfer (BTS) route, and then go to the place they want to go, so there are usually many people. Is there a myth when choosing a hotel? Choose one near the BTS route. At least that’s the case for me. I use this way of thinking when choosing a hotel to stay in.

So I personally think that when I come to Bangkok, it is the first time to buy Airbus BTS one-day pass. Although the Rabbit Card is recommended by many Bangkok Passes, it is a lot more worry-free to use it with a one-day pass, at least it saves the time to store value. As for which coupon or transportation card is better to use, it really depends on personal choice.

When taking their Airbus transportation system, you just need to know where you are going. For example, what I always remember is one K or two K. Friends who are familiar with it probably know that I am talking about the light green one of BTS. Route, this is also the route I take most often when traveling in Bangkok. No matter how big the route is, it is not too far away from this route. It is either a light green or a dark green route.

The only thing that is special is the BTS Gold Line. This is the Airbus route to Siam City. It is also the only route where you cannot use a one-day pass. You have to buy an additional ticket. I really have a small complaint about the Airbus time of the Gold Line. The distance is a bit long, if it's not right or you're waiting for the bus.

As for the subway transportation in Bangkok, it is an unpopular transportation. I don’t know how different its transportation capacity is from Airbus. The subway carriages are really close to Airbus, but the number of passengers is really not many. I have taken the subway several times myself, mainly because There is no BTS station nearby, so I chose to take it for convenience. Generally speaking, because there are not many, the ride rate has not been high. To be honest, there are not many people on Airbus and there are often on subway.

It is more convenient to transfer with Airbus. The main thing is to go up and downstairs and look in the opposite direction. Basically, you are right as long as you are looking in the right direction. Often, it is a matter of direction. The main transfer station of BTS is Siam. Get off. It also has the most people transferring. If you find it troublesome, you can get off at this station and take their sky corridor. I personally think this is the most special place in Bangkok. You can see and understand the city all the way, even if you take a bus. It's fast, but at most it only takes you time to look at your phone while taking a ride. On foot, you can get some impressions of the city through the city scenery on the left and right sides.

If you were to compare which transportation card is easier to use when I come to Bangkok, I would actually choose the BTS one-day pass. Simply put, it saves trouble and time. After all, it takes time to queue up to recharge, so I use the Rabbit Card to recharge at BTS stations. Yes, you need to bring your ID card. It is much more convenient to buy a one-day pass. You can buy it online and pick it up at the airport counter. Take it out when you need to use it and just exchange it for a new one the next day. Even the Rabbit Card is a convenient card. There is no standard answer as to whether it is a good deal or not.

postscript:
An interesting phenomenon I have observed myself. Generally speaking, foreign tourists either buy one-way tickets or buy one-day passes. I have never seen foreign tourists using rabbit cards. Most of the ones I have seen are from Asia. Tourists like you and me may have the impression on the Internet that the holy card to come to Bangkok is the Rabbit Card. I also had this myth at first, but if you fly Airbus a lot, it is recommended to buy a one-day pass, after all, it is Airbus transportation is not particularly cheap. Even if the Rabbit Card has a limit of 200 baht, you still need to top up the value if you take a few big trips. For example, if I take more than four trips a day, I need to top up the value. Besides, I have to take the trip every day, so I might as well start with it. It is more practical to calculate the amount and then choose the tickets to buy.

To clarify:
If you buy a one-day pass at the station, it can only be used on that day. Don’t buy more just to save time to buy the next day. If you want to use it on the second day, you can buy it again if you need to. If you buy a one-day pass through an online travel agency, you can use it on different days. For example, if you buy three passes in three days, there will be no restrictions.

I went on the sky train at around 9pm local time on 16/12/23 and it was the worse experience ever. Firstly, the train station ticketing machine malfunctioned and they gave us free tickets without telling us where. Althought I felt it was rather considerate, they did not have a clear announcement of where we should alight. To add on, we asked the person giving out the tickets where we can take it to ( asoke ) which he approved. Hence, we took it to asoke. However upon arriving at asoke, the ticket did not work. After visiting customer service, the person in charge around 10pm at asoke did not even bother to help us instead asking us to top-up 48bhat per person. Upon asking for a proper understanding, we were ignored. Fearing she did not hear us, we asked again but to no avail and got ignored once more. Having no choice we paid a hefty fee of almost 2x the price. Coming from a person who comes to Bangkok often I am really dissatisfied and disappointed with such low and egoistic behaviour. Such behaviour should not be tolerated. I believe that more could be done for example, explaining it throughly rather than ignoring and calling over to the other station to confrim( Ha Yaek Lat Phroo ). After this poor experience from the BTS train which I have trusted and complimented serveral times, I have too, lost my trust in their service.

Maps and ticket purchases are also in English. There is 1 ticket machine per station with an English option and cash payment. This is easy to use.
1. Choose English in the top corner of the screen
2. Tap the screen near your destination station and then tap the station name.
3. Identify the number of tickets.
4. Feed a sufficiently large Thai banknote into the machine to pay. Your tickets and change will be provided.
5. Tap your card to enter and go up the stairs on the side of the last train station of your journey. Keep your ticket, as you will need it to exit.

review_title: Clean, Safe, Quick, But Doesn't Take Credit Cards
review_text:
Loved avoiding the crazy Bangkok traffic by using the elevated train, called the BTS.  There are two BTS lines and you need to pay by cash.  If you don't have cash or are unsure of how to use the ticket machines, use the friendly ticket agents, who are located at every station and speak English.  Retain your ticket or token as you need it to enter and exit the BTS system.  

You can use the BTS to get to the underground subway, called the MRT.  Tell the ticket agent your final stop and they can direct you.  Not that there are two BTS stations where you can connect with the the MRT, but the MRT system requires another ticket, so expect to pay twice.

review_title: Be careful at Phaya Thai station - scammed at the counter!
review_text:
Arrived in BKK today and took the skytrain from Suvarbhum Airport disembarked at Phaya Thai. 

Decided to reactivate my Rabbit card since the last time it was used was 3 years ago…handed over my Rabbit card along with a folded 100 THB. The lady at the counter took both of them and asked me for my passport to activate the card.

She then asked me whether I wanted to add money to reactivate it..told her I already gave her the 100 THB. She then denies ever receiving the money and had no choice but to give her another 100 THB. Noticed that every time she receives cash, she immediately places it in her drawer. Neat trick to confuse unsuspecting people.

God knows how many tourists she’s scammed since Thailand reopened.

Fastest way to the city for the airport. Trains run on schedule. Plan your route before you travel and make sure to observe the stops. Example Blue Line 21 connects with the airport link. But you have to exit the station and go around the corner and then up to the airport link paying a separate fare. Also when you buy your token, keep it handy at your destination.

Great to get around but so overcrowded it's almost dangerous

review_title: Cheap and easy to use for a tourist
review_text: This transport is cheap and easy to use for a tourist, if you are going to use it more than a few times get a "rabbit" pre paid travel card, makes it so much easier than buying single tickets.

You know what you're going to pay, how quickly you're going to arrive and what time you will show up at your destination. review_text: The extension over the past few years has really helped make the sky train even more wonderful than it was in the past. review_title: I love BTS! Fast, cheap, clean, comes often, goes everywhere
review_text:
Yes, since it's pandemic times, the lines are not long and the train is not full, so never an unpleasant ride. I went all over Bangkok. The ticket machines have English and are not difficult to use. Some accept bills, though only return coins. I bought an individual ticket from a person for nearly every ride and never waited in line. The air conditioner on the train was cold, but a nice break from the heat. SIgnage is easy to follow. Has escalators and elevators. Everything you'd need from a public transport system in the city.

My only minor complaints are that you have to buy a separate ticket for the airport line. And the amounts aren't rounded. (I was always paying odd amounts like 59 baht.) And there does need to be a better system for tickets if and when the huge mass of people return. For now, though BTS IS MAGIC.

review_title: Expensive and slow compared to MRT
review_text: The BTS and the MRT are 2 different companies. The BTS has stops nearly everywhere, where usually tourists are going. For example BTS Nana, BTS Surasak, BTS Ratchathewi, BTS Siam and so on. The problem that I see is, that the BTS has to many stops, is in general slower than the MRT and is in average way more expensive than the MRT.

If you plan to use the BTS while staying in Bangkok, I would suggest getting a BTS card and putting a couple of hundred Bhat on it.  You can purchase these at any BTS station.  This way, you don't have to purchase individual tickets each time.   The only downside to the BTS is that it can get very crowded.

review_title: good way to travel around Bangkok
review_text: It is relative fair price , crowded in the rush hours - morning and evening.  eating or drinking is not allow in the station.

The Skytrain was our preferred transportation for touring Bangkok.  It was speedy, highly efficient and low cost.  Quiet trains with comfortable seats and provided access to most major shopping malls and other famous destinations in Bangkok.

After living in Vancouver, Canada and experiencing the Skytrain, and also having been to New York City, I would say the BTS is extremely good. It is fast, clean, has very good AC (much needed after walking around in Bangkok), is simple to use, and very cost friendly. It costs about 40 THB, or about $2 CDN to go 6 stops. All stations are fully covered to keep you protected from the wicked rainstorms. All stations have large thermo scans to take everyone's temperature before entering (due to COVOD19), and it is mandatory for everyone to wear a mask (and they have staff to enforce it).

Probably the easiest way to get around although not the most comfortable particularly at peak times. Journeys are certainly quiet as most passengers are concentrating on their mobile phones! It's useful to have some change when buying tickets but be prepared for quite long queues at the machines and ticket offices. Prices are very reasonable.
Within the carriages are some priority seats for the elderly, pregnant or those who with very small children. However, it seems that many students in Bangkok cannot read given that in the carriages we travelled in, all these seats were occupied by young people who studiously ignored anyone with a legitimate claim to a priority seat.
Using the rail system in Bangkok is much more preferable to going by taxi...much cheaper and much quicker!
It's very good at going around the town especially during peak hours where traffic is horrible. Also, most of the shopping malls are offices that have a direct link to the building.

We took the skytrain to Bangkok's biggest Saturday market. Another VERY hot day but the market was super.  Getting there from our godson's apartment was easy by skytrain.  Simple to operate and we bought our tickets at the platform with our godson's help.
The first electric rail transportation in Thailand. Fast & Covenience Transportation System, suitable for many place in Bangkok.

review_title: Very reliable rail mass transit, easy to figure out
review_text:
Save time and get a Rabbit card first. You don't save money, but you save time at ticket machines, finding change, waiting in line, and figuring things out. Also, you can change your destination in mid-route without penalty. Just don't lose the card - or you will likely lose the value you have on it. Don't put more than THB300 on it just in case. 
BTS is efficient, much faster than taxis between stops, and has plenty of English maps/signage to help anyone figure out how to use the system. 
At certain times, the train is extremely crowded, especially at central stations like Siam. Unlike NYC, BTS cleans their trains throughout the day and sanitizes at night. Seating is very limited and riders are expected to give seating priority to monks, elderly, women, and the injured. Be respectful and follow the guidance - their culture, their rules.
There are new stations opening and a new system opening to DMK. Transfers from one system to another (e.g. BTS to MRT or ARL) usually involves a bit of a hike (up to 1KM), but there are signs.
Easy, efficient travel around large areas of the city. In other words, it's a train system that does what it was designed to do... if that's what pops your cork  - enjoy!

review_title: Clean and quick to get around Bangkok
review_text: The BTS skytrain is great to get around Bangkok. Especially considering traffic in the city and the occasional painful process of dealing with cab drivers at times. There is good security too when entering the station and it feels safe. Trains were very clean and you are not permitted to eat or drink on the train so do not think it is a good idea to grab something to eat for the journey!

review_title: No Social Distancing,  on BTS trains
review_text: The BTS trains are a vital component of Bangkok's lifestyle, but the Social Distancing measures have  to improve. I don't know how this can be done,unless they have more staff stopping people from  hurrying into the trains, especially during Rush-Hour. Although there are continuous announcements about  wearing masks, not talking,not facing each other, the insides of the trains are a bit too crowded,for comfort. The seats are not socially distanced  at all. I hope the BTS  organisation take this seriously. Thailand is lucky to be Covid-free for more than 80 days,!But this Virus just can't be taken for granted, especially  in the public transport system.

Used everyday to take me everywhere. So cheap and air con... can get busy at peak times but really good way to get around. If unsure people at station are more than helpful to assist you with what platform to get.
Easy and convienient to use, even though few stations do not have lift to access the train floors however if you do not have too much luggage, it is the fastest way to reach places around Bangkok!

review_title: Great way to get around Bangkok
review_text: The Skytrain is a great way to get around Bangkok. It is easy to use and very reasonably priced. Easy link to the Airport Rail Link too which makes travel to and from the airport really simple.

review_title: A great way to get around
review_text: If the Skytrain is going your way (the system is still somewhat limited, but growing), it is a fine way to move around in Bangkok.  We used it to get from the Central Pier to the Jim Thompson House one day, and to get from the Central Pier area to the Thai Red Cross compound (to see the terrific snake show there) another day.  Not too expensive, and the brief sojourn in the air conditioned cars can give you a break from the heat.

Quite a few steps to negotiate. lifts at some stops. Need to understand the stations/stops for where you want to go. Information signs in view. Best to get ticket from the cashier. Listen to announcements as easy to get on wrong train otherwise. Locals are helpful.

review_title: Easy to use great for getting around and connecting to airport. Much better and cheaper than taxi
review_text: Easy to use- always plenty of people around to ask for help. Great for connecting to the airport. Main station next to water taxi port.
Excellent way around Bangkok convenient way to use, well priced, air conditioned,easy to use mapping system to pick destinations

The entire transit system in Bangkok is one of the best in the world.
I typically use the BTS as my main travel mode
Cheap prices considering how far you can trzvel.
Stations located near to many tourist destinations 
Clean, fast and comfortable
Don’t be afraid to use the sky train. So easy, clean and full of smiles. It’s also a chance to be in air conditioning. Super fast service.
The pay stations all display in English and are really easy to operate. Quick comfortable and great transport

One of the best way to hop around Bangkok and avoid traffic jam. 

Easy to travel from downtown to old town across the river.
Prices were very reasonable and a good way to travel during rush hour. Staff very helpful, but you need to pay attention as the station names are not always what you expect to se.

review_title: A great way to get around in the city or to the airport
review_text:
The BTS Sky train  is a quick and convenient way for getting around Bangkok and also links up with the airport express link which runs you directly to and from the International Airport. However if you are joining up with the airport express link you do have to change lines and purchase a separate  token for the journey to the airport. 

The BTS is air conditioned and its cheap and they have now modernized the displays above the doors in the trains which clearly indicate what station you were at and the next station you're approaching.

There are security guards posted at the stations and the system runs very regularly and is convenient.

In Bangkok the traffic can get very heavy and slow down your journey considerably and the BTS trying system avoids all of the delays.

review_title: Easy and fast way to get around town
review_text: The overhead rail system is fast, easy and fairly inexpensive...stops are announced and stops in all of the popular locales
taking family to Bangkok for one week holiday, was a great experience taking BTS there to go here and there.
As a european you don't get the change much to experience a ride like this with an amazing view. Just do it when you are there.

review_title: the fast way moving in Bangkok
review_text: BTS Sky train is the fastest way to move in Bangkok.....you must avoid the peak hours(7 am to 9am,3 pm to 6pm and 8pm to 9pm)...its working until midnight....they have new stations added from the last time i visitted(3 years ago)....its expensive for Thai people(to go from 1 station to next i give 12 baht.....its like in 40 second i give 12 baht)....very cold inside BTS(strong aircondition)...the new ticket machines accept only coins(for the ticket)...so it would be good to have coins for your ticket...you can make coins in the information desks that are near the ticket machines...and be careful with your ticket...you must put exactly in the map where you go and pay the ticket...if you make mistake,you have to go information desk to pay the correct price for the stop you get down and the open the barrier to continue your way....

Everything you could hope for from public transport, but seldom received in the UK.
User friendly touch screen ticket machine if paying with coins or you can use the manned booths if you're brave.
When we were here 18 years ago it was brand new and only on the Sukhumvit road but now has 3 sides of the city covered.

review_title: Easily get around Bangkok area on the BTS Skytrain
review_text: Convenient, easy way to get around. Extends to far away locations. Only drawback - extremely crowded during certain parts of the day and standing in a very crowded space is the only option.
Great trains, and so clean it’s unreal. The uk could really take a look at this network and learn a lot. A real pleasure to use.
Typical sky train but nice, clean, and cool. Easy to navigate just remember to get your ticket and keep it to get off where you’re supposed to.

For anyone used to traveling on the London Underground or Paris Metro, the BTS and other related city metro lines still have a lot of catching up to do. But there is a lot of development happening and new stations and lines are being added all the time  The BST (sky train) is on elevated lines above ground and the MRT is underground. The lines tend to be owned by different companies so on a single journey you can’t buy a through ticket if you are using two different ones. However, you can buy a prepaid top up cards which speeds up the process. Announcements concerning the next station are in both Thai and English which is very helpful. 
Having said this the BTS and MRT are by far the quickest and easiest way to travel around the City. Trains are fairly frequent but still get crowded at times. There are less seats and more standing. 
The system is already being expanded so expect it to improve over the next few years.
A great quick cheap way to get from Ato B and not stuck in Bangkok traffic . The carriages are clean. Can be busy during rush hour

Ideal, cheap and quick . The traffic in Bangkok is bedlam and scary! We used the sky train to get from our hotel to get to the hop on hop off bus for a tour of the palace rather than using taxi or tuktuk. It’s a crazy busy place.

Station alerts in English and Thai. Nice! In peak times you are NOT allowed to wear a backpack. The Thais themselves work around this: swing it around to your front! Fast, efficient, regular, clean and safe. You think Bangkok is a 3rd world backwater? Think again!

Having been to BKK recently the one thing we realised is that the best and fastest way to travel around the city would be the Sky Train which is convenient, clean and user friendly.  The staff at the stations are very helpful and it is not difficult to work out your route and where you need to go.    If you can take the BTS then I would recommend you choose this over taxis or tuk tuks which involve a lot of traffic jams!  

Happy safe travelling people!
You can take the BTS Skytrain from the Airport all the way in to central Bangkok. This is ideal if you have multiple places to visit.

review_title: BTS is a cheap and efficient way of travelling around Bangkok
review_text: My wife and I used BTS several times to visit Icon Siam, some food festivals and just to get around cheaply.  It is safe to travel late at night as there are security officers at every station.
Great train system! Clean fast and user-friendly. Specially during rush hour this is the way to go. Amazing also how modern most stations are.

User friendly and quick transportation service.

Had a hotel walking distance to 2 stations. Used to go to jim thomson house, shopping centers and chao phraya express boats.

Clean, with AC.

review_title: Great way to get around Bangkok
review_text: Short walk from Marriott Marquis Queens Park, and gets you to most places for just a couple of bucks, we used the BTS many times. Some times very crowded suggest they employ Pushers who squeeze people in past the doors [Joke]

review_title: Great Way to get Around Bangkok
review_text: I've used BTS Skytrain off and on for a couple of decades.  It's gotten a lot busier but it remains a great way to get around Bangkok's terrible traffic.

I had a stay in Sukhumvit for 4 days and went to Chatuchak market, MBK center and Indra market by BTS. Though the BTS train is good and not very crowded like we have in India but i would say its a bit costly. If you are a group of more than 2 people i suggest you hire Grab taxi or a Grab bike. It might come out to be cheaper. The reason for BTS being a bit costly seems to be the traffic snarls they have in Bangkok. Huge traffic i must say.
We took this train everywhere. It was easy to use and the workers were very helpful for directions. Much faster than a taxi because of traffic.
A great model of a superb public transport system. Clean, polite and helpful service. Punctual, regular and the best way to avoid Bangkok's heavy traffic.

English option for buying tickets. Single ticket options are good I'd you aren't staying too long in Bangkok. Swipe/insert your coin or card and just double check which way your train is going before boarding.

Public transport, that is always busy.. even worse during peak hours. 
Early morning and late at night.. it seems about the best time to be on any public transport in Bangkok. Moderately priced, so far, I have only had need to use the system on a point to point bases.. but I have seen commuters using cards that would probably offer a greater discount.

review_title: Easy to use and very cost efficient!
review_text: The trains are great. They come very frequently and are a fraction of the cost of a taxi, plus get you where you want to go much faster than driving.

A great way to explore Bangkok the Sky train is a quick and easy way to get round. Being careful when to use. Certain times it gets very busy hot and can feel a bit daunting. It’s quick station to station but be aware the stops can be very quick you need to be by the door when ready to get off.

So simple and straight forward to use. We trained it everywhere. The taxi drivers were the most corrupt group of humans that we had ever encountered across Asia. Most refused to turn  their meter on. We then refused their service all together and opted for the train instead. It was an economical investment on our part. Highly recommend!
The BTS Skytrain is pretty easy to navigate. The map is self explanatory. And the counter desk staff are always helpful as well.
Easy to use and very good value. An excellent way to cover distance in what is otherwise an extremely congested city!
The skytrain and MRT are by far the best options for getting around. Cheap, reliable and air conditioned. Much better than sitting in traffic

A must for travel throughout Bangkok.
It dont go everywhere but it will get you clode enough to where ya want to be.
We took the train from the airport to our hotel, easy and uncomplicated for a sweaty weary traveller
 We used it daily its remarkably clean compared to the uk trains.

I love the BTS Skytrain.  It is always clean and on time. It is the best way to travel around Bangkok and the price is very reasonable.  Traffic is the city is horrible...you can’t beat using the BTS!

review_title: Great way to escape the traffic!
review_text: Why spend time in a cap taking 4 times as long to get where you need to go when the BTS is well connected, runs very frequently and is inexpensive!

Great easy transportation system.
Labelled in Thai & English. Quick clean reliable.

Links to other lines or the metro are clearly shown.
Sky train is easy to negotiate and cheap. Suggest that you download app. Tuk tuks are also fun. Ask for help if confused; staff are very helpful.

review_title: Great service hard to figure out our destination
review_text: It was a challenge to figure out which direction and how to pay. But the trains are super.I loved the way everyone lines up and waits for everyone to disembark before they get on.
A good way to get around Bangkok with ease, inexpensive and helpful to all ages, I highly recommend this way of travelling.
Inexpensive tour of the city, I used to to travel around Bangkok - a lot faster than a taxi. Less chance of being ripped off by a taxi.... as tourists have a big sign on his head.
The Skytrain is easy to use and is an easy way to get around Bangkok.  I use it and the Airport Link to get to my hotel.  The stop was only two blocks from my hotel.

With Bangkok traffic, the sky train is often quicker and easier to get to destinations. 

We enjoyed taking it particularly during peak hours. Despite its busy nature, theres AC and it always feel safe. 

Far better and cleaner than western train systems.

It's super clean and super efficient,  the areas and stations it covers are easy to follow, just remember look at the last station on the line to confirm you are going the right way.it gets very busy at morning and evening rush hours, but if you are old, you will be surprised at how many young Thais will give you their seat, with a smile.

If your in Bangkok use this service it's really good to get about.. 
sometimes a little busy but always on time.
If you really wanna keep your commitment then hop into one of the sky trains. The traffic is bad almost throughout the day and hence the best way to get around the city is to move on a BTS.

When I first started travelling on the Skytrain twenty years ago it was a joy to whizz above the crowded streets but now is anything but. Far too crowded without enough carriages mean that almost every train is packed. Unhygienic to say the least but if I were a young woman I would not want to be in such close proximity to my fellow travellers, even as an older bloke I feel uncomfortable and spend a lot of time apologising!

We used the a Skytrain twice during our stay in Bangkok. It was clean, efficient and quick. Also, very reasonably priced with great views of a Bangkok whilst travelling. Highly recommend as a way of travelling in Bangkok.

review_title: The most convenient and safe way to travel in bkk
review_text: A must use. Rabbit card should be used although you must bring a passport to get one and sometimes to top it up

The BTS Silom line was good value for us but most of the main Bangkok sites are not near any stations.  So it is a good alternative to taxis and tuk-tuks during rush hour, but only if near where you need to go.

review_title: Efficient way to travel in Bangkok
review_text: I only used the skytrain once on our last day in Bangkok but wish I had used it sooner.  So much easier than taking a taxi as Bangkok traffic is very heavy.  The trains are very clean.
Clean, efficient, cheap. My only reservation was not being able to speak Thai, but signage is in English.
An easy and cheap way of getting around Bangkok while keeping cool. The trains are also immaculate too.

review_title: 15 times card  is a option to dodge around the city
review_text: BTS has more lines covering most of the city than MRT. A 15 times BTS card costs 375B (local currency, approximately 12 US Dollars) can valid for 30 days, while you can buy a 3 day free pass (300B local currency approximately 10 US Dollars) of MRT which lines cover a portion of the city most populated area. If you don’t want spend have if half of your day time on trips of Taxi.

Economical way to get around Bangkok. Clean, efficient, well signed. If you plan on using for a few days get a rabbit card and reload as required and use again when you come again to Thailand. Good option to grab, taxis and tuk tuks. Go on a. bTS adventure hop on hop off - you can’t get lost :)
Want a quick and easy way around Bangkok... this is it . Fast , efficient and very clean . With very helpful staff , all the ticket ladies spoke English.

review_title: easy to use and get around
review_text:
If you are unsure of your destination, ask at the counter when buying your ticket - they are very helpful.
Probably one of the fastest and cheapest way to get around Bangkok on services routes.  The aircon makes it very pleasant and each destination is announced beforehand and flashed on a screen so it helps you figure out where you are.
A great way to get from one side of the city to the other.  Clean efficient train service.  We bought the one day ticket which was a great idea and very good value for money.
Great value at around £1 for a ticket you get to travel in clean, easy to use and frequent trains above the streets of Bangkok.

We always use the BTS to navigate Bangkok.  It’s reliable, air conditioned, although crowded at peak times.  Consider it as an alternative from airport as well, if you don’t have too much luggage and have a hotel close to a station.

review_title: Great way to get around bangkok - traffic is bad
review_text: Excellent service, runs every few minutes and the best way to get around bangkok. Buy your tickets from windows or machines.

review_title: Great way to get round Bangkok
review_text: Used the service to move around as the roads were manic - very cheap as well. Spoke with a member of staff to buy tickets as we were not sure where we were going - very helpful and spoke perfect English as well

review_title: The best way to get aorund Bangkok!
review_text: WE were lucky to book a hotel nera Phaya Thai station which was a very convenient stop to link with the 2 BTS lines and the airport link.  The BTS skytrains proved to be very convenient.  It took us all the way to Chatuchak for the night market on Friday and to Erawan Museum on the other end.  The interchagnge at Siam station was also very convenient as it was linked to Siam Paragon, making shopping in the area very accessible. This transport system is highly recommended.

review_title: Low cost way to explore the city
review_text: We road the BTS multiple times and found it easy, inexpensive and efficient. Often it is standing-room-only which we didn't mind. Using google maps made it easy to see the direction and stations we wanted to hit and then we would usually walk to our end destination.

The BTS Skytrain is the best choice to travel around Bangkok especially in the core business area with easy access to shopping malls such as Siam Paragon, Central World, Terminal 21 and Emquariter.

If you are staying for more than a couple of days, consider buying the Rabbit card, which is like a prepaid card and saves you having to worry about buying a ticket and the fare each time you ride the BTS.  This card can be purchased at the station and when you are done with it, the remaining money can be refunded.

review_title: Easy affordable fast travel in Bangkok
review_text:
This is the best way to get around Bangkok for many locations.  Combine it with the MRT Subway and the Airport Train (and for the adventurous the Khlong Saen Saeb Water taxis) and you can get most anywhere you want to go.

The BTS Skytrain is easy to use and will save you hours of time sitting in a taxi in traffic

review_title: The only way to travel Bangkok.
review_text: Easy to use ticket system with heaps of security. Found the Skytrain the best way to get along as the taxis took ages in the traffic. Its well set up and well worth the try.
Given the traffic on the roads the skytrain is a brilliant way of getting to the airport. Quick, frequent and cheap.
Best option to move around. Avoid rush hours and watch out for pickpocketing. Modern system and can beat the banykok traffic

review_title: A great way to get around
review_text: Cheap, clean, safe and efficient. A good way to get around Bangkok without wasting hours stuck in a traffic jam

By local standards the BTS is very expensive. There are very few lines, the trains have few carriages and far and few between; therefore they are usually packed. I think the basic design is at fault; the trains are very heavy because they use a land-based train chassis and the elevated sections probably couldn't handle heavier trains. Still, those wanting to go up and down Sukhumvit Road may want to use the BTS to avoid the horrendous traffic jams below.
Modern and he fastest and cheapest way to get around central Bangkok. Avoid peak rush hours when it can be very crowded.

The sky train wasn’t here the last time I visited Bangkok many years ago, and I have to say that it has transformed the city for me. You can now get around the city without being stuck in all the traffic, and it can take you straight to the ferry pier which is one of the best ways to get to some of the attractions.

The sky train is quick, clean and easy to use with plenty of helpful staff to help you get to your destination

review_title: An excellent way to get around Bangkok
review_text: My partner and I have been staying in Bangkok for a few days and have been using skytrain to get around where possible. We always use public transport when we are able and this is an inexpensive and comfortable way to get around. One thing to mention is to watch young Thais as they happily push older westerners out of the way in order to get a seat when its busy. Otherwise a quick, and cool, way to get around.

Trains are always on point, organised, air conditioned and just convenient. 

When it's busy, people stand in line and wait their turn. At every stop, all passengers not getting off move further away from the doors making it an easy process to hop on and off.

Definitely my favorite mode of transportation in bangkok
Our guide took us around town using sky train, subway and other suitable public transportation. The sky train connect with subway and public ferry. So you wont get struck in traffic.

BTS Skytrain is very inexpensive and a quick way to get around Bangkok.  The street traffic is usually a nightmare, so this is by far the quickest and most certainly the cheapest way to get around the city.  The signage at the stations and on the trains, as well as anouncements, are all in English.  I would recommend BTS Skytrain to all my friends and family.

We tired of the touts, taxis that won't pit the meter on, tuk tucks etc and got the sky train-it's beautifully cold, efficient and incredibly cheap, once you work it out it's faster than the roads, and all staff speak English, great system
The staff are very helpful. It s easy to travel with this train. Clean. English and thai annoucements
used this daily during our 4 day visit to Bangkok-easy to navigate and cheap to use to move from one side of this big city to another
Crowded icecold train moves you fast threw Bangkok. Together with MTR its make big part of the city reachable.  Go for it!

The Emerald Buddha cannot be missed, allow 3 hours for this incredible place. Remember to cover your shoulders & knees. I took a scarf to tie around my waist. Trousers for men & women for sale outside
Nice traveling with bts there are inside trains very clean and very useable and fares also not expensive

Without having BTS (Skytrain), movement in Bangkok would be very stressy. BTS brings you quickly in the center from one point to the other. Just be aware, at rushhour the trains are more then full and you have maybe to wait for 2-3 trains until you have space to enter.

this service is no where big enough for the amount of people that use it daily..... they say on google maps there are odd seats on each line...that will be very rare,... its a very busy service..... if you get the bts from siam it will be packed and many times you may have to wait for the next train unless you are doing the journey first thing in the morning..... if you plan on doing a lot of travel around Silom or Sukhimvit area then i may suggest you buy a Rabbit card as waiting in lines will take up a lot of your day..... the BTS does not take you to a lot of tourist areas so just google where you are going and what you want to see as the MRT may be a better option for you !!

review_title: the easiest way to get around in Bangkok
review_text: The best way to travel in Bangkok. Clean, fast, on time. Unfortunately now everyone has to wear a mask especially when its crowded.

review_title: Best way to move around Bangkok
review_text: Best way to move around Bangkok! Very well organized, fast and clear on the changes. You can purchase different kinds of tickets depending on the usage

If you're in Bangkok, forget about taxis or tuk-tuks. The only way to travel is on the sky train system, it's the fastest and by far the cheapest. 
Only one word of warning, if you're one of those people who needs personal space maybe it's not for you..
Actually, maybe Bangkok isn't for you either.

review_title: BTS and MRT are top 2 public transportation you must travel
review_text: By taking on BTS, you got to prepare your passport at first, it is required when you buy a card for taking BTS. It takes 260 Baht.

The Bankok BTS Skytrain is by far the easiest way to travel around the capital . Unlike the roads which are bumper to bumper and slow moving , the skytain runs about every 5 minutes with clean inexpensive air conditioned carriages to whisk you around the capital delivering you atvor nearby most of the popular tourist sites you might want to visit. The rail system continues to expand  i further into the suburbs with another 6 new stations opened recently. The only downside it that with so many wanting to use the system the trains can become crowded at peak times and would benefit from extra carriages being added. At present the top fare is 52 baht per person which is a great deal when compared with a taxi which charge 35 baht as soon as you get in . Definitely worth using to move quickly around the city as,an alternative to sitting in the traffic below .

With options of fully express into KL central, partial express stopping at a few stations, or stopping all stations the rail out of KL airport is fantastic. Efficient, quick, lots of trains, and good amounts of room. Getting to the airport can be quite crowded and busy.

Since I haven’t been back to Thailand in years. The BTS was sorta new to me. I had to do some YouTube studying so when I got there I wouldn’t look like a rookie. In my opinion this is the cheapest and quickest way to get around. For me I started by saving points of interest using google maps. (Yes Google Maps) When the time finally came I selected the areas I wanted to go and picked the BTS. Google maps is amazing when it comes to this. It tells you the time and correct train to take. We got to see and taste almost everything using the BTS. My only advice is that be patient during rush hour. It can get pretty crowded.

review_title: Best mode of transportation in bangkok
review_text: I use the BTS Sky train always when in bangkok i have a rabbit pass and find it very convenient.Highly recommend any visitor to use this service on your visit to bangkok.You will be glad you did.
When you are Bangkok , do not miss to use BTS to avoid bangkok's traffic .it'quite cheap and you can station every one kilometer or less .Highly recommend it!

review_title: Such a fun and efficient system!
review_text: We used BTS every single time we were in Bangkok; it is such a seamless, clear and efficient way to travel, with AC, cheap costs. Only wish it went in the older parts of the city, but if you are on the business side and shopping in one of the main malls, you can't miss it!

review_title: Can’t beat it for efficiency speed and comfort
review_text: Great way to get around the city. Cheap travel in spotless and air conditioned comfort. Here’s wishing the uk transport system was this good!
45 bhat from airport to Phaya Thai last stop , took 25 mins . Traffic is iffy in Bangkok so at least you know you’re not taking the chance if getting stuck in 90 mins of traffic
A great transport network in BKK.  Fast, efficient, timely and of course excellent value for money. Trains every few minutes and the carriages are fully air conditioned.
Bangkok BTS system is so well organized and the trains arrive very timely. Recommend taking the BTS instead of cab or grab as the traffic situation is quite bad in Bangkok

BTS in Bangkok is an efficient and safe way to travel.The ticking staff is helpful and speak English.
If you plan to make multiple day trips,consider getting a day travel pass.

review_title: Good for getting around in busy traffic
review_text: Whilst this network isn't extensive, it does get close to a number of key locations so its a fast way to get around Bangkok above the traffic. Slightly more expensive than some similar transport systems in the world but not too bad.b

Clean, efficient and a joy to use. Sure it gets packed during rush hour but thats expected. 
But I have absolutely nothing negative as about Bangkoks BTS. From its Ticketing systems and its wonderful skywalks, it is 
A joy to use.
great for taking photos of scenery, buildings etc. and the traffic below. Nothing like this in UK. So very exciting. Seems to be very efficient and clean.
The BTS is a hassle free way to travel around Bangkok save your self a fortune and get to areas without sitting in taxi,s in traffic jams the bts is so easy use .
Inexpensive quick travel!  It was a little crowded however, everyone was courteous and we made really good time from destination to destination.

review_title: Best way to travel in Bangkok
review_text:
Easy and reliable, get to your location Quick,
Using car in Bangkok cant Take hours even for a short trip.
Also cheap and safe

review_title: Easy to use and lots of English directions
review_text: We only used the sky train once to get to Asiatique and found it very easy with lots of English directions and plenty of people willing to help and give up their seat. Had some great views too

Used the sky train to get to Lumpini park. The ticket staff are friendly and helpful. get a map so that you can see where you are.  The trains are air conditioned and very clean.  There is a lot of walking and steps too, although there are lifts. In the train itself it is easy to see where you are and where you are going a lit up

review_title: Efficient and affordable way to get around
review_text: I loaded my BTS card, the staff can speak English, and able to read maps to get to where I need to go. No complaints.

review_title: BTS - The way to travel in Bangkok
review_text: The traffic in Bangkok is horrendous, so do yourself a favor and take the Skytrain to your destination! The cars are air conditioned and although seating is limited, you get to your destination stress free. Get the day pass option if you want to see several spots in the city!
Amazed by the efficiency of this service and even more impressed by the cleanliness of the trains.  Compared to our "Tube" there is no contest !

The Sky train is very easy to use, and takes a lot of stress out of getting around the city of Bangkok. 
We tried walking to some destinations, however we felt unsafe crossing some roads, the train makes life easier and quicker.

well organized. If you understand English (or Thai) the ticket machines are really easy to use. Accepts coins and notes. Probably also cards, but I did not try. Helpful staff (and travelers)( around to help you out if needed. For short rides it is not that cheap. But very convenient and no traffic jams.

review_title: All our Travels to Bangkok we use Skytrain
review_text: My partner and I have traveled enough we don’t shy away from public transport.  We actually enjoy the adventure of not really knowing if we went the right direction or not.  Skytrain however is extremely easy and nearly every time we stop to question our route someone has asked to help.  It’s much easier to get around the city this way.  Busy times of course can be tricky but sitting in traffic on the road not moving could be worse.  My partner and I travel very light with just a couple carry-ons.  If we had more luggage I would encourage us to use a taxi.  I highly recommend giving the experience a go if you haven’t already.  It’s super easy to buy a ticket at the counter for your destination.  Pay attention to the signs pointing you to the correct platform and ask if you don’t know.
A very cheap and efficient way of traveling around Bangkok, easy to use, fast and an experience in it's self!
The train and station are both very clean. Directions are in English and very easy to follow. The price is very low
The BTS is the most efficient way to see Bangkok.  The trains are crowded during rush hours, but very clean and dependable.

review_title: How else would you get around BKK?
review_text: BTS is the way to go! I have the BTS skytrain map in my phone that I refer to when wanting to figure out where things are. Taxis suffer from jams, take the skytrain instead!

The BTS covers most of the interesting regions of Bangkok. There is a train every 5 min no matter which station you are at. The trains are airconditionned and clean.
If you are planning to stay for more than 3 days, buy a rabbit card (100 baht for card creation, be sure to bring your passport) and load it up with money. Even if you don't use it all on transport, many places accept payment via the rabbit card so you can use up the credit that way.

review_title: One of the safest and easiest way to travel in BKK
review_text:
1. it's extremely clean
2. you can do some shopping for drinks, food and sometimes clothing along the different stations
3. guard at every station checking bags (slightly useless)
4. most shopping centers are attached to the station so you don't have to walk outside
5. thai locals are very polite when exiting and entering and queuing 
6. buying tickets are convenient however not all stations accept larger bills yet and you must purchase from window
7. if you are familar with the bts app, you could use that but I stick with window
8. always an escalator available for going up, cause those hot days, am i right?
Mass transit is super easy to help you get around! Went to a handful of fights with no problem. Stations workers eager to help with any questions.

Great way to get around Bangkok no traffic congestion no pollution 
Really efficient way to travel also the thais are so polite in letting people off and queuing. Cheap ad well
First time on it to go to chatuchak Market. Very easy to buy tickets from machines. Only 44 baht each way. Air conditioned and comfortable.

review_title: Most convenient and easiest way to get around Bangkok.
review_text: With great connectivity BTS saves you a lot of time. The stations and trains are veey clean. The staff on duty are ever willing to help. The maps will also direct you on how to go about using the multiple lines. Morning, evening office times and holidays can get busy.

The BTS is our transport of choice in Bangkok - forget taxis which are forever caught in traffic for hours on end. Buy a Rabbit card (you need to show your passport for ID) and then keep topping up as needed. It's fast and convenient.
BTS is very easy to use and very cheap. Machines for tickets are in English too. Can get crowded at peak times. Air conditioning throughout.

Also...if you are into urban photography you can get some great shots of the urban scenery from the BTS platforms.  My one criticism of the skytrain is that the fares for short distances are excessive.

If you’re needing to get about Bangkok then I thoroughly recommend to use the BTS skytrain. They are clean extremely well air-conditioned and very well maintained. Every time I use this system the trains are on time with no hic ups or problems. I also felt very safe and it’s easy to use with plenty of signposts for easy to understand.
Easy to use if you refer to the map before you travel. There are few maps on the stations! Easy to buy tickets. Great for visiting Sky Walk . Use Chon Nonsi station.
Great way to get around Bangkok and welcome air conditioned trains.  Can get busy at times but overall it is a good experience.

Be careful when you were sweating before. 
The aircon is unnatural adjusted to 17 degrees. You feel cold immediately. Wear some caps or and shawls.
Works perfect and is clean and save. Therefore I highly recommend to use it. Only 140 Bath for a day ticket.
This train system is very easy to work out! The stations and trains are clean. Staff are there to help so always ask questions!

The skytrain is clean, orderly and air-conditioned. The maps and announcements make it easy to travel on. The ticket booths are easy and if you have trouble at the turnstile someone comes to help straight away.

Very easy form of Transport whilst in Bangkok and very cheap too. We travelled with our 2 year old son and it was very easy with both him and his stroller. Everybody wants to make a fuss of him on the sky train which was a bonus. Just make sure you understand the different lines and you’ll be fine.
The ticket kiosks are available in English.  Fun to be above the city looking down.  Easy and cheap.  Very clean and on the newer side.   Definitely an excellent way to travel.
Easy to use and very cheap, we paid less than £2 to get to the Chao Phraya River dock for the hop on hop off boats.
Easy to manage as a tourist.   The ticket kiosks offer the maps in English.  We have ridden them several times this week with no problem.  Better than navigating the insane traffic here.

review_title: Excellent way to get around Bangkok
review_text:
Let’s start with the thing they don’t tell you, buying a Rabbit card is the best thing to do, however you will not get your deposit back and even though it’s only 100baht, they don’t tell you this when you buy it.  Takes 15 days to process and you only get 50% anyway.
Positive it’s easy way to get round, can be busy at peak times but carriages are clean and air conditioned.

review_title: right next to the Bangkok Marriott Marquis hotel
review_text: Easy access, inexpensive and fast. I ride it a lot and it is very user friendly. Every station you get off at has something fun going on. Have fun!
Our main transport choice in bustling Bangkok. Easy on easy off ... all around Bangkok, clean, efficient, and convenient.

Easy access during my stay at SLEEEP Bkk Silom..just infront of the amazing capsule hotel..u have 4 exit direct to the hotel i was staying, Silom Mall Complex, patpong n night bazaar. Every time if i come to Bangkok I will stay in this area..and perfect accessibility even to go to the riverside. Good service of BTS here always punctual.
The best way to get around bangkok, so handy! Also really cheap, get a day pass and its worth it in 1 ride!

review_title: So handy and great air con respite
review_text: Always love taking the train around Bangkok.  Ticket staff are always helpful and their english makes things so easy!

review_title: easy and safe way to travel
review_text:
for my time in Bangkok, this was the best way to travel around the city, safe, clean and fast.

also the best way to avoid the traffic of the city
We took this system to and from the chatujak market. It was clean, easy to use (plenty of help at the counter if needed) and a good means of getting across a busy city.
BTS SKyline is very easy to use as a tourist, staff were helpfull and it was cheap to travel within bangkok. local street food is amazing

review_title: Good to experience but MRT is probably more convenient.
review_text:
Most 'must dos; in Bangkok mention taking the BTS skytrain.
We rode it a number of times. 
It gets VERY crowded but is airconditioned and trains are very regular. 
I couldn't tell you how much it was but it was cheap for western standards.
On some stretches of it, you get a great view of Bangkok (if you can see through the window).
We tended to use the MRT more often as there were more conveniently located stations for most of the tourist sites we were going to but I would agree with the reviews that it is something you should give a go. :D
Average looking timely train service... It is too crowded.. But cheapest options available..procedure and tickets are simple to buy
Just about any place of interest you would fancy the BTS covers that location. Very conveniently located & clean stations. Directions & Announcements all are in English as well.
Really a great way to get around the city. The maps are easy to follow, the platforms and trains are clean.

We as family,  visited Thailand this year January.  We have done the research and figured that the best way to get around is by BTS. The cards, easily available and good priced. Well decorated maps and organized platforms. All the information is delivered in both thai and English.real time map and info regarding the current and next stop again in both languages.  I was impressed and stringly recomend to all foreign tourists to use BTS to get around Bangkok.
My favorite BTS sky train is for fastest way to get a location in Bangkok. I suggest to catch this train. Aircon is very strong inside

review_title: So easy to use and great about
review_text: Perfect and cheap transport to see and get about the city, nice aircon and can get cold on the trains but you see so much.

review_title: travel from airport in 40 mins on train
review_text: great to get around low cost and no stuck in traffic easy to get to shopping malls or visit all the sites in the city

and it’s a speedy alternative to the exceptionally bad traffic. Can get really hot in the station and inside the BTS. 
Be sure to have plenty of water to hand. 
Clean and safe to use.
Good way to get around. Easy to buy and use tickets. I did the one use tickets, and not a pass. Single use can only be used on the day purchased, just FYI

Trains are are efficient people movers, but generally very crowded, standing room only. Even when there are escalators from ground level, stairways connect to platforms. Rabbit cards are very convenient, need passport to buy one.

Like the Tube in London, but cheaper, cleaner, faster, more modern, easier to navigate, better signage, etc

OK, better than the Tube in London 🤣

BTS should be your first choice when you choose a way of transportation. With Bangkok crazy traffic comes the BTS. The only setback that you have to buy the ticket from the window if you don’t have coins to get a ticket from the machine. The stations are numbered with letters. So when you get to the window you don’t have to worry about pronounsing the station, you can just give them the number (since each station has a different fare)
Theres no better way to get around Bangkok,  We bought a day ticket for just a few pounds and used it all day.
Its not that cheap, i compare with indonesia train😅, But the train on time (late several minutes its no problem), and the people dicipline with the line

review_title: Easy way to travel around bangkok
review_text: Easy way to travel around Bangkok. Easy to buy a ticket, very nice people who help you to buy a train ticket. Trains are very clean!
We used the sky train and found it cheap, airconditioned and uncrowded.  Much better than being stuck in traffic!

Twice I have used, in 2017, and 2019. 
I was very satisfied with prices, lines and cleaning. The stations are easy to reach, connections with MRT very easy. 
I would like in my country this!

review_title: Easiest way to get around the city
review_text: Best way to beat the capital traffic and get around the city. It goes to any place you need as long as you're OK with walking a bit. Trains are cheap but still very clean and well kept

We used to travel to most of the shopping places using this transport system as it is easy to use and not much crowded. It is pocket friendly as well and there's a map that helps you understand where to get down and which way to go.

review_title: Good services should be used for transportation
review_text:
We have used this services for going from airport to hotel ambassador.
Google gave.all the details and staff was available.at each place to guide that where to go and help the tourist

Services are very good and efficient and also ac works well and it is good services and should be used extensively

Other important part is connectivity and connectivity is very good for this services 
Everyone should use this services

review_title: Great way to get around Bangkok
review_text: Great way to travel around Bangkok without having to endure the infamous heavy traffic, especially when they are building and extending BTS and MRT lines all over BKK. Nice and cool....no smell of food as you're not allowed to eat or drink on the train.It can be very crowded at peak time.To most tourists from Europe,UK ,USA and Australia etc the costs are very reasonable or quite cheap but to the Thais with average incomes, I would imagine that it's rahter expensive....too expensive.
Easy and clean    easy to avoid traffic.   Can get crowded sometimes but AC still cool!   They sell trip cards as well for the short term traveller.

review_title: A very good way to move around Bangkok
review_text: We found the Skytrain very nice to use but down rate it slightly due to large crowds during rush hours and challenges with ticketing. You do get nice views of the city as you move around on it, kind of like a mini-tour. The cars are air-conditioned and there are several stair/escalators to ascend to or descend from each station. Over Sukhumvit there are even extended elevated walkways in places to avoid having to mix into sidewalk traffic or cross streets.
As always the BTS system was core to our family visit to BKK. Fast, clean, safe, friendly and with welcomed aircon .... use it
It's the best way to travel across to avoid the busy and  jammed streets of bkk. Best way to travel to Pratunam & chatuchak weekend market

It's works very much like the London underground but it's overground. Easy to navigate and ticket machines in English with cheep fairs. Staff are very helpful should you have any problems. Recommend to use during your stay.
I fail to understand why it is so crowded. People pay to be packed together. Why not add some trains or increase the frequenscy?
We had our hotel get us prepaid Rabbit-cards before we arrived. Then we used the train whenever needed. Wonderful way to beat the traffic.
Most convenience transport mode in Bangkok. Able to travel around easily and escape the massive jam in the city. Train are always on time and clean with cooling aircond

Great service, good value for money, very clean and secure.
The spread of the skytrain in Bangkok is now such that you can get to most places using it.
The online maps compliment the clear signage and maps available at each station.
Overall we were impressed 👍

We picked up the train for a short hop.  It took me 10 minutes just to find the name of the station we were standing in.  Although there are station maps, they're aimed at knowledgeable users, not first timers.  On the positive side, the ticket agent was very helpful.
At 7pm, our train was packed, and you must squeeze in.
All in all, still much faster than city streets.

review_title: Best Way to Travel Around Bangkok During Rush Hours
review_text: BTS is by far the best way to travel around Bangkok during rush hours, PERIOD! Once I was so tired and decided to take a taxi from Siam Paragon to my hotel near Asok. It took more than 2 hours... a dreadful mistake!
So efficient, especially to the international airport. Fares very moderately priced. What’s not to like.
Super easy and cheap to use. It takes you to the mains sites in BBK and the trains have air con. It can get quite busy at rush hours.

review_title: Great way to get around, BUT....
review_text: Beware that each stop has 2 - 3 flights of stairs to climb to get to the first level, then you still have to go up after that.  The train itself is cheap, seriously crowded, but a great way to get where you're going quickly.  There are so many things to do at the stops so even if you don't have a plan it's a good way to find things.
We used the bts everyday and it was the best way to travel around bangkok and avoid the traffic on the roads. Highly recommended

review_title: Best way to get around + views of the city
review_text: Quick, efficient, and clean way to get around the city. It’s cheaper and quicker than taxis or other methods.

Bangkok BTS is the easiest way to travel around. Fairly prices and super fast, because it can take forever to cover just a small distance in taxi or other transport on road. Very well connected skyways to all big malls and buildings.

This is without a doubt the best way to get around Bangkok.  The prices are very reasonable, the trains are clean and comfy and there is not too much you can screw up since there are only a couple main lines.  We used it every day to get around and it never failed.  Much better than fighting Bangkok traffic!

review_title: Excellent way to travel fast and easy
review_text: Cheap tickets, trains every 5-10 minutes, easy and safe way to transfer all over the city. And to rest during really hot day - inside there is a nice air-condition :)
we used the BTS and was the best way to get arounfd Bangkok....easy and friendly...thanks to all the BTS staff

review_title: Only Way to Get Around BKK
review_text: Traffic Jams in Bangkok are legendary so I always plan my hotels  within walking distance of a BTS or MRT stop.  This is the easiest way of getting around BKK
Clean trains, good value, with good links to all areas within Bangkok. Staff were always willing to help.  Never had to wait long for a train.

This is such an affordable way of travelling around Bangkok but can be very busy and highly unlikely to get a seat for many/certain stretches of track. Looking at less than a £1 to go to the airport from city centre!
Modern efficient service for getting quickly across town. Easy to use ticket machines. Trains get very crowded at busy times, most of the time we were standing.

Fantastic service, it does get crowded at times but the BTS beats sitting in Bangkok traffic. Trains are clean, air-conditioned, safe, on time and at a good price.

I use the BTS when possible as it’s so reliable! Recommend using it!

A very convenient means of transportation. But always crowded. 
Also good literally to cool down     -    efficient a/c!!
Helpful staff and a good service . Clean , efficient and very easy to understand where you get on and off .

Very easy and cheap way to get around Bangkok. Staff are helpful if you get stuck but it is a simple system and very easy to understand. With a little pre planning you can really get to anywhere in Bangkok.
All trains in Bangkok are clean and super efficient, found the BTS expensive compared to other services such as the Metro
Would suggest this for anyone who needs help in traveling tips. Traveling the world is my favorite sport.

I was impressed with the cleanliness of the BTS in Bangkok. Just make sure you have an idea of were you need to go because this a big system with many routes. There were tons of workers and locals willing to help.
Using the Skytrain is almost mandatory in Bangkok: fast and efficiënt way to get around town. Really, really busy sometimes

Not going to write 200 characters on Skytrain .
Great way to travel around . Air cond and stable price using this plus the river transport best way to get around Bangkok

I found BTS to be the most convenient way to travel in Bangkok. We were 5 people and yet most of the time BTS worked cheaper than a Taxi who would negotiate rates. BTS has lifts and has wheel chair access. Cost vary from THB 20 to 40 per person. My child below 3 was not charged.
Cheap, clean and very well air conditioned. The best way to get around Bangkok. Even if you’re not near one it is probably more efficient to get a taxi/ bike to the nearest stop and go on from there.
Bts sky train is the easiest way to get around bangkok. As well the fastest way. It connects with underground metro as well.

review_title: Worth it for the A/C Alone
review_text: Can't wait for them to extend the lines further; would use this to get everywhere and avoid the Bangkok traffic if I could. Would be nice if the ticketing machines accepted credit cards or larger bills, but that would be my only complaint.

Outstanding train service, trains were clean fast, smooth riding, on time, and safe.  We used the BTS almost daily and were very pleased.  The terminals had lots of stairs, needs more escalators and elevators for people who have a difficult time walking.  Good current and up to date ticketing and turn styles.

I highly recommend using the Skytrain. It is very easy to get around. Just ask if you aren’t sure. The air conditioning is a nice break from the heat and the trains are always clean and safe. This is a much better way to get around rather than sitting in traffic.

review_title: Inexpensive and efficient way to go around Bangkok
review_text: The BTS Skytrain is the way to go around Bangkok. It is inexpensive, clean, and efficiently run. Most importantly, you can avoid the infamous Bangkok traffic congestion. However, the BTS routes are still being expanded, and the geographic coverage is still quite limited today. Also, it is quite inconvenient that you have to buy tickets with cash, though the ticket machines have good English instructions and provide changes. There ticket booths at the stations too if you don't want to use the machines. You can also buy prepaid cards that you can recharge and reuse from the ticket booth. But it is a nuisance that you need a different card for the BTS, the metros (MRT), and the airport express link.

review_title: IT WAS GREAT AT ONE TIME, BUT NO MORE.
review_text: When they first opened in 2000 it was great. Since then they have expanded the rail line, but not increased rider capacity. The result is overcrowding on the limited rail cars so that you are crammed in like large sardines in a small can. Typical greed mentality, reap the profits instead of improving service by increasing capacity.

review_title: Use Skyrail better then Taxi ( traffic jam)
review_text:
Buy a BTS one day Pass or just buy a stored value card “Rabbit card” to flask the card at turnstile 

NOTE: 
For PASS and Rabbit card are only for BTS, NOT for MTR.
Trains run at an amazing frequency. Every single train and station is super clean. The daily unlimited pass is just 140 THB - all in all, excellent value to get around most sights in BKK.

The Skytrain is a great solution, I can only recommend it. 
There are ticket machines where you can buy tickets by chosing your destination from the metro map. Be careful by some machines you can pay only with coins!
If you stay on the same line, the ticket costs 26 baht, if you change (once or more) the price is 33 baht. There are tickets available for 15-20-30 journies for a bit lower price. These tickets can be bought only at the cashier.
Be careful, there are 2 lines, which are don't belong to the BTS system, and you need to buy tickets seperately.

Easy and convenient to explore Bangkok. You won't be stuck in traffic jams as the train is above street level. Much faster and cheaper than taxis. Connections to MRT at certain stations allows full coverage of central and greater Bangkok.
Fast, easy and Very clean. We enjoyed both the metro and the skytrain in our last week in Bangkok. Gets you where you want, and on the skytrain you can even catch a glimpse of the city as well.
The best way to move in Bangkok, we try a couple of taxis but was impossible to move in the traffic. BTS is great and easy to go around.

review_title: Best way to move around in bkk
review_text: Taxis are hard to use, so I would always go for the bts, great they do also the link to the airport, very comfy

We spent 5 days in Bangkok over New Years and used the BTS sky train almost exclusively for getting around the city. You can get to almost any point of interest using the sky train and your sneakers. We only used a Grab taxi (Thailand version of Uber) one or twice during the week. It was easy to navigate, clean and we always felt safe.

review_title: Getting around Bangkok the easy way
review_text:
Bangkok is a busy, busy city. The tourist strip is the worst for traffic. To get from one point to another the best option is the BTS. It’s clean, reliable, AIRCONDITIOINED however busy as well. 
It is not so pram friendly as it could be, but the wardens there are gracious and kind, they will help if they can. 
The system is easy to use and will stop at the locations you want to see. Don’t just stay in the tourist areas though, pick a stop out of the main area and see what there is to see there. Bangkok is not just about the shopping malls. Go walk through China town from Wat Mangkon station, see the flower market from there, walk through to the next station heading west.
