review_text: Safe and fast transportation, going most of popular places and commercial malls , the price is good but if you are big numbers may be it will be expensive

review_title: Perfect way to get around Bangkok
review_text: A fast and easy away to get around Bangkok. Well connected to the MRT as well as the Airport Link. Take a day pass foe more value!
My friend and I took the BTS Skytrain to Siam. The air con was cooling. Clean and fast train ride! Recommended.

we found the train user friendly, clean and frequent enough for holiday purposes 
and absolutely wonderful to get out from the heat into the air conditioned train

Unless used during rush hour, there is always a place to sit or stand in the wagons. The stations and trains are pretty decent in terms of cleanliness and it's easy for foreigners to find their way around it. The employees don't always speak english but they do their best to help whenever they can
Train service is realiable and very cheap. All carriages were clean, but can be crowded during peak period.

It is a little expensive compared to other transportation. I prefer to use it instead of GRAB or Taxi since BTS always arrive and go on time, especially in city like Bangkok where you usually found yourself in traffic jams moving nowhere.
Its the cheapest & most convenient way if you do not have issue with the crowds. Very economical, specially travelling to the airport or from the airport.

review_title: BTS Best Way To Get Around Bangkok
review_text: The best and only way to get around Bangkok is BTS.  Best time for travel on BTS is 6am to 8am and 10am to 2pm, else it will be packed with commuter
We use to travel by the skytrain during our stay in Bangkok. It was clean and had good connection around. Easy and good option for local transit

To avoid traffic jams, I always use BTS. Especially in city centre along Sukhumvit road. I suggest to buy day pass or a card with top up as there is usually long ques at ticket booth. Recent years a bunch of new stations have opened and makes it even more convenient to get around Bangkok
Although the train we were on was rather crowded, it efficiently transported us to the places we wanted to go.
Forget taxis. They don't stop for you. They don't use meters. The traffic is horrendous. The BTS is the only way to get around. I try to avoid taxis like the plague here in BKK.

review_title: Very convenient to get to the places.
review_text: Safe and quick.way of transportation. But sometimes getting the change to purchase the tickets is difficult.
Convenient stops with plenty of trains. Carriages were very clean considering the number of users. Try to avoid the crowds at rushhour.
On rush hours or weekdays, it can be crowded. If you're in a hurry, do spare some time for waiting and squeezing yourself in the train.
its the best way to explore Bangkok and avoiding the worst traffic everyday. It has many stations that you can easily stop on your trip.

review_title: Going to find street foods in Bangkok
review_text: BTS is the best option to travel in Bangkok without having the hassle of going through bad traffic and pollution.
Great way to get around Bangkok. It's cheap and fast. Trains and stations are clean. Train cars are air conditioned.

BTS took us from one side of Bangkok to the other in just 20 minutes. This could have taken hours by road, and only cost a few Baht instead of probably THB500+ in a taxi. It also gives an overhead view of the city as the train goes along. I can't think of any other sensible way of trying to get around Bangkok. Why sit in traffic jams below? there aren't any up above where the BTS runs.
Fastest way to travel in Bangkok with all the traffic jams. Make sure to use this mode of transport.

review_title: Buy a day pass, get on and see where you end up
review_text:
The sky train is brilliant if you have one of those days with nothing planned. It’s cheap and because it’s elevated you get to see the world go by.

I spent an afternoon riding the green line, getting off at Udomsuk to look at covered markets where I took copious photographs. At another stop I walked a short distance and found fantastic food in a large open shed.

The further out the more local it became - I got a real taste of Bangkok doing this and would recommend it to anyone.

I should add that the BTS is just a good cheap way of getting around and really simple . Better than cabs. 

Be prepared for rush hour when it gets a bit cramped.

It was so easy to use most people speak English
I would recommend you to use it because the traffic is madness
Visit places of interest, meet friends, shopping medical follow up checks and enjoy Thai cuisine. Enjoyed the evening outings.

its very affordable.  At first, I hired myself a tourguide for a day as I was alone. She taught me how to use the train and other public transport (and how to not get ripped off by the taxis and tuk tuks!). After that, I was fine on my own, and was able to get to outer places, armed with my gps, tourguide map and the train. :Yes it is packed, especially around commuter work time, but I also had times when It was good. People speak english and are willing to help at the counters where you buy the tickets.

The BTS sky train is one of the cheapest way to get around Bangkok. There are two lines for now but it touches the most important places of the city. It is convenient but at times it can be very congested, so one might have to stand all the way to the destination.

review_title: Great way to travel around the city.
review_text: I purchased a 25 trip travel card when I arrived in Bangkok. It was easy to buy and made travelling around Bangkok a lot easier by avoiding the traffic jams you get with taxis and tuk tuk.

review_title: I took this train going to my hotel
review_text: It's my first time to have experience train to be honest, and it is on a foreign land lol. I can say that it was nice and the train is clean.
Great way to get around if the places you want to visit are on the route or close to it, but be warned that it is seriously packed, even on a Saturday.
Easy and convenient to get around ,staffs are friendly and helpful too.And the cost to travel around are fair and cheap.The train is clean and well maintained

I've been using the BTS for the past 20 years, it's by far the best and safest way to get around in Bangkok. It's also clean and kept in immaculate condition. The only downside is that due to the the expansion of the system it is now very crowded during rush hour. If you're in Bangkok for a few days or a regular visited like me then buy a rabbit card at the payment kiosks located at every station this will save you a lot of time queuing up at to buy a ticket.

review_title: The only way to get around!
review_text:
If you're in central Bangkok, this is the only way to get around.   It's clean, safe and efficient.
It will get you to all of the major sites.     Don't waste your time sitting in horrible traffic.   These  above ground trains run until midnight.  Take advantage of them!
Bangkok has a terrific transit system and the BTS is one of its jewels. English language friendly, if that helps however very intuitive regardless of language needs or experience with public transit.
We had a quick ride as part of a day tour around Bangkok. I’m so pleased we did! I would definitely recommend it to anyone who is visiting the city.

Overcrowded every time I used it, they should definitely run longer train units. Rabbit Card makes it easy to use, but recharging the card could be easier. Would prefer to recharge the card at a ticket vending machine instead of queuing at a counter. Preferred the Bangkok Metro instead of BTS Skytrain whenever I was able to.

review_title: Want to get around BKK fast
review_text: For speed moving around BKK, you cannot beat the BTS which also interacts with the MRT at certain stations. There is now an extensive regular frequent service which is being extended all the time. Pretty cheap travel too in air-conditioned, clean and safe carriages. The best way, if you are there for a few days, is to buy a Rabbit card at around 100B I think and load it with the value that you are going to use. If unsure load a small amount and reload as required. Otherwise, a day ticket is always good as it saves queuing for a single journey ticket. Avoid peak hours if you can, it is so very busy.
The BTS in Bangkok is fantastic. So easy to get around. Easy to purchase tickets. If your stuck there is always someone to help you.

review_title: Easy to catch and you get a local experience
review_text: We took the BTS from the connecting Airport train to Nana .  It’s air conditioned and a smooth ride.  We had one large case so that had to be carried up the stairs which was a bit of a struggle otherwise it was fine .  You can’t get ripped of by local taxi drivers if you take the train and the view over the city is great.  It’s pretty crowded so just expect to be a sardine !!
Super convenient and inexpensive, but can get really crowded. Tickets are easy to buy, everything is in Thai and English.

Very convenient to move around Bangkok with the Sky Train. Its easy as well and user friendly. Not too expensive too. The station staffs are helpful so were the Thai peiple. Ask them for help and you will get it. The train stations are strategically located besides or near shopping malls. Packed wirh commuters on Saturday afternoon.

Easy to use and get around. Stops are conveniently located at tourist attractions and many hotels. Direct link to the airport
Supor packed during busy hours. Purchas the 1 day pass i$4.00 for unlimited train rides and ease of getting tickets

Took the skytrain to various malls like Terminal 21 in Asuk stop, MBK mall and Victory Monument,  etc. Great experience and regret I didn't choose a hotel near any BTS stops. Had to Grab most of the time and the traffic was awful.

The BTS is the quickest way around Bangkok. It is well connected to the major areas of the city making it super convinient to reach any destination from major shopping malls and even night markets.

The Bangkok BTS is for me better than the hongkong MTR... my thoughts only.

review_title: A good way to get around Bangkok
review_text: Convenient in combination with MRT. Wish more lines/stations are available. Not as cheap as the river taxi.
As long as you live close to a BTS station, then everything is fantasic - commuting wise! BTS keeps a world class standard, much better than e. g. London subway

review_title: Fast, nice a/c only way to get around
review_text: The SkyTrain is fast and will allow you to bypass the miserable traffic. Single ride tickets are easy to get and there are other passes too.
About the best way to get about Bangkok. Cheap and once mastered easy to use. Trains may be packed most of the time but taxis in traffic worse.

review_title: Good way to beat Bangkok traffic
review_text: BTS Skytrains are fast, frequent, efficient, and air conditioned. The network of lines has expanded over the years so that it now covers much of the city. Connection to the subway (seperate fare) gives an even longer range. Cars can be crowded at peak times but not during most of the day or evening. Easy connection by boat to visit hotels, temples along the river, and Iconsiam shopping complex (free or very cheap fare). NOTE to buy a stored value card to use on BTS you need to pay 100 bhat (about $3.25) and show your Passport so it can be regestered to you. This is only when you first buy it and is not required to top up value later for other trips.

We recently had a 10 day stay in the executive level of the Marriott Marquee Queen’s Park hotel Bangkok from 12-22 October 2019.

It is our second visit and we have a further visit in 2 February 2020 for 7 nights.

We write to compliment your staff they were all unfailingly polite and helpful but of course we had an opportunity to get to know the staff in the executive lounge club better over ten cocktail hours and would particularly compliment Gail, Eak, Mu and Toon for their service. 
            
Charles Goh

Is what you expect from a transit system. Clean, on time, and relatively hassle free. Bit of a stair climb up and down from the stations and can be a bit crowded however gets you close to most of the popular places in Bangkok and is in expensive. If there are a couple of you, sometimes cheaper to catch a taxi, but then you would have to contend with traffic.

The Bangkok metro system (to include the BTS) is decent with one drawback. Each train line has its own ticket systems, so for example, if you are transffering between the BTS and the MTR, you need to go buy a new ticket. Otherwise, its a decent system which can get you close most places in town.
Nice, but crowdy. Fare is not that expensive, less than the popular tuktuk. Best to beat the traffic out there.
good mode of Transportation, tickets only with coins or at counter, Coverage of town could be (will be better in future), Need walk or tuktuk for last meters

It's a convenient to travel to and fro airport and city center. It took less time and money than taxis. No bargain needed. No need to be cheated by the driver. It costed us THB40 per head per adult per trip.

The SkyTrain is a very useful above ground metro train linking Bangkok south to north via Siam Square the heart of modern Bangkok. It is cheap and easy to use. Downsides are that it gets very crowded so you often have to stand, and that it is elevated so you have to walk up. Sometimes there are elevators but not always where you need them. Also, it is an ugly monstrosity that dominates the streets it is on.
Quick, easy transport around parts of the city. Nothing to get excited about... it's a city train system!

review_title: Easy way to get around BKK
review_text: Took the BTS to the King Power Building, get yourself a Rabbit card at the kiosk. You can re-load it.
It's very cheap and convenient for travelers. I highly recommend to use when there is traffic! Great job BKK!

review_title: Very cold temperature inside the train, compared to the outside
review_text: You may want to take a sweater/scarf/light jacket, because if you have been walking outside for a while and then have to travel a long way, the contrast in the temperature feels uncomfortable. Though it is not as quick as I would want it to be, it is still appeared to be a faster way of traveling, than by taxi. The personnel at the ticket desk speak English quite well, no problem to buy the ticket (if you are buying one way ticket, do not forget to return it into the slot at the exit tourniquet).
By far the easiest and fastest way around Bangkok. Just beware the train does get crowed. plus you can get to Metro stations and transfer over.
By using bts in Bangkok, you can save time as well cost . You only wait train no more than two mins .  It saves your time and cost compare to other transportoin.

review_title: The easiest way to get around
review_text: The Skytrain was worth every penny. Such an easy & convenient way to get around Bangkok. Very cheap too!!
Both the skytrain and metro are excellent means of getting around Bangkok.  Clean, easy to manage and inexpensive.
We took a short ride from one emd to other. Good view and exiting. You can see the city and understand the major places better. And most important, you get a feel of the locals

review_title: Skytrain a  great way to see the  city
review_text: There is  an extensive  network of  public transit is  Bangkok.  It  permits  one to get  around and avoid the horrendous traffic.  Its  very quick and clean.  We  went  from the Ratchechewi station  to Chang Erawan station in about 30 minutes.It  cost  about 60 baht.

This train connects to the train from/to airport to various locations within Bangkok. I was looking for ticket for the whole day for my convenience,but did not find any such ticket, I expected maps with important places marked and closest skytrain station marked but did not find it, the stations only had the skytrain maps. The only saving grace was the information center who attend to you in English. Tre skytrain is comfortable and seems well connected provided you know which place is close to which skytrain station

review_title: Nice way to avoid car traffic jams
review_text:
As of now, there are still few lines operating. Two main city lines, one to the airport and another one from the suburbs.
But many are under construction.
The two main traditional lines so called Sukhumvit and Silom lines intersect in Siam.
Another one goes to the airport, with a few stops in between.
The connection is fairly easy - cross the platform if you change for a train going in the same direction , change floors if you are going in the opposite direction.
Trains are air conditioned and very well equipped in terms of handles or bars to hold on. Each station is announced at least two times on the speaker system.

The problems are that it can get overly crowded at rush hours. The lines were extended 4 years ago going into the suburbs. It took close to a year to go from 3 to 4 cars per train. One line got extended again and there are still 4 cars, whereas the platform could accommodate 6. No consideration for the customer. 
Price is steep. Students get a break, but extremely small. Senior citizens also get a break.... but only Thai nationals!! 

If two or more persons are travelling, a taxi is more than likely cheaper and will take you door to door.

Overall a great way to move around if on a schedule, but would require a change in management to a more consumer/customer oriented staff.

Great way to get around Bangkok, nice, clean and reliable. Has Air con on the train itself and lots of maps to explain where you are and where you are going. 
Very easy to use, similar to London Underground
Great views as you are travelling and lots of windows to see the whole of Bangkok!

The in itself superior skytrain is losing customers simply because they can not get in. Reverted to MRT instead but that is not an option for all destinations however.

Please BTS make te trains longer

Does what it says on the tin - very simple and quick way to travel round the city. We found taxis were about the same value for a family of four travelling short distances but by skipping the traffic this was far more relaxed. Easy, self-explanatory ticket purchase and very easy to follow route maps and station call outs. Believe you can save time and money by buying pay as you go tickets in advance.

review_title: A great and affordable mode of transport right in the heart of Bangkok
review_text: There is no better way to get around Bangkok, as it could take over an hour to move 1km in the city by car at any given time of the day. It's safe. It's reasonably inexpensive. It's clean. Its fast & efficient. What more cpuld a traveller ask for?

review_title: Great Way to Travel, However Many Steps!
review_text:
For travelling it is an option however some stations do not have escalators - so many steps to climb to get to or from the train. Very crowded during peak times and rarely the opportunity to get a seat.  The distance to get to a station versus hailing a cab 🚕 needs to be calculated. 
If you are claustrophobic this is not for you during peak times.
First time using the sky train. Was very easy to use and the ticket staff are most helpful. Will use this again in the future.

cool a/c,clean ,on time. You should spare time if you take bts during7-9 am working day coz super crowd and no space for backpack or big luggage.BTS is not allowed to keep backpack on your back in crowd condition
A goid alternative to taxi and tuk ruk. Sometimes very crowded. No chance to buy a ticket at machines with paper bills. It could be felt as to cold. Other might enjoy the cooling down f a while.
Good service to opt for, slightly expansive though. But you don't have more options as Taxi's are more expensive

We used the BTS train a couple of times, and found them to be really reliable and efficient.

Being from Australia, we're used to trains that are late, heavily graffitied, and unsure where the doors will end up at the platform.

Well, Bangkok's BTS trains are the opposite - they arrive on time, they are clean, and they even have the spots to stand on the platform AND the train actually stops in the right spot!

And very cheap to use too.

review_title: Good way to travel in Bangkok
review_text:
Modern aircon trains with added stations (recently) to cover most of the thaï capital. Need to buy single tickets at each station (lw price)
Downside: often crowded with people, need to stand and wear your backpack in front of you. Check local station to see the latest opened stations and access to main tourist attractions (indications are usually good)

review_title: Excellent trip on the BTS that saves time and money!
review_text: I’ve always preferred to travel by skytrains in countries that have a great system with up to date networks that save a lot of time and money. The BTS in Bangkok is just amazing and I had used it over 20 times during the 1 month I stayed in Bangkok as a tourist! Definitely recommend it to everyone going to Bangkok
Amazing experience! You will never get lost here as everyone speaks English. Very inexpensive, saves you time as during the day there is a terrible traffic on the highway.

review_title: First class public transport in Bangkok
review_text: My wife and I are here for a short weekend vacation via BKK airport and staying at Asia Hotel. Plan to visit the usual tourist landmarks (not the temples and palaces though because we have plenty of that in Malaysia too 😅). So we know that Bangkok's traffic is the worst in the world, we opt to use BTS here. We bought a Rabbit Card (prepaid card that can be reloaded) through Klook. Costs us 200 baht. We used it to go around Bangkok. All main shopping mall has its own stop. As for Chatuchak Weekwnd Market, we can go off at Mo Chit and it's just a short walk. And we also use BTS to go to Asiatique Riverfront. Just take the south line BTS from Siam or National Stadium to S6 Saphan Taksin. And there will be a free Asiatique shuttle ferry there (the clearly marked red long boat). For Sukhumvit line, all 5 first stops are at a shopping mall such as Siam Paragon, Central World, Nana, Terminal 21. So expect a very large crowd on BTS. To take ARL to the airport, the interchange is at Phaya Thai stop.
It seems only has a relatively limited number of routes but they work very well. One goes straight to the MBK department store, best choice for food and gift bargains in bangkok bar none.

review_title: The best Way to go in town!
review_text: The best public Transport to go in town.You can reach to the center in 20 minute.But it is very busy and  full in rush hour.
Easy, convenient, cool, good temperature, clean, punctual, new, smart, elevator, connecting to all part of bkk
easy to use, not expensive. Cuts the traffic jams. Air conditioned. Should have more lines for better coverage.
Very busy and a little expensive. With little info on where to go once you arrive at a stop. The staff were blunt and not friendly at all
Skytrain is the quickest, cheapest and easiest way to travel in the center. Just remember to check which line and right direction.

We travelled from Phuket to Bangkok and used this service to Nana where our hotel was. We were BTS virgins and had 3 suitcases. It was rush hour and busy but we still managed to get on board without any hassle.

Although the track is limited I understand that they are planning to expand.
It is very fast, cheap and easy to get around. The staff are helpful if you don’t speak the language. Security is high but that is to be expected.
A rabbit card will get you on the bus or sky train. Load the card then keep travelling.

The biggest difficulty in Bangkok is traffic jam.
If you don't have time, I recommend traveling by BTS.
The price is higher than the bus, but it is quite convenient to go to the center of city.

We bought "rabbit"card from the Bts ticket office and loaded an additional 100 bahts for the usage of the sky train (normal cost per station approximately 19 bahts.The bts is very user friendly,it literally shows you where you are and what is the next station.For example if you are in Nana station in Sukhumvit ,then it will be E3 ,and one can basically see which station to drop off or get one via the Bts map.Highly recommended especially because of the bangkok traffic ,and also it is airconditioned.The ticket sellers are generally quite proficient in english.

Biggest plus point is you beat the traffic. This alone is incentive for me to take the skytrian on my every visit to Bangkok. Add to the fact that if you travelling alone or as a couple its an cheaper option too. 
If there are more of you then i guess you split the cab fare and that works out well too, for a local twist you can  try the Tuk Tuk as well.
Other than the distance to and from the station I see no reason why not to travel by the sky train.

review_title: Excellent, easy and convenient way to travel
review_text: The sky train is a great way to get around Bangkok and avoid being stuck in horrible traffic jams. The train system is very easy to figure out. If you are going to be in Bangkok for several days recommend buying a rabbit at station and loading money on card so you don’t have to go to kiosk every time you get on sky train. You will need to have passport to purchase card, this is new for security purposes. Highly recommend moving around Bangkok this way.

review_title: Another choice of travel mode in Bangkok
review_text: The lines are now expanding and cover more of outer Bangkok. So, it's quite a good choice to travel in Bangkok. Avoid the rush hours and you will find it a very convenient way to travel.
Although you can buy tickets from a machine, you can also get them easily from the kiosk. Maps are simple to follow. Very crowded but organised.
The proximity to our hotel was perfect.  Use of the skytrain to get around is a must.  So easy and fast.
Perfect to hop around. Use a smartphone with a local sim card, and you can go wherever you want in Bangkok! Also good for going from and to the airport.

Once I got the hang of this good train system I was sorted travelling around. Just walk up to the window and tell them what stop you are going to and get a one way ticket. Normally the train was squishy so be prepared to get close to people.
Sky train in BKK is a good option as traffics are so bad in BKK . Rush hour on the train is packed so be aware and ready to be a sardine

review_title: Cheap and it beats the traffic
review_text: If you need to get through the main touristy areas of Bangkok then BTS is the way to travel. It does get very crowded but it's fully air conditioned to escape the heat. Always carry coins as the ticket machines aren't fully updated to accept notes at all stations.

review_title: Quick and easy way to get around Bangkok
review_text: Trying to get around on the roads of Bangkok, particularly at rush hour is a serious challenge so the sky train is a great option.  Just 2 lines so very easy compared to some cities' systems it is really well airconditioned so even when packed it can be more pleasant than being out on the street!
I can only speak from our experience but we were very impressed with the sky train service. It was clean and easy to navigate, the lines being well signposted in both Thai and English.

review_title: Very convenient way to get anywhere in Bangkok
review_text: Taxis are so cheap it sometimes isn't worth it in groups, but super quick and easy easy to get round

review_title: Convenient to travel within Bangkok, but not for everywhere!
review_text: This train is above ground train system that is very convenient if you know where to go. It stops at Siem Paragon which is the most popular shopping place in Bangkok. But, it has limited location. The train and ride is similar, however it is nothing like the subway system in Seoul, Tokyo and Manhattan. It has very limited locations compare to other big cities. For city tour, I would recommend Hop on/off tour.

review_title: Super convenient way to get around Bangkok
review_text: The BTS is an excellent way of travelling in Bangkok. Beats the roads any day! Trains do get very crowded in peak hour though and suitcases/big bags cant really be taken on board. Trains come frequently and travel fast. There are taxis and scooter taxis outside most stations. Ticketing is easy and reasonable.

Really efficient, good priced service
Does get really busy, so be prepared to be standing
Easy to use. Staff are friendly and helpful.
Best way to avoid traffic jam in Bangkok. Easy finding station on the map. Bought a BTS rabbit card 3 years ago still work, Cashier were very friendly can speak English.

Take the BTS to avoid jam. Easy and quick. Need to purchase tix by queuing and queue may be long depending on timing. 

Please count your money before leaving the counter, prices are stated on wall near the coin machine. One of the ladies shortchanged me.

The traffic in Bangkok is HORRIBLE so the sky train is a fantastic option. Safe, clean and inexpensive. The routes are well marked and the air conditioning is great which is awesome on a hot day. So much better than the NYC subway.
Its is very easy to use the skytrain. It easily gets you to places you wanna go. Best way to travel is to get the rabbit card as it cuts down the need to have lots of coins to get single tickets
In Bangkok cheap, quick easy way to travel and no Bangkok traffic you will get to where u need to go much quicker

Bangkok’s BTS system with access to the Metro and other public transportation systems is set up similar to European and Japan style system. They have kiosk in English as well as attendant stations. The staff was always helpful and go out of their way to help including a needed toilet access to the ‘staff’ area when none was publicly available for this desperate ‘tourist’. Service windows are always available but bring coins if you want to use the self service kiosks

review_title: A great way to get around
review_text:
The BTS is fast, efficient and air-conditioned.
Tickets are easily purchased at the counters and platforms are clearly signposted.
Finally can travel with their transport top up card (Rabbit card).  Grab 1 to move with ease & convenience.
Amazing!!!! It's a must when you are in Bangkok cause taxi take too long time from A to B. And its cheap also

Forget getting overpriced tuktuk or a taxi that just sits in traffic... jump on the sky train and you’ll be on the other side of town in minutes.

We used it for 3 days solid while we were there on our short break in Bangkok,  staff all there to help you, people on board were friendly to us foreigners and we wizzed from place to place with ease.

Great experience, can get quite busy but that’s part of being in a bit city.

Cheap, clean and easy to take! 
Nothing to add except they do not provide map and ticket should be thow when you leave the metro!

If you want to move in bangkook use bts.
It is very saftey.
Very clean.
You fill very comfort.it with air condition that mostly needed in bangkook.
Take hotel near bts it's very important.

amazing experience
clean, effeicient, self explnatory, no loss of time as compared to road traffic, no pollution
no pushing when entering or exiting
During our visit in Bangkok, BTS Skytrain was our primary transportation to explore the city. It's very comfortable, clean, fast, and efficient. Definetely the best transportation in Bangkok.

review_title: Use the sky train in Bangkok.
review_text: It is convenient and cheap. A day ticket valid till midnight at 140 Bahts lets one go from any point to just any station. One is literally on the go. We found one train a bit crowded. The next train was just a few minutes away.

review_title: So simple to use as an English user
review_text: We was really worried about using the sky train as we cannot speak Thai at all. But the ticket machines allows you to press ‘English’ and then they are so simple from there. Once on the platforms everything you need is in English, and once on the train it’s written in English and spoken in English too so there is no need to worry. We tried Taxi’s a few times at all times of the day. Whilst they’re cheap, this is cheaper and SO much cheaper and I would use every time.

We had a wonderful family trip to Bangkok in February, and everything was perfectly organized by Nishat Tours And Travels. From the very beginning, their team handled our itinerary, hotel bookings, airport transfers, and sightseeing with great professionalism.

If you are planning a Thailand holiday with family, I highly recommend Nishat Tours And Travels. They truly made our Bangkok vacation memorable and hassle-free.

Highly recommended travel company!

Excellent neighborhood, with subway area and nearby stations, a lot of commerce and many and many shoppings. Quiet to walk during the day and night tbem. Good restaurants and street snacks. I like it. Some squares, etc

review_title: An excellent area to stay in whilst visiting Bangkok.
review_text: I come often to Bangkok for work and Sukhumvit is always my first preference when I am looking for a hotel. The area is always buzzing. You have malls, parks, nightlife districts, restaurants and more on your doorstep. Excellent transport links with easy access to the BTS SkyTrain and MRT. This bustling boulevard is one of Thailand's longest roads.

We stayed at Siam, but we took the BTS Sukhumvit line to Asoke and Phrom Phong, where we made several round trips for food and shopping. It's a lively metropolis with a variety of luxury brand stores, large shopping centers, and even local restaurants and food stalls.
In Soi Cowboy, Nana and other entertainment districts, the atmosphere is raucous with neon-glaring bars. You can enjoy it day and night for different purposes for different people.
Looking down from the skywalk to the Asoke intersection, it was heavy traffic, as is usual in Bangkok.
It is a place that can be said to be Shinjuku, Shibuya, Thailand. It's downtown, full of people and cars. There are many nice department stores and restaurants, which is fun.
Bangkok at its best...swishly scurrilous yet fascinating. You get along well and never feel insecure.

Sukhumvit is one of the the red light districts of Bangkok. Prostitution is omnipresent. But there is no greater concentration on the streets than in Hamburg, for example. The streets are full of freelancers (some are men, obviously). I entered Soi Cowboy, it is a big mess, everyone (and especially all of them) wants to tell you something, to invite you to come in somewhere. At the entrance to Nana Plaza you are given a cursory check, you have to empty your pockets, they even looked through the women's purses (yes, a lot of women enter too). It has about 3 floors, I think, the fauna is diverse and abundant. Therme Café is a kind of gathering. I have never seen such a concentration of prostitution per square meter in my life. There are about a hundred or two, I think, in a rather tired basement, sitting piled up like rabbits in a cage, at tables and standing, along the walls. Access is free but you have to consume. A beer is 120THB.
Otherwise, there are a lot of streets full of Go-Go bars and freelancers. Hundreds of street vendors selling food but also sexual aids or aphrodisiacs. A nice mall, Terminal21. Lots of nice restaurants and bars on the adjacent streets.

Stayed a few days in Sukhumvit and really liked the area. So many restaurants, cafes and shops around you can always find something to do. It’s busy for sure but has a good energy and felt safe even at night. Also easy to get around with the BTS. Would stay here again next time.

Hustlers pool and Sports bar - great place to improve your pool game, lots of tables but they fill up early. Play as long as you want, beer and food and the girls are great.
Closes around 1:30 am. The girls are great also.

The neighborhood is really nice.
There is everything nearby, it is easy to get around.

It's nice to stay in this part of Bangkok.

I recommend.

Sukhumvit is one of the best parts of the Thai capital. This was my third time in Bangkok and I always stay there.
There are plenty of hotels, million of restaurants, as well shopping malls and rooftop bars. The skytrain runs along it, as well the MRT so it's easy to get around. The area is safe both day and night.
Such a nice area in bankok with a busy and lively night market. The restaurants in the area and coffee are on par with Melbourne.

Good area, central to everything.
Plenty of bars and restaurants in walking distance.
There is plenty of access to Bts and Mrt trainlines so you can avoid using overpriced taxis and tuk tuks.

Sukhumvit is the most happening place in Bangkok. It has everything a traveller need.lots of hotels, restaurants, massage parlours, shops, shopping malls, banks, ATM booth etc. From here you can move anywhere in Bangkok very easily.

review_title: Sukhumvit is always a good time.
review_text: I've been staying in Sukhumvit for years because of its superior shopping, nightlife, and restaurant scene.  It's a great place to have a good time, get a suit made, and enjoy some rest and relaxation at one of the many hotel and resort options.
Sukhumvit is delivering on reputation, it is easy to navigate, watch ur wallet as u frequent the  bars, shops and malls, the people, the skytrain are friendly and safe

review_title: Just a mix of real people getting on with life intermingled with hotels and temples etc
review_text: It’s s real mix of people going about their business and busy roads but then a stumble and theirs a temple or a good place to eat or just see real people . Some good value hotels and accessible walking around here to see some of the places you should see.

There are a lot of places to eat, drink and have fun nearby. It is very suitable to hang out with friends. We have massages, eat and go shopping every day. Sukhumvit is our happy hometown. There are 711s everywhere, which is very convenient.

Worst place to stay in Bankok. Avoid Soi 11 and 4. Dirty, filthy filled with prostitutes and middle aged men looking for sex. Disgusting atmosfere in restaurants and bars that are filled again with white men accompanied by barely teenage girls. Try to avoid at all costs. If “vibrant” means fake rolex watches being shoved in your face, sex toys and canabis along then street. Then thats exactly what you are looking for.
Good place for a short stay, with lots of hotels, restaurants, cafes and shops. You can go everywhere with BTS train
Modern area crossed by trains and subways, full of people and tourists wandering through the numerous clubs and hotels. Pleasant.

review_title: Only place to stay in Bangkok
review_text: This is the second time we have stayed in the Sukhumvit area.Soi 15 this time.We love it as everything is on your doorstep.Public transport,restaurants,bars and shopping.All the spots to visit are accessible by the railway system.We really liked it
Excellent service, and good design of the air terminal, very functional, the automated cleaning service is something that attracts attention
Love the atmosphere, architect, the cleanliness, and, learning about the religion of Buddha, and the food. Was able to visit other places, too.

review_title: Excellent area for a couple of nights stay in Bangkok
review_text: There are shopping centers, decent restaurants, and massage parlors in the area. There is a metro nearby, from where you can get to the bustling city center. This is more of a business district. We liked the area: in the evening you can even calmly stroll through the elite back streets. It will be quiet and beautiful.

This area is full of restaurants, offices, department stores, minimarts, and the BTS Skytrain is used for traveling with time control. But if traveling by car during rush hour In most cases, traffic jams will be encountered.

Good area to stay. In the heart of the city with many restaurants, shopping mall and bts  sky train. No need to take taxi. Everything is within walking distant. I recommend eating in the restaurant area in sukhumvit3 . 20+ of restaurant to choose by. Many open in late night around 2.00 am. Soi sukhumvit4 is good area to have fun too

review_title: Hotel Rembrandt Shukumvit, wonderful welcome, excellent service from the staff
review_text:
Hotel Rembrandt Shukumvit, wonderful welcome, excellent services from all the staff
Serge Imperato

review_title: Former shopping and business street, now run down
review_text: Once the prime shopping and business street in Bangkok with the first BTS line. Today, only second class hotels sit here as the best hotels are by the river line. Also shopping has switched to the malls that have been erected next to BTS and river line.

Just coming to the end of three days in Bangkok with my partner Sue and it’s been a brilliant experience. We stayed at the Rembrandt in Sukhumvit and got a taxi there paying 500 baht which some might say is a little steep but we were happy with that.
The Rembrandt has a lot of positives including: lovely friendly helpful staff, a great breakfast selection and two first class resteraunts being a Mexican and Indian. We tried Mexican although a little pricey, was great service and lovely food. The rooms are a good size, clean but a little tired but hey!! Your only sleeping in it!!

The street food was amazing and great value for money. We also ate in a local Thai cafe which was amazing but sadly can’t remember the name as was a little tipsy.

We walked around, took in the sights, smells and lovely people. We done the sky train and the underground , both great value and also the tuk Tuks ( barter with them) . We shopped locally and went to Chatuchak weekend market, wow what an experience and some good value stalls selling all sorts. ( haggle).

We done the Co van Kessel  cycle and boat tour lasting five hours. A great experience to see parts of Bangkok we would never of experienced. It included bike hire, two knowledgeable tour guides and meal on the river at a cafe that looked rundown but the owners and food were fantastic and probably one of best meals we had during our stay. River trip also included. We visited temples, Chinatown, seen where locals lived and loved the friendliness of the Thai people. 
And that people was our experience of Bangkok. Thank you so much.

We stayed 10 nights in this neighborhood. Our lodging was awesome @ Adelphi49; we ate at different restaurants throughout the n'hood and had many excellent meals. We loved the vibe of the people living and or working there - I hear it's "trendy". The BTS stations are a great option to Grab if you are without a car.

review_title: My favourite area of Bangkok to stay, shop, & enjoy life
review_text:
The majority of Bangkok better hotels are located in this area, and clustered close together. Lots of shops, restaurants, cafes, bars, massage parlours, and seedy establishments. Beware if walking with young kids, some of the goods offered for sale by the street vendors would be rated XXXX. Likely lots of fake meds also being offered by street vendors. 

Over the past ten years, the air quality has very significantly improved.

only place to stay Sukhumvit I stay at nana area many nice hotels place is for single men mostly definitely price right night life lively but I've seen many families stay there without any problems you never have to take taxi or anything all within walking distance even Mall 2
Excellent, lively place to visit with many places for food, drink and shopping. There are a number of good hotels either here or nearby.

Sukhumvit is a very big area with tons of shopping malls, restaurants, hotels and all those lanes and streets with residence, foods, and commercial buildings.
The traffic along the main Sukhumvit road can get super congested. BTS is recommended, it has stations all along the road.
Popular places for tourist such as Terminal 21, Nana, Thong Lor, Siam, On Nut are located in Sukhumvit.

It's a busy area in BKK. I stayed there and especially during night time it changes to a big party place. I was just walking around but if you wanna go to celebrate, here is the right place. During day time it's also interesting, as every street has its own touch. Explore it on your own was a good adventure.

review_title: Pullman Sukhumvit did not have the rooms that were booked and relocated us to another lower rated hotel.
review_text: Pullman Hotel should actually have rooms that had been booked. When they made mistake they should put us in a hotel that had better rating and pay for the extra.

I was so excited to visit Bangkok after hearing so much about it. But I wasn't expecting quite as much seediness. As I walked along the street lined with shops and tables selling all kinds of things I didn't feel comfortable at all even stopping to look at anything. The vendors latch onto you immediately and I felt targeted and greatly taken advantage of. I really liked the idea of seeing 'real' Bangkok so I gave this rating a 3 but I mostly felt sad to see the beautiful young women doing what they need to for money, and many Caucasian older men with significantly younger eye candy on their arms. One walk down the street was enough for me and I spent lots more time in my hotel room than planned.

There are many areas to stay in Bangkok depending on your needs.  This was my favourite area.  Some may find it busy and hectic but I loved the buzz!  Good shopping malls, restaurants, bars and transport links to easily get around.

Sukhumvit area is the center of business and tourists haven in Bangkok. There are many shopping malls and parks where you can shop, five-star hotels to stay in and fancy restaurants. If you are in a budget there are also affordable and clean hotels and hostels for only 500- 1,000 baht per night along Sukhumvit 22. Aside from that, Sukhumvit has a fantastic transportation connection via Skytrain, The Metro, and the Airport Rail Link.

review_title: sukhmvit asok bts to nana bts soi11 soy 15 soi 7/1
review_text:
The section between Asuk and Nana is actually one of the expensive residential centers, you have Terminal 21, Robinson, 11th Street very upgraded, and you can export everything there, street food restaurants, bars, hotels, pubs, very close to the train, 13th street has sports clubs and you can see All the football games and beer, you actually have everything in this area, no need for a motorcycle, every street corner you will find motorbikes, the prices of condos are up to $ 1000, if you choose to live one station on a train far from there you will pay half price for the same condo ,i live soi 13 last 2.5 years ..
jak biton

The Sukhumvit neighborhood was a lot like the USA. There were several US franchises in the neighborhood and all the little VW vans that sold food    
We were told about were no longer there. They were required to leave we heard because of lack of licensing. Sad as one of the reasons we booked our hotel in the area was to experience them. Prices were higher than we had experienced earlier in a different part of Bangkok earlier in the trip.
Best place to hang out in Bangkok, Thailand. most of the malls and 5 star hotels located this place.
We stayed at the Athenee hotel within the Sukhumvit area. The area feels smart and clean but many small businesses along side the big hotels and malls.

Except for the hookers........real mixture of cultures, side streets have every culture imaginable and every cuisine, massages (some even professional and don't offer extra services!), great exchange rates, tuk Tuks and cabs, great area, we were staying at the Sofitel and it's a lively friendly area but the hookers drove us crazy

review_title: higher end shopping, hotels, and eateries
review_text: Sukhumvit Road is packed with higher end hotels and shopping. And since those involve people there is also food there. This is not a place for bargains - it's a great place for air conditioning and hassle-free shopping (at higher prices). You can find most anything that you would want to buy here somewhere. Several shopping malls exist on the road. Due to the attraction of tourists the road traffic can be grid-locked at times, so you should anticipate utilizing mass transit or your feet here. Get off that road and things change markedly. Eateries, massages, bars, and affordable hotels abound nearby.

Very Good place in the center of city. Terminal 21 is the good place.
I stay here for 2 Nights. Have good hotels around.

Recently took a trip , just last December 27-31, 2019 to Bangkok, Thailand. We stayed at the Holiday Inn Hotel along Sukhumvit Rd, which happens to be the main road ( i think ) in the area. First Id like to commend the staff of Holiday Inn for being courteous , helpful and making you feel comfortable upon arriving the hotel . The lobby or should  i say Front  Desk is on the 9th flr where they have a lounge as well that serves cocktails  daily from 6pm-8pm , aside from the free drinks of scotch, vodka , wine , sparkling wine and juices they also have a small spread on a buffet table of cheese , salami and different local dishes , just the right amount not to fill you up. For those that look forward to breakfast, .... STAY AT THIS PLACE, ( no need to elaborate ), Across the street from Holiday Inn you have a very good steak house (also recommendable) .further down you have a grocery /deli open 24hrs thats got good meat, a vast selection of wine and a lot more . Just a few more meters walk is your shopping malls thats got high end goods to affordable not to mention discounted items. Planning to visit Bangkok soon, stay along Sukhumvit and you wont go wrong.

I always stay in this part of the City.  I enjoy the location, the hotels, restaurants and vibrant retail and nightlife too.  Something for everyone, and I feel safe there too. There is the train nearby and of course the traffic (be prepared).  Lots to see and do and take in. Also, I prefer to stay there and then taxi to the historic temples.  Less tourists too actually.

Well worth checking out even if you are not staying in the area.

Every time I am in Bangkok I choose hotels right in this area.

First because of the BTS - Sky train, meaning easy to get there from the Airport or to the Airport, with no Traffic and a record time and price.

Then again thanks to the Sky Train I can get easily to the shopping centers in Bangkok - MBK, Siam, Big C, and many others

There so many shops and booths along the streets where you can buy garments for your kids or souvenirs, as well as eateries, and not forgetting Nana quarter...
In short, one of the heart of Bangkok

Definitely the place to be for where the heart of adult entertainment is located at. There are also many great hotel chains in the area, with easy access to the train and metro. Soi Cowboy and Nana are both within walking distance, and quite entertaining!
nice busy road with markets and malls and shops and many many restaurants. We went to the street market which was great during the day

This place is great place for first timers as it is very lively and convenient. Everything that you need during here is nearby and accessible. BTS is nearby as it is the fastest way to get around Bangkok and avoid traffic.

I would highly recommend Sukhumvit, the place comes alive after dark and the bars awesome.

This place is a must if traveling in Bangkok

This is a good area to stay for regulars and first time visitors to Bangkok.  It is very well developed, modern, and filled with the conveniences of good hotels, excellent modern malls, local and well known  restaurants, cafes, shops, department stores, currency exchanges, convenient stores, etc BTS stations like Asok (next to Terminal 21 mall) and Phrom Phong (next to Emporium/EmQuartier malls) are never too far out of reach so other neighborhoods in Bangkok can be easily reached.  There is also an MRT station located next to Terminal 21 mall.  This also seems like quite a safe neighborhood with brightly lighted streets. Of all the districts in Bangkok, Sukhumvit seems to have a lot more Japanese businesses (shops, restaurants, department store) than others and a favorite hangout for Japanese visitors.

We’ve stayed on sukhumvit many times as it’s a bustling area with plenty of food, drink, shopping and whatever else you can imagine. Crazy traffic but the BTS runs along it. Many gyms and malls along with a few beautiful parks including benchasiri.

Having stayed at a hotel in Sukhumvit, one had to find one's way around this area. In the day time its bustling with cars and buses. So good to find a way to walk and reach one's destination especially if its shopping in malls like Terminal 21. The Tuk-Tuks provided by the Hotel only brought us till the main road and post that one has to find a way to move around Also a number of offices are in this place.

Most interesting section of this jampacked main road in Bangkok is in between the BTS skytrain stations Asok and Phloen Chit. This section is full of restaurants, shopping malls, streetfood stalls and upscale hotels. On top of the very busy and chaotic road traffic two elevated skybridges can be found: one for pedestrians and one for the skytrain. All together a chaotic but lively road ideal for shopping, eating and people watching.

Sukhumvit is a vital artery of business and social Bangkok. This street is jammed with outstanding shopping malls, extraordinary restaurants, and exclusive hotels. It also is the gateway and access point to many colorful and typically unique Thai neighborhoods that take on the flavor of their culturally distinct inhabitants. Experiencing Sukhumit is a great addition to a visit to a fascinating city

This is a main tourist area here. There are lots of shops malls hotels and restaurants. There are super market and food court also street food outlets. The foodland super market at soi 5 has a 24hr food outlet which is cheap freshly made and tasty.

One of the main street is bangkok. It has everything, restaurants, bars of different nationalities. Transportation methods are all around. There is also different malls in every corner. Recommended for people who are visiting for the first time. It will give you a chance to understand a lot of things about bangkok.

Bangkok is more expensive than Kuala Lumpur:
1) Food is 50% more expensive in regular restaurants in Bangkok than in K.L. 
2) Prices of food and drinks are almost the same as in Europe 
3) money withdrawal cost €6 charge by all banks in Bangkok. Above this amount your bank in Europe will charge their bank costs also
4) Even Dubai is cheaper than Bangkok 
5) Grab is in Bangkok 50% more expensive than in Kuala Lumpur.
6) A cold drink at Starbucks cost around €6. In Europe it would cost €4.50.

review_title: Not a place for the physically disabled
review_text: Steep stairs with no assistance for the disabled. A very difficult place to get around. Even the lifts are locked up and the road traffic is not suitable for pedestrians.

A very long thoroughfare through the central heart of Bangkok, this is the place to visit if you want to have any of several air conditioned experiences in the very many modern and expansive shopping centres that line this road. It helps as well that for a substantial portion of its length the BTS sky train tracks follow it overhead. However it’s still possible to experience walking its length, albeit a tiring one.
  Lots of local eateries outside beside the road, but if you’re after traditional expressions of religious piety they’re not here along Sukhumvit, except maybe at  Erawan shrine. There are also seedier attractions pulling in male patrons of sexual slavery. Best to avoid unless you want to knock the teeth out from one of these “lads”, since in Thailand laws on assault are ignored.

Sukhumvit is a vital artery of business and social Bangkok. This street is jammed with outstanding shopping malls, extraordinary restaurants, and exclusive hotels. It also is the gateway and access point to many colorful  and typically unique Thai neighborhoods that take on the flavor of their culturally distinct inhabitants. Experiencing  Sukhumit is a great addition to a visit to  a fascinating city

A cool and trendy place with eatery and plenty of young people to mingle and walk around with 
Nice safe people watch

review_title: A good place to wander along and shop if you find what you want.
review_text: Sukhumvit Road is not as it once was. When I first stated travelling to Thailand the market stall were open most of the day, Alas the authorities changed this, and now they are open mostly in the evening. That said there is lots to look at and haggle over, some of the stuff is imitation, but from my purchases there, most is of reasonable quality. One tip, as a matter of courtesy, once the vendor has agreed to your offer, its very bad manners not to buy, so if you just want to haggle rather than buy, don't. It can get very bust at times, so children in prams/strollers may find it difficult to navigate. The stall run all the way from Nana Road to Asok station.
This region is really very good- at night we have so many activitries- many restaurants and very delicious food- except we have rats

review_title: Everything you'd expect of an amazing city
review_text: A great place to visit; good food, good coffee to be found, great night spots, interesting people, great shopping; it has everything

Sukhumvit is very renowned for its busiest roads in Bangkok. There are large numbers of hotels, shopping malls, different types of restaurants, bars, clubs, etc on both side of this road. The BTS Sky train stations are so close and perfect to roaming around Bangkok. You can get off one of the stations at Sukhumvit and explore the different Roads/ Sois. The area is becoming more crowded day by day.
This is the area where we stayed. It is a busy nightclub type place. Lots of places for food and drinks as well. Its great the the train is so close perfect to get around Bangkok.

We first stayed in the Sukhumvit Road area nearly thirty years ago, way before the Skytrain was built.  Then, I was completely overwhelmed by the heat, smells, crowds and noise.  Staying again at the end of 2018, things were hugely different in that the high-rises surround you wherever you look, and of course the BTS Skytrain runs right down the centre of the street.  Even though physically vastly different this time around,  there is still the same uniquely Bangkok feel to the area and I enjoyed myself as much as I did thirty years ago.
Even though a long way from the Grand Palace area and the Khao San Road and surrounds, we chose to stay in Soi 1 off Sukhumvit Road due to the sheer number of restaurants and nightlife which meant we did not have to travel far each evening.  Even though the sleaze is ever-present with Nana Plaza, Soi Cowboy and similar little pockets that you stumble across unexpectedly, the area is good-natured and safe, provided you keep your wits about you.
We're travelling back this year and have booked ourselves into Ariyasomvilla, and cannot wait to experience the same chaos as before.
This place is good if you enjoy the nightlife and frequent pubs and bars. Its got a very lively vibe however, its also almost always crowded.
I had a great experience when I was in Bangkok. The people are really friendly and respond back with a smile.

Hey you can relax your tough weekend in Sukhumvit by go through pubs, bars and offcourse for your personal entertainment alot of beautiful ladies as freelancer there. Don't miss the Nana Entertainment plaza. Alot of fun is there waiting for you. Cool.
This whole place is swamped with traffic and it’s associated pollution. Everyone here needs a mask to venture out.
Excelent place for shoppers and nightlife, there are many bars and great massage studios you know what i mean, good restaurants around every corner

I must say that this is not my favorite area in Bangkok. To me Sukhumvit is bit too messy. The area is strange mix of many things... It has big shopping malls with all those famous brands. Some fancy villas and many 5 star hotels. Corporate offices. Then suddenly you walk directly into red light area (like soi 4). Lot of bars and restaurants. With no doubt good nightlife for those who are interested. In general very mixed crowd. Lot of Arabs and Indians.

BTS Sukhumvit line is good. Walking is not nice along the Sukhumvit road. Traffic is very bad. Also air quality along the road is less good. To me Sukhumvit area is messy and at such overrated.
Perfect place to stay and enjoy the night live when you are in Bangkok, many BTS station and the street is long so take a closer look when you want to stay here

Whenever we stayed in Bangkok it was a given we stayed in this part of the city. It was vibrant, fun and have lots of interesting places to walk around. Over the years though it appears to have gone downhill and we have moved to another part of the city near the river.

Lots of our favourite food stalls and bars are now gone to make way for bigger buildings. It’s it’s got more crowded and it’s just not a place to enjoy and relax any more. We still visit on occasion and notice so many changes. It may be progress but there are now many areas in Bangkok better to stay in.

This place is buzzing with activity. I felt like a child in a huge toy store. Electronic gadgets were cheap. I shopped until I was very tired. I enjoyed travelling on metro rail as well as the local transport. Plenty of good restaurants to cater for all tastes. The only problem was that I could not understand the local language, but this did not pose a problem. I would love to visit again.

review_title: A great place to base yourself in Bangkok
review_text: Stayed here on our 4th trip to Thailand. Very accessible to public transport and what I thought was a fairly safe area to walk at night with families.
It is in the heart of the Bangkok city and a super cool place which have access to almost all places there. The hotels in this place are very good and we metro connectivity is also good.

Sukhumvit is the melting pot of all kinds of action and people that it attracts a lot of travellers as well as locals. Soi 21 in Sukhumvit is the 'Walking Street' with a number of bars and restaurants, many offering adult shows of different kinds. Walk around Sukhumvit to grasp the pulse of Bangkok and its a truly mesmerising mix of sound and light especially in the evenings.

review_title: Nice area of Bangkok and road to Cambodia
review_text:
Nice area of Bangkok and road to Cambodia. Plenty of neighbourhoods alongside numerous Soi’s, where good can find food and/or entertainment. 

If you all the way down, you will pass Erawan Muziek, Muang Boran (the Ancient City) and eventually Cambodia!
You'd better visit Sukhumvit at nights. It's really nice to walk around , eat , drink and have fun. This area is really full of energy and joy. Just pay a visit and enjoy a nice Thai night.

Sukhumvit Neighbourhood is named after the fifth chief of the Department of Highways, Phra Bisal Sukhumvit. He was the most important road builder of Thailand. Sukhumvit is the longest road in Thailand and it runs across the middle of this district. It was completed in 1936. This area is famous for it’s villas, fancy apartment complexes, hotels, restaurants, bars and nightclubs. The Prime Minister of Thailand lives at Soi 39. It has two interesting malls called Emporium and Terminal 21. We found many interesting shops and massage parlours in the sois of Sukhumvit. It also has the city’s red light areas on two streets called Nana Plaza and Soi Cowboy. The district is saturated with BTS stations. The most important ones are Nana, Asok, Phrom Phong, Thong Lo, Ekkamai, Phra Khanong, On Nut, Udom Suk, Bang Na and Bearing. I always find this area very  very interesting.

If you want a base from which to use when staying in Bangkok, this is it.
Access to both the BTS and MRT travel systems.
Lots of bars and restaurants for all types and tastes.
Can have a quiet drink or night party - your choice.
Can stay for 400 baht per night or 20000 baht - your choice.

review_title: Reduced to a soulless shopping road
review_text: Once one of the bustling centres of Bangkok have the seen military clean it up to disastrous effects. All the urban chaos has gone, no more street market, no more street food, and you are left with shopping malls that you find in any city, horrible pavements and pollution from traffic. Avoid.

the center of all action..if possible stay on the sukhumvit road ...there are a large variety of hotels catering to everyone...the road is bustling throughout the day and night..you can take the sky train to wherever you want to go...lots of places to shop and eat as well..
We went to a local restaurant by taxi and after a great meal took the sky train from here. Really great visit

review_title: Central to transport, shopping, quality accomodations
review_text:
I've stayed in the Asok/Sukhumvit area 4x over the past 3 years, choosing hotels near the BTS/MRT stops.  It makes for very convenient access to these air conditioned transit lines.

There are several TA Certificate of Excellence accommodations within a 5-minute walk to the metro interchange.  The closest and best value is Room@Bangkok Boutique.  For more amenities I favor Hotel Clover Asoke and Park Plaza Sukhumvit.

I like the choice of eateries in this area.  For budget dining there's Terminal 21 or the 24-hour food service at Took Lae Dee.  Vegetarians can satisfy their tastes at May Veggie Home.  Palm 18 has nice Thai dishes in a patio like environment.

Those who enjoy a morning walk will appreciate Benchakiti Park with its paved walkways around a large pond. It's a nice respite from Bangkok's frenetic traffic.
This street is the most active street in Bangkok. You can find cheap hotels or condos. Different Restaurants are available there with different prices.
the Sukhumvit is the main attraction and downtown areas in Bangkok. This is good place for shopping. The nightlife of Sukhumvit is full of fans.

review_title: Street with everything you would ever want.
review_text: The place to be in Bangkok. Whatever you want is available. Recommend the BTS line with stops at basically every corner.
This place smells of something really wierd, although the location is pretty central. There are a few malls in the area which are accessible by walk

review_title: Business area and mixed all culture
review_text:
If you looking for place for office,  here the place for you.
Sukhumvit has Japan town , Korean town and others 
Many offices and business here 
Besides shopping malls are locating here too

This area is what many think of when visiting Bangkok, and you will get a full on view of many sites, just walking down the street.  It is an active area supported by the BTS train system, with many stops to get out and walk.  you will see night vendors selling all kinds of items, along the north side of the street mainly, street food - don't be scared - try a fried waterbug, restaurants, bars, massage places, at the far end of my walk you come to a indoor mall with normal type mall stores.  Nightlife keeps this place busy at all hours of the day and night.  Note - the BTS train system stops at some hour - like 11 or 12 midnight, so you will need to take a TukTuk or Taxi after that, and traffic along this road is very unpredictable - rely on the train system to get in and out of the area - it is the best way to travel here.

Very busy part of Bangkok, very popular among tourist and expats and locals st work. The most expensive on average part of town in terms of shopping and dining. Not much to see in terms of proper tourist attractions. It gets equally busy at night for different reasons, adult entertainment takes over in various parts of the neighborhood.

Sukhumvit is right in the heart of the city. The road is one of the busiest and is choked up with traffic at most times. The BTS runs above the road and is the best and cheapest means of travel.
There are countless shops, bars, restaurants, street vendors and malls on either side of the road.

review_title: Busy bustling BTS stop for access to MBK and Siam Paragon
review_text: This is one of my landmark train stations. Once here I can navigate my way around quite easily. Love the little shops inside, selling some very interesting items, that you are tempted to buy, even if you know you don't need them! Can get extremely crowded at peak times, but you can wait for it to calm down at any one of the restaurants close by, or just sit on the benches outside the shopping centres

This is one of the busiest roads in Bangkok.  There are shopping malls, shops, hotels, restaurants, bars, clubs, etc on both side of this road.  

It gets very busy and the traffic is heavy at times.  The best way to experience Sukhumvit is to take the BTS train.  You can get off one of the stations on Sukhumvit and explore the different little Sois.

review_title: People in Sukhumvit area  of Bangkok, Thailand were nice
review_text: The people in the Sukhumvit area of Bangkok, Thailand were very nice and accommodating. The people at the Double tree hotel were outstanding. The area is very interesting with allot of expats. The rail system was very easy to use to get area Bangkok. It was very inexpensive.
If you are a city person and likes the hustle bustle of a big city, you will love this area. Best for shopping and dining experiences. Beware of the traffic at rush hours. Best to take the BTS then.

The Sukhumvit  area  of Bangkok  is  a fantastic place to start your Thai  experience  lots of cheap hotels and bars  for single men it's  near Nana   plaza  and the famous  Soi Cowboy  even though I have a  Thai  girlfriend is love going to  Soi Cowboy to see live music  in Country Rd or Corner bar some great bands in Thailand  sukhumvit is a great area and always  something to do

review_title: WARNING: Watch out for Taxi Scams
review_text:
During our visit to Bangkok we stayed in the Sukhumvit area.
The location is great, close to Nana Skytrain Station, close to Starbucks, Terminal 21 mall and many other shops and fun places.
BUT NEVER negotiate with a private taxi driver!!!!!!!
This is what happened to us.

Outside our hotel (Grand Swiss) I was approached by a taxi driver (see photo) whom I have seen there every day. He approached me every morning but I refused his offers. The third morning we (family) planned to go on a river cruise with the local river bus (15TB per person) to see the palace and temple. 
This man again approached us and would not let us go. He asked us what we were going to do the day. Not wanting to be rude, I told him about the river cruise. He said he will take us (4 people) out for the whole day, wait for us while we have the  river cruise and take us to the floating market as well. He will then turn us to the hotel the evening, for only TB500. This sounded like a good deal!!!
We were in on it and very excited.
Our excitement did not last long......
He got on to a highway, made us pay TB50 for the toll gate and travelled for 2 hours to another city. When we asked him numerous times why we are travelling so  far, he said he is taking us to the best floating  market. Eventually he stopped at a horrible place, an old dirty floating market.....with shock and horror we realized the entrance fee is TB 6000!!!!
When we told him that we do not wish to pay that much to see the dirty floating market, he LOST it. He told us that he will not take us back to the hotel, and he spoke (in Thia) to ALL the taxi drivers at the place and EVERYONE refused to assist us. There we were, stuck.....no other taxis, trains or busses going to Bangkok, and he just 'dumped' us there. 
We had to walk away from that floating market, and begged someone to give us a lift (obviously at a cost) to the nearest Skytrain station, so that we could get back to the hotel. We got back to the hotel, very late afternoon. What was supposed to be a great day out in Bangkok, was made one of the most stressful days ons can think of.
Please! please be aware of private taxis, even those ones the bell boys recommend. 
They are all in on these kind of deals.
Stick to the tuc-tucs, Skytrain and official taxis.
This guy stole a whole day out of only 4 days we had in Bangkok.

Check the 2 photos, the guy and his car!!
Sukhumvit is a large, diverse area in Bangkok. Apart from the trendy Terminal 21 and convenient location, it has little to offer.

review_title: It is the most vibrant part of the City with a 24 hour street life
review_text: It is important to stay in a good hotel with a pool because the heat and noise of the City will be tiring.  I stayed at The grand Sheraton Sukhumvit and it was wonderful with the most attentive staff, amenities and food. I will certainly stay there next year when I visit.  The personal attention of the top management was impressive.

This place is super pack during rush hours morning and night time when folks have to go to work or going home from work. The MRT at this station will also take you to the airport. There are plenty of restaurants and shops near by. Terminal 21 and etc.

review_title: Includes multiple areas along the Sky train / Subway lines
review_text:
Sukhumvit road is a main road that runs through Bangkok downtown to the East.
The sky train and subway run along this road so making it very convenient to move around without facing the traffic jam. There are several hotels, shopping malls, bars and restaurants along Sukhumvit line. It is a great general area to stay.

Having lived in Thailand , traveling there at least 4 /5 times a year for the last 10 years , this is one of my favorite parts of BKK -- the busy streets the food stalls , the walking people watching it has it all ! -- Ride the Sky train to any part of BKK so this makes the IDEAL stop point to do some shopping or access other parts of BKK with ease

review_title: Depends on what your looking for.
review_text:
Lower numbered soi's (streets) are the most crowded in the PM. All sorts of stalls (and women called freelancers) line the road selling everything imaginable. Offer them 30% of their asking price, settle on 50 (not for a companion). Walk away and watch them chase you down.
Interesting sights, sounds and smells. Many great inside eateries. 10 years going there and I'm always finding something new.

We traveled for a three days trip.. Stayed in Radisson Suites Sukhumvit.. Here is something important for everyone to know.
1. When you are going to Thailand and you r taking visa on arrival the fees is 2000Baht.. But if you go for FastTrack which is faster and it costs 2200 Baht.. That's just 200 baht extra which is not much.. I suggest you go for that so that you can get out of the long queue faster.
2. Since there is tax in Thailand make sure to get a vat form so that you can get back the vat you paid in the shop. You can get that money back in the airport while traveling back..

review_title: One of the best areas in Bangkok
review_text: Sukhumvit is a very famous area, this area is call Sukhumvit because of the main road where it's located. Nightlife of Sukhumvit is also famous but this area is very big and it depends where your hotel or guest house it. There are BTS and MRT stations which makes it very easy to move around. There are several restaurants, bar, convenient stores like 7Eleven, Familymart,hotels, guest houses,parks,malls.. But this area also has a bad reputation of scammers... so stay alert ..

review_title: Best area to stay in Bangkok for 2 nights
review_text: We stayed  at the executive club at Windsor in Sukhumvit area . Ideal location as all trendy hotels,bars , restaurants , clubs , red light district, massage parlors  and  the train station (Nana stop ) are all within 3-5 minute walk from the hotel . Sukhumvit is a very Vibey, busy area with lots to do at night . Bangkok in general is chaotic but thrilling . You can see and enjoy more of Bangkok if your location is right otherwise you will be sitting in traffic jams getting from one point to another . Take into account  From Sukhumvit it takes almost an hour to or from the airport in average traffic . Upon visiting the red light district, it’s safer to drink beers or buy sealed drinks as the cocktails sometimes are way too strong and you find yourself extremely drunk after one or 2 drinks . Don’t know if it’s a ploy to get you drunk quickly , either way,  be careful ! Use meter taxis as they are almost half the price than pre booked cabs .
Everything you are looking for is here, Sukhumvit is surrounded by everything, also it’s close to the main transportation BTS.

I stayed in sukhumvit 12.
It's a nice area with malls .food options etc
But it is  very far from reasonable shopping malls. If you visiting Bangkok only for  cheap shopping then I wouldn't recommend .
Even Bangkok safari zoo is also too far, though the area itself is very lively

Stay away from the Sukhumvit road during the Songkran festival, seems like every single local in Bangkok has arrived and going mad splashing each other. Spent 10 minutes here and had to run away, should there be a stampede wonder what the plan is? I'm sure on any other day it is a pleasant road with lots of high end stores and restaurants.

Lined with high end malls cafes and massage parlours sukhumvit road through dpi 10 to 24 is a delight for the eyes
The sous 11 are crowded chok a block with hotels bars ...while 22 onwards are quite residences
24 has a big seafood restaurants...with its large signage...IF IT SWIMS WE HAVE IT

Thanon - meaning road in Thai - Sukhumvit is also known as highway three. You can ride it all the way up to Cambodia. Most people probably know Sukhumvit as place to be for entertainment. Alongside its Soi’s you can find many bars and restaurants here. Some a little bit more subversive than others. Mind the hustlers too. There are a lot of people there that will approach you, try to sell you something, scam you or get you engaged in some other kind of activity...
Best place to stay when visiting Bangkok. Well connected with both BTS and bus services, you can easily reach any part of Bangkok from here. It all serves food for different taste buds.
We stay in this area every year and I love it. Great mixture of shopping, bars and nightlife aswell as great food and the best hotels. Perfectly located to travel around Bangkok.

I come across this area as one of the must places to visit and thought I would add my view. 

We were staying off one of the side streets (I think they call them soi) so would visit this street regularly.

To me it's just another mainstreet under the sky train with various arrangements of shops, offices, small malls which does come alive in the evening with street vendors but no more so than any other street in Bangkok. 

It's OK but wouldn't recommend it for a particular trip!

We only spent 2 nights in Bangkok so have nothing else to compare this too but it did seem like a busy bustling area with street shops and bars. the Sky train makes this a must stay place and there are plenty of fly overs to get across the busy main road
This area of Bangkok is very lively and has lot of budget hotels, restaurants and good access to other parts of Bangkok by metro. Not too far from Siam and MBK malls.
Sukhumvit is relatively less busy than Siam. This area is both business and residential. The Emporium is a good mall.

A busy place full of life. There are many offices and restaurants here, lots of traffic and people everywhere. Sometimes easy to get around on the two wheeler taxis which could be a good alternative to the tuktuks
