Sukhumvit Road is very long and covers a large area of Bangkok. You have everything from high end malls, to gogo bars, to restaurants to massage places. Everything you want or need you can get here. The BTS sky train runs most of it so it’s easily accessible. 
It’s a safe place to visit any time if the day or night. Obviously you still have to be careful, being drunk and stupid at 3am will get you in trouble in any city in the world.

Sukhumvit is a large area right in the center of Bangkok. Major hotels are located here from 2 Star to 5 Star. Many shops some well known, others just small local Thai shops such as tailors, clothing and many street food stalls. Prostitution is rampant at night time to the extent that some of these girls or boys are actually chasing you.

This main strip is a busy, bustling strip with some great, over the top malls and oodles of restaurants, cafes, bars and shops.  Fantasic train runs this route making transport hassle and traffic free. Each Soi than runs off of Sukumvit is a little neighborhood onto itself with hotels, massage places and restaurants.  If you like the big city vibe - you’ll enjoy Bangkok! BKK has an energy all its own - just stroll down Sukumvit at any hour and soak it in!
Very nice place, during the day a little bit crowded but nice. A lot of shops, bars, restaurant. If you are in Bangkok you need to come here!
one of the most beautiful area for night life and near everything in Bangkok, it is very nice for singles who likes adventure in the night life and ladies.

The Sukhumvit neighbourhood is on both the skytrain (Nana Station) and the underground metro (Sukhumvit Station) so it's become very accessible. All the major international brands have moved in to the ever increasing shopping malls, including all the top of the range, 5* brand names. Many international luxury hotels have also arrived. The street food is pretty good around the side streets and there are many, many bars & cafes serving the international tourist crowd.

One of the main stops along the tram system.  The roads are always busy and the night life is worth taking in.  There are many hotels in the area as well, so one can get a place to stay and be within walking distance to local attractions in the area.

Bangkok can be a very claustrophobic city with a lot of air pollution. I however find that my journey along the Sukhumvit area and easy to follow Skytrain that service this area makes it more comfortable and safe to visit. Great restaurants and shopping experiences can be found.

It’s great- lots of Soi’s coming off of it, with bars, restaurants, shops, hotels, hostels, cafes, etc. Perfectly located if you were looking for places to stay in Bangkok; you’ve got the BTS Sky Train as well as the MRT Subway/tube type thing. It is very busy especially at rush hour. Can grab taxis and tuktuks from pretty much anywhere along the road.

Sukhumvit is a large area of downtown Bangkok. It is extremely busy area. Well connected by public transport. It is a good mixture of high end properties alongside budgeted properties. Bustling nightlife. 

It is a good option if you want to book your accommodation in this part of Bangkok , it has a lot to offer !

review_title: The right place to enjoy nightlife
review_text: Sukhumvit is a large area in Bangkok and a large portion of this place is occupied by pubs, bars and restaurants. Walk into this famous arena in the evening and you can see the place turning into one crazy street with so many young girls dancing in the pubs with loud music.

review_title: Very famous street, Hotels, restaurants, shopping and night life.
review_text: Sukhumvit street consider the most popular street in Bangkok, here you will not get boring, the Big hotels, even budget hotels, restaurants, shopping malls, night life, easy to reach to and from with sky train goes through, The Soi means the small streets on left and right the street, very nice street and Sukhumvit station is easy to reach.
Crowded but has everything you want. Good hotels, good places to eat, good shopping areas and if you are a late nighter

review_title: Shopping malls and sleezy sex dives.
review_text: If you're into shopping stick to the malls. If not avoid Sukhumvit. We stopped into a bar for a drink and had to make it clear several times that that was all we wanted ! We're not far from 80 !
Sukhumvit offers so much variety from classy shopping malls down to seedy bars. I would highly recommend spending a day (and more importantly a night) wandering around here!

This is nothing but famous area iof offices shopping market etc in thailand.
There is also skyline station name with this area.

Sukhumvit is a large area of downtown Bangkok which offers you tall business offices to top notch hotels, rooms on rent for hourly basis (if you know what I mean) to delicious street food to red light areas. Name it and you find it here. There are also numerous massage parlours lined up across all its sois (lanes) and you can cuddle yourself into one of these (some might offer you extra services as well)

review_title: Bustling place in central part of city
review_text: Located in the central part of the city.  Accommodates the Sikhimvit metro and ASOK BTS Sky Train links.  Terminal 21 shopping mall mall is also located next to these stations.  Lots of hotels in this area.

review_title: Hotels, modernized, and bars, and more
review_text: This is probably the most active and new part of the city. Lots of bars, restaurants, hotels...you get the idea. It’s basically where you should stay as it is also easy access to MRT and BTS.
this is a long street and its full of many shops, restaurants (especially middle eastern) and also you can walk up to mall called terminal 21 which is a theme restaurant..
If spending time in Bangkok, this should be one place you should visit.  There are a number of hotels and restaurants that go from very inexpensive to real expensive.

Lots of interesting places to visit and great restaurants nearby. The Emporium Shopping Mall has many specialty stores and cinemas. You can spend a whole day doing retail therapy and if you get hungry there is a great gourmet food hall.
the place is centre  of bangkok andeasy access to all shoppign places restaurants and malls must visit for the travellers

Every city has that one neighborhood that becomes popular with all the cool people and is alive with activity. This is the place in Bangkok. It's the neighborhood with great cafes, malls, and fun shops. A great place to walk around day or night. We have stayed in a great hotel Legacy Suites right off Sukhumvit. We loved walking in that neighborhood.

This road apparently is very much a main road through the city and as such it has a lot of traffic.  But usually one can drive through it except at rush hour.  So many malls, stores, hotels, restaurants and about everything one could think of wanting are along this street.  LOOOONG - have no idea how long
We like to stay in this part of town as it's central and easy to navigate around Bangkok from here. Easy access to the skytrain and loads of shopping places.
this is an awesome city with everything within walking distance or you can take the train. very lively night life and lots of shopping opportunitites
Must go place for party lovers. lots of option for clubbing and party. sports bar and bistro add to nightlife attraction.beware of cheap clubs

Modern Bangkok with all the big fashion names, a huge mall catering to modern western tastes. Burger King is there and so is good women's and men's attire. Give me "OLD STYLE" Bangkok anyday we could almost have been in Singapore.
If in Bangkok you cannot miss this central district.From Malls , Restaurants, Go Go bars and raunchy entertainment, it has it all. Just be careful tho.
Everything from here is close by so ideal to stay around here and move about to different places as it is very convienient

review_title: A very crowded narrow area good for nightlife, dancing, bars, resto's and massage etc.
review_text: We passed in day time, the crues were busy to clean the scene and refilling the drinks and food. The area looks realy like a rough nightclub scene, good for youngsters and those who feel lonely needing company.

I stayed in Sukhumvit throughout my stay in Bangkok and loved it. 
It's lively with its many restaurants, shops, bars and high end hotels. It served as a good base to commute to and from other points of interest by BTS or taxi.
The area also felt quite safe. 
Next time I visit Bangkok, I will definitely stay in this area again.
Sukhumvit is a very large area and the Bangkok's most active places are here. It is a must to stay in this part of town for tourists.

For some reason most tourists prefer the Sukhumvit area. To me it is crowded, not very interesting and much more expensive than other places in Bangkok. Try insted the Silom(Sala Daeng ara or - even better Suanphlu(Nanglinchi. Her you will get a taste of real Thai living.

Had a lovely time in Bangkok city...if anyone is looking for a great taxi driver well I found one. Mr KEE...+660850738829 who takes bookings. He is such a lovely and professional driver. I will definitely use him again next time I am in Bangkok.

If you want to do things near the river, stay near the river.  However if you want access to Bangkok’s array of luxury malls then Sukhumvit is your place.  Situated right in the middle of the city and with the Skyrail cutting right through the middle this is a high end location with some of the best city hotels.  There are still plenty of street food and cheap eateries but not as abundant as near the river.  Better for the airport.  So all down to choice.  Having stayed in both locations this week, the Ibis Riverside wins.
I had a chance to visit this place. It is really nice. This is really vital street. it has a lot of restaurants and massage centers.

Great location to stay.
Terminal 21 mall + various massage places nearby
Also have both BTS and MRT. Very convenient
This is  a wide avenue with plenty of restaurants and hotels.Easy access and lots of noise. It is a must to visit and feel how the locals work and play.

Sukhumvit is great, right in the middle of bangkok. It has been cleared up and some power cables have been moved underground. A lot of previous issues have been resolved. Night life and shopping all near by too. Nana for night life and Asoke for shopping.

Sukhumvit is busy, busy, busy. Thousands of people on the footpaths, hundreds of cars & taxis on Sukhumvit Rd, and dozens of street stalls selling food, drinks, souvenirs, massages,  and tours. If you are into people watching or just watching the world go by, there are plenty of interesting people & things to watch.
Lots to see and do In this area during the day and at night.  Pricey stores to street vendors. Fancy food to Street food. You name it you will find it

Nice location to stay.
Nice neighborhood.
Located close to metro and BTS.
Also indian restaurants in this area.

Been here for a couple of months. First impression was not so good. I stayed around NANA station which is very close to the halal Area (big area btw). You will see people from the middle east, their restaurants and stores. I walked around to buy groceries for the first time wearing regular clothes and men won't stop staring in a weird way. Pretty uncomfortable but i got over it. 
Sukhumvit area is always busy anytime of the day. Traffic can be really bad even late at night (11:00 pm). There are lady and lady boys bars. Many solo tourist mostly old guys. You can find many things around here: even your vialis or "kamagra" right on the street, along with sex toys. After 9 or 10 pm, you see ladies and ladyboys offering "extra" services. If guys are alone, some women grab their hands when they walk by, trying to convince them.There are massage shops everywhere but not all of them offer sex. How do you know? I do not even know that but after a while you get to figure it out with common sense. There are plenty of places to have suits done and they have different prices. 
Good thing is that if you don't mind walking and exploring, you can visit the mall Terminal 21, Benjakitty park, Central Embassy Mall, Erawan Shrine and more around the same sukhumvit Road. 
If this is your first time coming to Thailand, please do not expect the best service. Just relax and enjoy because they are not very concern about delivering the best. 
If you are taking a taxi, make sure you tell the driver where you are going before you get on. They decide if they take you or not because the traffic is really bad in some areas and you can be stuck for 1 hour just to pass 2 miles. Make sure they turn on the meter. If you want to be prepared download the GRAB app and this will give you an estimate of prices for transportation (Bike, taxi, Uber etc). You will know the average price and you won't be ripped off. I live about 1 mile away from benjakitty park. Asked for a motorbike ride and he asked 150 baht. I walked away. I knew it was only 20-40 bath. 
Food Is not the cheapest if you go to a restaurant. Street food is the cheapest but could be also expensive if your stomach is not prepared. Just in Case, there is a hospital nearby (Bumrungrad Hospital) and the service is very good.
Finally, be careful when booking a car, tax, van service. I have seen the drivers drinking late night. They gather to drink and next day, they are ready to "work". 
Do not generalize but always be careful, use common sense. The area is very safe, in general. No need to walk grabbing your purse like you carry a million dollars there.

review_title: Stayed here twice in one trip!
review_text: Great place to stay! Loved the vibe and energy this part of town had. We stayed off of Soi 19- right down the way from the giant terminal 21 mall. So many great things within walking distance! We could even walk to the train stop if we wanted to travel farther. One day we took a cab into the old city and walked around all day. I was so happy that we didn't stay there and instead stayed in sukhumvit. Much more clean and hip- with fabulous dining and rooftop bars!
A long street to explore with the BTS plying the route. Shopping malls, restaurants, hotels, parks and nightlife ensure there is always something for everyone.

Fantastic area to stay with great transport links, many restaurants and bars along with plenty of shopping malls. Would highly recommend as modern and a very clean area to use as a base for your sightseeing.

review_title: One of the must see places in Bangkok and to experience the traditional food and night life.
review_text: The longest road in Thailand, which is quite famous for traditional tourist attractions and rich in shopping, eating and nightlife.  The many fashionable restaurants, bars, air-conditioned malls, luxury hotels are located here in this place and it will be an amazing experience to spend almost a day.

review_title: Some gentrification...and still going from strength to strength!
review_text: I was here again very recently and was impressed at all the renovation East of the Grande Sukhumvit SPG Hotels. The area around Benchasiri Park and the Dinosaur Theme Park has gentrified apace and there are more shopping centres and apartment buildings. I loved the whole greater Sukhumvit area this time. It warrants a great deal of exploration.
Sukhumvit is a very central location to stay in Bangkok city. It has access to some of the most varied range of hotels and best of Malls to offer tourists everything that they look for locationallly

This is a great neighborhood to explore and experience with plenty of restaurants, bars and nightclubs, parks, eateries, retail and mom and pop shops, and just plenty to see, eat, and do.

It is easily accessible by the BTS through various stations and also is quite walkable.

There are tuk tuks, regular taxis, motorcycle taxis, and hotel shuttle cans you can always take, as well as simply walk.

Plenty of 7-11s and little places to go get some water bottles from to stay hydrated as you explore.

The lodging ranges from the very economical to the very expensive and everything in between.

The locals have been extremely pleasant and helpful when I have had any interaction with them.

Best rate on a money exchange I have found is the "Super Rich" money exchange which are in a few different places.

I used to like this area but it doesn't really "do it".  Places change  - people change 
It might be fun for some

This Sukumvit area is quite full of tourist and Bar all over the place. i was having my hotel just in that area so i just went on a walk around the area and was really having a lot to do over there, more for shopping malls if you wanna do shopping should take this place. ;)

We actually stayed in a hotel in this area & found it terribly congested & very costly to get in & out of.  That said, there are many food options nearby, very authentic, local style. But the area is also filled with many seedier bars, strip clubs, massage parlors (not the legit kind) so it's a hassle to walk around getting pulled in all directions. Avoid if you like a bit more quiet & prefer not to be disturbed walking down the street.   I was with my grown daughter & we had to almost run out of that section as people were way too aggressive trying to get our attention to buy their goods or go into their bars etc.

It's one of the most visited place in Bangkok..it's bustling with Shopping malls, restaurants, mac Donald's,Starbucks,4/5 star hotels,the sky train,and people all over..Yes you can also see massage parlours also but that is up to the person travel purpose..the Thai people are very friendly and welcome their guests with warm gesture.. enjoy your stay 😁😁😁

Regards
Gautham
except there are no red lights here.it is the adult part of the city.i would not visit with kids,as the massage parlours,bars etc are unavoidable.they even are next door to mcdonalds
Just take a walk in the evenings around sukhumvit, you will find everything under the sun. cheap shopping for kids and adults, good food and drink.

It seems the heart of Bangkok life is Sukhumvit. Lively and bubbly all the time. Without coming to Sukhumvit, one does not feel the pulse of Bangkok life. The roads are busy with different vehicles. Traffic jams on this road are notorious. Lots of shops are located along with eateries. We love our walk on this road to go the different renowned shopping malls and restaurants. Our main attraction on this road is MBK, Central Plaza, Terminal 21, Robinson and many other shopping malls. They are also connected by sky trains. I sit in a coffee shop and watch lazily all the passing vehicles, trains and people. It is just great. At night, the eateries become full with people and smoke from freshly cooked food. Lovely busy road for all time.

Sukhumvit is one of the main streets that runs through the heart of Bangkok.  Along the street there Soi's (streets) on either side.
The odd numbered streets are on one side and the even numbered streets on the other.  It is a good street to use as a landmark to get to a lot of places in Bangkok.  I have many videos of my trip posted on my You Tube channel "I Went There".  Bangkok is a place that almost anyone will enjoy.

After having stayed near Khao San Road before We stayed in Sukhumvit it was very expensive for manicure, pedicures and hair services. Also the massages are expensive and have to spend to much time sorting out the good from the bad.

review_title: hard to work out where you are at times
review_text:
A hectic area but lots to see and do.
Loads of choices to eat or drink and literally thousands of shops to choose from. 
You wont get bored but night get lost. Its a friendly area so you wont feel threatened. One tip would be always agree a price with a tuk tuk driver or taxi before getting in, they are renowned for making up an inflated price if this has not been sorted before travel commences.

This was a neighborhood I was told I could find a good airbnb in but my street, soi 7 was really not somewhere I would advise anyone to stay at.  Soi 8 which is on the other side of Sukhumvit is different however... you really need to know your Bangkok when you reserve an airbnb!

review_title: Lots of restaurants, beautiful neighborhood, lots of expensive and budget hotels.
review_text: The Sukhumvit neighborhood is a very safe place with lots of street vendors, restaurants, both national and international, number of shopping centers, and movie theater in one of the shopping malls. You can reach there with both subway and sky train, There are also a number of five star as well as budget hotels in the neighborhood. If you have extra time, visit the area or stay in one of the hotels.

review_title: Crazy busy and lots of pollution
review_text: This is the main street of bangkok or the center of the city they say. It was quite a hustle and bustle area and quite aot of pollution and way too much traffic

Sukhumvit has everything, be it fine dining, luxurious hotels, glitzy shopping malls or cheap massages of all kinds of quality, fake clothes and Rolexes and numerous pubs, bars, night clubs and ladies who are hoping you may take them to your room for a while. 
It bustles with traffic, food hawkers, beggars, hookers, average Thais just minding their business and loads of tourists. My favourite thing to do is sitting in one of the cafes or pubs and just watch what is going on. Could do that for hours.
Sukhumvit Road is also the longest road in all Thailand which takes you from all the hustel-bustle all the way to the border of Cambodia (although there are faster ways to get there by motorway).

It has always been my "go to" area of Bangkok but like a lot of things..it is changing. Gone are the days of the VW Campervan bars, the street food restaurants the lined the streets and even the endless night market. All moved on by the council/government. It is still filled with clubs/pubs/restaurants and hotels...both on and also just off the street in sois. A lot of big western style shopping malls along here as well and it is well served by the BTS.
This is a great area to visit for entertainment, food and much much more, people a friendly and the shopping is great.

review_title: A stretch of night market after 10pm..
review_text: Cheap goods, worth the money spend if you are just looking for clothes. May not be original, but does looks like the same.

review_title: If you don't visit here, you haven't visited Bangkok
review_text:
A large portion of Bangkok's shopping and entertainment occurs on Sukhumvit Road, between the National Stadium and Phrom Phong BTS Skytrain Stations.  To be sure, there's shopping elsewhere, but the convenience of the Skytrain makes this area the most popular place to go to.

Street traffic is often terrible during rush hour, so using the Skytrain or moto taxi is  absolutely recommended.  The MRT subway crosses Sukhumvit at Asoke Station.  The Bangkok Bus Terminal (Eastern) is at Ekamai Station.  Note:  the bus station has regular cheap transportation to Pattaya.

Sukhumvit is the heart of Bangkok. all the happening place here, restaurant, shopping, bar, club and hotel. Whatever you want will get it here or around here. Soi 11 is the best place to explore. Soi 2 the famous but cheap area. well connected via BTS. taxi is available everywhere. just go and enjoy!!

This street is very long and its in the heart of Bangkok. There are so many malls, Shops, tailors, resturants and many more in this road. The good thing about it is the MRT metro go throgh all this road.
its a district in the center of Bangkok. Skytrain passes though this district and many shopping plazas are in it.
Staying in Sukhumvit area is just amazing. Hotels are good life is buzzing. The sky train is a convenient way to travel anywhere. It's a must stay area.

Lots of hotels to choose from and lots of bars, clubs and restaurants to go to.
Stayed at the Davis Hotel at Soi 24... Nice hotel, but the breakfast selection was a little limited.
Went to various bars and clubs in the neighborhood in one night, forgot the names of all the places that we wen to :) 
It was a "one night in Bangkok" trip.
Loved the walks I took at sukhumvit with my Thai friend Somkuan. I felt safe on the streets and enjoyed much watching vendors on the side selling different street foods and beverages. Unforgettable!😍

This is the main road of Thailand. The traffic is so bad same other towns in the world.
you will find everything that you need on this road, Big department store and public park area.hope you like it.
Stayed in Soi 11. The area has lot of Construction and Development, hence didn't enjoy it much. Always has lot of traffic. Suggest to avoid.

The shopping district. There are expensive models and small local shops and Caroline my eyes with open air stalls. In some spots, night markets set up close to sunset.  The area is easily reached by public transport, either taxi rail although time-of-day will make a difference to the length of the journey.

review_title: nice area to visit and stay
review_text: The place is very nice and seems like New Delhi. There is something for everyone in this area. From shops to eateries to entertainment places such as Nana.

review_title: Great for families and close to sky train
review_text: Straight off the sky train at Phrong Pong (spelling?) you can walk into the Emporium or its neighbor on the opposite side. Lots of shopping, restaurants, book stores etc and plenty for kids to do. There is 'Bounce' which is quite new, full of trampolines and Dinosaur Planet just down the road next to the park. Further down sukhumvit road you will find an assortment of street shops and markets with loads of bargains. If you have young kids it's probably a good idea to be aware of Nana and Asok as there are a lot of girlie bars, especially after 6ish.

We were a young group so we had a merry time in & around Sukhumvit. I have traveled to Banngkok quite a few times & make sure to stay around this location. Everything is at your finger tips !! Fro hotels to shopping malls to massages.

fun walk from soi 22 to nana and back. about 1 mile each way. great shopping from soi 5 to soi cowboy/emporium shopping center. on soi 5 is a place called buathip massage. BEST MASSAGE EVER!!! $6 US for one hour foot massage.
Sukhumvit area offers a lot! Anything from local restaurants to world known restaurant and bar chains to enjoy as well as the night market along the big street!

review_title: Always my favorite area of Bangkok!
review_text: I just recently (late June and early July 2017) visited Bangkok with my wife. As always, I chose to stay in the Sukhumvit area. This time I chose a hotel on Soi 8. Wondeful area that is full of open-air restaurants, coffee shops, bars, and shops both small and large. It truly is a wonderful area to relax and also to roam around.

review_title: Sukhumvit - great shopping and entertainment area
review_text: I always choose a hotel along Sukhumvit as I enjoy going to the shopping malls along the BTS and having lived at Ekamai (Sukhumvit soi 63) in the past, it is easy to get around with the BTS or taxis to see friends and go to Panipa Salon at Ekamai soi 19.
Skumvit is a lively street at night, from streed food to live music and so many bars, restaurants massages foot care to facials. You name it this street has it all.

review_title: Full on busy, crowded - better places to go
review_text: What a hectic part of town! There are lots of better, less crowded places to see. Even getting to Sukhumvit is one massive traffic jam.

review_title: An area that has almost everything you need
review_text: Stayed at the Marriott Marquis while I was there.  Out of the hotel were endless restaurants, massage places, street food vendors, boutiques, money changers, salons.  There are some high end malls in the area connected to the BTS. Traffic is very bad in this area most time of the day.
This airport is amazing. There are a lot of other cities that need to take note. Very easy to find your way around.
The best place in the world to hangout. You have to go to this place to enjoy bangkok. THe best in the whole country to hangout.

I lived my life in Thailand for more than 3.8 yrs. My apartment is a 5 min walk down from Nana Station which is on Sukhumvit Road, Soi 11. Sukhumvit Road has lots of street vendor selling lucky charms, momentoe, beautiful hand paintings; clothes to porn movies all on the road. Beware if you are with small kids as some of the items may be objectionable.

There are fruit sellers as well on the road wherein you can enjoy fresh fruits if you are not used to thai food taste. There are a few 7-11 shops which can be handy incase u need to purchase essentials. The road is good from 7:00 to 9:00 after which the scene changes a bit.. There are night bars ahead of Ashok BTS. Beware of a few pimps who offer you sex shows and will probably drop you at some sex parlour. Do not entertain them as you are not sure where they will take you..
Many ladies offering sex also are visible after 9:00pm and it is as simple as picking up a grocery. Post 10:00pm majority of shops closedown and then the nightlife in Bangkok begins. If you are with Family then better end you trip last by 9:00pm. There is one Iindian family restaurant that offers good food (veg/non-veg) nearby Nana station.

review_title: Good mix of street food, malls, hotels, and plenty of tailors and massage shops
review_text: Sukhumvit is huge, so do research the specific areas you want to stay in or visit. While it is very central, do note that the traffic jam can be crazy at the peak hours (in the morning and after work hours). Recommend using BTS where possible for those peak hours.
This area is an upscale location in Bangkok. it has best 5-star hotel s and good nearby shopping malls.

review_title: Best area to stay in Bangkok
review_text:
In my opinion one of the best places to stay in Bangkok. It has pretty much anything you could wish for. There are many shopping malls both high end and budget, hotels, bars, restaurants, temples and attractions. It is also accessible to other places via the BTS Skytrain which provides and efficient service. 

Only downside is the traffic but hey that's Bangkok.

This is typically traditional Thailand District mostly Muslims and Indians prefer to Stay in this Area as Halal Food and cheap Shopping is available in surroundings. Best part is few Nipali's here and some of them speak Hindi/ Urdu.  
It has huge markets and also small carts good range of Hand Bags, clothing, Mobile Phones and Asseceries but mostly its average in quality. 
All kind of Food is available in the streets having proper Dine in setups and also on go carts. 
Small massage parlours in shops must try foot massage it's decent so you can visit with family without hesitation. A lot to write about this but better to experience yourself.
Vibrant area of Bangkok city. There you can find all kinds of hotels, restaurants, bars shopping malls and night clubs. Especially, nightlife is very active.

We statyed int he Sukhumvit region of Bangkok. The streets are easy to navigate with plenty of restuarants, hotels, hostels, street vendors, food stands, restaurants, massage palors and more.  This part of the city never sleeps as vendors and tourists alike are actively seen in the streets 24 hours a day!  Rush hour is a NIGHTMARE taking up to half an hour to go one city block.  During this time, I highly recommend using the metro.  Rush hour is from 5 pm to 9 pm.
Everything within walking distance. This is a well connected road, Lot of malls. Open spaces can also be found.

Sukumvit is a constant buzz of people shopping, eating, cooking, drinking, entertaining. It seems like it never stops no matter time you go there. 

Explore the alleys too. It's not all action on the main arterial roads.
We decided to stay in this area and we are happy. Not so close to major attractions but fare in UBER is not so bad. Love that there are a LOT of food stands with tasty cheap food everywhere.

review_title: Good city for traveling, eating and sight seeing.
review_text: Very convenient BTS/MRT and other lines, cheap Taxi/Tuk-tuk places Bangkok in a very attracting place among Asian cities, we also enjoyed this city for 6 days in early July 2017.
During visit to Platinum Mall , you can see the Surrounding Malls and other locations for foods and attractions.
Sukhimvit is a long road with lots of spas, shopping malls such as emporium and terminal 21.  It's a great place to stay but more expensive than most areas.

review_title: A delight for upper scale shoppers
review_text: Visiting Sukhumvit was always a delight to visit and spend time shopping.  After finishing my job at one of the International Schools as principal I went to work for King's College of Thailand, which has one of their schools in Sukhumvit and I had plenty of time to shop and of course it was on the BTS line.  The place is right on the main street so you have  plenty of taxis and motorbikes going up and down the streets.  You will find fancy shops selling international styles, furniture, dinning establishments and businesses on the upper floors of the large building complexes.  Many street sellers will be found on the streets selling everything and anything.  It is one of the better area, however, to visit.

Great place to shop, loaf around and experience the tastes, sights and sounds of Bangkok, at all hours of the day. This is the heart of the city, with lots of busy shopping areas, lots of eateries of all kinds, and entertainment avenues of all denominations and tastes. Best way to travel here is by foot, but one can also go around by the Tuk-tuks, or by the Skytrain (BTS) which travels all along Sukhumvit and offers a good view (besides being Air-Conditioned)

If you visit Bangkok, try to book an hotel in this fantastic area . Very modern area with everything you need from shopping malls, international restaurants, transportations and night bazaars.
Enjoying the Bangkok night life.

One of the main roads through center Bangkok. There is high end shopping near the Ploenchit BTS station (BTS runs along Sukhumvit making access to any number of sites easily accessible while avoiding traffic)  Many street food carts along the way...side streets (sois) offer endless food and low end shopping opportunities if high end malls aren't your thing...
The area of Sukhumvit has everything, food, restaurants, the skytrain, street sellers and the adult entertainment.

A very good place with all in one, shopping, eating, BTS, night life, treatment, fresh fruits and street foods all you want. Very good shopping centers like Robinson, Terminal 21, Landmark etc are in Sukhumbhi, street markets are also available during evening time.
Just amazing you can walk for hours and be astonished how old an new tech overlap in these  inner Bangkok sois

I spent like 6 days in Bangkok by myself. Stayed in Marriot. I did Tai chai, that boxing and took cooing classes so was pretty indoor all the time. Didn't go out to comment on nightlife etc. A trip to Soe Cowboy - not recommended :-) unless you are looking for some sort of fun of that kind and maybe diseases :-)
I tried food of the streets and followed best restaurants as per trip advisor and as always, no disappointment with options. I will go again to try more in Bangkok and around...

review_title: Busy street for shopping and eating
review_text: Lost of shops, hotels, food. On the BTS line so very easy to move around. Look for restaurants around the BTS stop prom pong and after and you will see lots of very interesting wasting places. New Emporium shopping mall is full of amazing restaurants and food halls, old Emporium building - great new food hall.

review_title: You will either love it or hate it!
review_text:
Sukhumvit is one of Bangkok’s main thoroughfares stretching from Ploen Chit it passes through most of the tourist areas, continues out through the ever expanding dormitory regions and in fact continues all the way to Trat.

Don’t expect any of the Palaces and Temples from the tourist brochure but a thriving and cosmopolitan area combing business centres, entertainment areas, small streets and everything in between.

Sukhumvit is basically a 6 lane road out of the city and is dominated by the BTS Skytrain that has been built above the street. On each side of Sukhumvit are the Sois. These are the small streets that meander through luxury properties and simple areas.

Sukhumvit offers so much with major shopping centres, the red light districts of Nana Plaza and Soi Cowboy and thousands of restaurants, cafes and bars. If you want to experience real Bangkok try to stay on Sukhumvit as you will enjoy the atmosphere and be close to everything that you want to see in Bangkok.

In addition to being one of the Business Centres it is also a highly regarded residential area with many Bangkokians and expats preferring to live along Sukhumvit. Whilst traffic is stalled often the amazing Skytrain wizzes above the traffic making it so easy to move along this amazing street.
Easy access with the BTS skytrain, many shops and markets with street food and some beer gardens. Good area to life very convenient.

Take the long walk along the Sois' of Sukhumvit between the Sky Train stations and you will find the new and the old of Bangkok in an everchanging environment trying to find a new beginning or a return to an older era...  It is amazing how it has changed in some places and remained the same in others..
I've lived in Sukhumvit on and off for 6 years and can recommend the area especially for Expats. Great location and easy to navigate, good restaurants, shopping, transport options, hospital and safe.

review_title: Nice area with a lot of stores etc
review_text: This area of Bangkok was nice, "clean" and not so hysteric puzzling as other parts. We stayed at Møvenpick hotel, and used MRT and BTS to get around. Very nice part of city.

review_title: Bangkok Marriott Cafe and Excutive lounge a must place to visit if want to enjoy and have a memorable time.
review_text:
When visiting Bangkok JW Marriott hotel you must try eating at their café and enjoying the night time at the Executive Lounge. At the café there are so many items to select for breakfast brunch and dinner. All delicious food and desserts to choose from. All right for the price. When your enjoying your meals you will also be pleasantly surprised by the employees who served you. Their outstanding attitude and the willingness to ensure your visit is an everlasting memorable one. The Executive Lounge is the place to be at night time. Beside the comforts and the pleasures of the lounge. You will enjoy the delightful small meals and desserts that are their for your enjoyment along with various drinks to choose from. Again I don't want to forget the staff worker who work there. The also have a positive attitude and a willingness to ensure you have a pleasant evening every time you visit the Executive Lounge. In closing please say hi to the  following people who make your stay a pleasant one. These individuals deserved the acknowledgement for the job they do.  
Marriott Café
Champ, Joe, Rose, Noi, May, Fah, Joke, Pahn, Benz, Mild, Boat, Name, Pym, Aom, Satang and Net.
Executive Lounge
Manager Anja, Manager Pat, Look-Tao. Honey, Kuang, Pla, Natty, J-Dai, Kao, Noon, Chef Kan and Chef Day.

Sukhumvit is in the heart of Bangkok with lots of shopping malls with upmarket shopping.  There are also numerous good restaurants and sightseeing spots in the area.  It is easily accessible via BTS Skytrain.

If you are planning for vacation in Bangkok... then search for hotel in Sukhumvit. You will find better hotels. Specialty of this area is, it is designed for tourists. You will wide range of food options (fine dinning, street food, etc), massages  parlors (Authentic high end, notorious one and high end sex oriented). Great shopping experience (premium to mid range shopping malls, street shopping). Best place to stay in Sukhumvit if you are in Bangkok

review_title: its a tourist and hotels hub
review_text: famous for it 24 h opening places... its hub for hotels ... two most famous adult entertainment areas are nearby ... nana plaza and soi 9 cowboy ...great place to stay ..
Sukhumvit is as central as it gets. BTS runs through the road and connects one to all the places - be it mall or restaurants.  Shop, food and places to stay - you have it all in sukhumvit.
There is a lot of street food and restaurants. It is a pretty central place in Bangkok, and a good place to stay.

Main Road in Bangkok. The BTS that runs along it is excellent and best way to get around, Taxi's can get stuck in traffic.

Within easy walking distance from Sukhumvit BTS are Terminal 21, Emporium, Siam Paragon and EM Quartier for Up Market Shopping.

The "entertainment" areas of Soi Cowboy and Nana Plaza are also within easy walking distance.

stayed in Rad Blu which was great..
local bars and food stalls and right next to Soi Cowboy..
great place and must do...
Sukhumvit It is a straight line in the  Bangkok, and you can reach anywhere in Bangkok using the BTS train and at very affordable prices.

Stayed in this district in the grand Swiss hotel which was amazing. Highly recommend. The location has the skytrain which is the link to everywhere in the city. Lots of massage parlours shops market stalls etc.
Booked my hotel room in this area. Its one of the best places in bangkok to live, metro is near, terminal 21 and also soi cowboy...convenient to access many places from here
Central to all of the malls, bars and entertainment, everything including the BTS was easy to get around.

Downtown Bangkok central for most things whether looking for malls, restaurants, bars, adult nightlife  or street food and markets! Many high end hotels along Sukhumvit rd and excellent priced hotels on the sois with only 5min walk including rooftop bars and swimming pools. Travel by taxi will take ages! Use the bts and mrt both underground and overground metros which are fast, reliable and cheap
I consider Sukhamvit like home. I just always feel comfortable returning to Bangkok and this area whenever I return to Bangkok. I love it for its scenery, attractions, and liveliness.
Nice place with lots of commercial shopping malls and food joints. Easy to reach via metro. Spare at least an hour to walk around the various lanes.

I had no idea this road covered such a large area. Before I visited I have the naïve idea that I could probably walk it LOL. After having got off the sky train at Nana and having to walk to Phrom Phong to Bangkok Ink tattoo studio I realized just how much ground the road covered. Once I had my traditional sak yant tattoo done I did the sensible thing and walked to phrom phong BTS, just two minutes away ha ha. Be aware that there are different areas along the Sukhumvit line and road. It confused me. Phrong Phong has some nice areas and a lovely park. The malls are fantastic, but a bit up market with places like Cartier diamonds and Armani.

I know now the answer is to get on and off the sky train near the area you want and to explore, then get back on it to another part of Sukhumvit. It is a lot easier than trying to walk it.

Sukhumvit is a long road and area, centre to much shopping, food and entertainment

The BTS runs along the street, so if you are going from one area to another, you don't have to walk the whole way, just do some segments on the very inexpensive overhead rail (BTS)

Generally as described elsewhere....if you like jazz go to Saxophone bar located very close to Victory Monument on BTS 
. GREAT HOTELS BY ASOK. ....
I live on Sukhumvit. It has  BTS line and many restaurants and coffee shops.  The best stretch is from Soi 31-39

review_title: Very busy area of Bangkok, but interesting.
review_text: There are some lovely restaurants in the Sukhumvit area and  we felt very safe .Several nice modern hotels. Easy to find a taxi, and reasonably close to the Malls.

If they would just use the meter and not make up prices it would be so easy to get around here. 

Cool street food. 

But the crime and rip offs make this a 3*** place.

We stayed at a very nice hotel in Sukhumvit. The area has everything you could imagine but didn't leave us with a great impression of Bangkok. Loads of restaurants, bars and all. It's a shame that a lot of the 'tourism' in this area brings it down and sends the wrong message.

The best place to stay and go for shopping in Bangkok is Sukhumit Area.Connectivity is very easy.BTS train, Tuk-tuk and also Buses, Prepaid taxis all available which can connects you to different parts of Bangkok.Night walks is awesome with streets lively with People, Street foods and loud songs playing in the background.Street Hawkers everywhere with nice things and souvenirs to bring back home but make sure you bargain with them. Easy access to the shopping malls, Fine dining restaurants and Mid-range restaurants too...All the International Famous Hotels are located nearby and All Branded shops too.
I had a meeting here, at K-Tower which is in Sukhumvit road, the bad thing was the traffic jam here .... and the mall near here below my expectation ..

Sukhumvit is the best location , and safe. There are many restaurants to suit all palates, and their shopping malls great. We only paid 400 bahts to get to the airport, door to door. Don't attempt to go by the sky train, as it may work out slightly more, and the inconvenience is not worth it. Their cinema in the shopping mall is great too.

The Sky Train (BTS) Serves Soi 8, Asoke, Soi 24, all the way out Sukhumvit. For me, the Nana station is little Middle East; Asoke leads to restaurants in Soi 20 (fondue), Soi 22 (Chiang Mai food); Soi 24 (Phrom Pong Station) features Japanese on one side and the Emporium for shopping on the right.

review_title: Great location, great spa and great staff.
review_text: This hotel is located in the middle of the real Bangkok. I walked outside and saw small shrines and bought some items for myself. I also went to a store and had a dress costume made. The spa is great..yes, it is expensive but great. Massages were also great. Perfect for a honeymoon or anniversary. Nice pool and friendly staff without going crazy with your wallet. We had people translate for us to taxi drivers where we wanted to go and once walked and took a Tuk tuk. Great fun. I always like to be in an area where I can see the locals in their everyday life, and still be in a great location and great hotel.
This is an interesting area to stay in.  Public metro is easily accessible.  Lots of restaurants and shopping malls nearby.  Good hotels to stay in.
Its a central Bangkok. Generally preferred by tourists being a center point. Evenings and nights are live. Music, food, market, sex, everything sells.

Sukhumvit is conveniently located in Bangkok and is home to many good hotels and shopping malls.  The connectivity is great and one can enjoy different shades of Bangkok at the place.  It has now developed in the place to stay in when in Bangkok.

review_title: Full of good hotels, shopping attractions and food
review_text: I always choose hotels along Sukhumvit Road for easy access to the BTS, Not only that but you will also find a lot of small to big shopping malls to your choice for souvenirs, luxury items and even food. You can find many good restaurants serving local Thai and international foods and most important of all - inexpensive ! lol good night life as well !!!
Convenience place which connected to many public transportation. Fine dining, a hundred of relax cafe, many nice shopping places, a lots of comfortable hotels around.
This is just the name for an area of Bankok.   There is the usual array of shopping mall, shops, restaurants, foot spa and bars.  Felt safe walking around here even in the evening.

A lively area for tourists as there are many Hotels ,Bars , Restaurants , Massage joints etc. But cleanliness required.
Sometimes its very inconvenient as there are many commercial sex workers are all around .Streets are smelly and sometimes doesnt give you good feeling.
But still fun for tourists.

review_title: This is the street to stay when in Bangkok
review_text: I prefer Sukhumvit Soi 11 where all the good restaurants, pubs and bars are. Be aware of the Ladyboys.

review_title: Nice area. Central place in the city
review_text: Sukhumvit is central placed in Bangkok. Mix of new and old. BTS (as Nana and Asok) make traveling easy. You get everything you need, but the prices is higher than normal.

This is a bustling very busy area with lots to offer from street food restaurants  to high flying shopping malls. My brother lives here hence why I went, but I am not sure I could live here its all a bit too much and the air pollution is very heavy. I feel like I am on holiday now I am back home. Very easy to get round on the sky train, do not even attempt to cross over the roads, they are death traps

Lots of hotels and and restaurants services for the Bumrungrad hospital.  Lot's of sex workers on the streets and usual chaotic traffic but there are a lot of traffic police trying to control it all.  Easy access to the BTS which will get you around Bangkok easily.

review_title: A really great area to stay in while in Bkk
review_text: The place has a lot of accommodation to offer, numerous clubs, bars and pubs, and most of the main shopping attractions.
Great for eateries, bars and street shopping. Sky train makes it well connected. Great happy buzz going here!
The party district in Bangkok and also very good place to stay transport-wise. A lot of street vendors and small shops

review_title: Saw a brawl between Arabs.  African prostitutes grab you.  Bars are a ripoff.  Not much street foid any ore.
review_text: Used to be cheap and good..now a ripoff and sucks.  Never seen more transvestites in my life.  Most cabbies are dishonest...and all tuktuks are dishonest.  BTS is unreasonably crowded.

This area of Bangkok has it all.  Found many places for lunch and dinner.  Then you have Terminal 21 and Robinsons plus many more for shopping.  Night markets and bars plus more more more.  Would stay again in this area as everything I need is here.  Loved it.

Best place for shopping with multiple shopping malls including Terminal 21. With Nana Plaza and Soi Cowboy, Sukhumvit is the ultimate destination for nightlife in Bangkok. Has better connectivity with BTS Skytrain and MRT. Can find decent hotels and street food.
We stayed in Sukhumvit and enjoyed the proximity to many great coffee shops, restaurants and shops. Great centralized location.

Plenty of bars and great music with live bands the bar I was in had 2 live bands for the night and the drinks were reasonable. Had a great time with the music and the bands take requests which makes for a great atmosphere.

We were in bkk for just one night. Our hotel was in Sukhumvit. It has plenty of hotels, restos and pubs, catering, I guess, mainly for backpackers. This area used to have a reputation for being rather sleazy, but this time it did not have that feeling apart from a few cat-calls from one or two massage places.

A very nice area with shopping mall that is with a theme of airport. The shops are listed as flight timings on the screen and it's very clean inside! There are also many shop houses located along the way. It's actually a CBD area for the Thais so it would be quite crowded during the lunch hours but there are many street foods that you can try.
This area of Bangkok has a good night life where you will find different night clubs and good atmosphere for partying all night. It is  better to go with friends since there are plenty of women,
A shopping paradise. Very well connected in terms of commute. Loads of shopping centres and eateries.

This road is the main artery through Bangkok its where the the main street  life happens from market stalls on the road side to street food vendors although the Government are now trying the clear these areas.Sukumvit soi 5  &7 busy bar areas around Nana .
Sukumvit 23 soi Cowboy if thats your thing a must visit for 1st timers in BKK. We were showing friends around the area  Before moving on to Bangsaray near Pattaya which is a quiet fishing village  20 mins away from Pattaya so not so far if you want the crazy nightlife but enjoy the peace and tranquility during the day.Bangsaray Villa very private Villas in Tropical Garden.Around sukumvit check out Cabbages & Condoms restaurant soi 12 Nice environment  for evening dining ask for out side table.

It is centrally located area. very good connectivity to many prime locations. the streets are named as "soi" and allotted numbers. One can commute to any part of the city by bus or MRTS/tube/tuktuk. The nearest staton is Asoke. Too many hotels, restaurants, hostels,bars, massage centres to cater everyone's budget. all types of foods are available. The place picks up momentum by 10 am and remains busy till midnight.this place shall be recommended for tourists.

This is where everything goes on, so it is recommended that alal visitors to Bangkok go and check it out. The stalls selling clothing items, accessories and toys are my favourites. One can bargain with the stallholders and experience the vitality of street life there!
you can come to Sukhumwit by BTS, MRT, and bus. There are many shops and restaurants here. However, the food is expensive for Thai people.

Sukhumvit is the main street of Bangkok, having so many world class shopping centers and office building, traveling along Sukhumvit is very convenient since it does have BTS along the road, so many hotel and high quality restaurants are situated on the street too, if you stay in Bangkok, should find hotels on this fabulous street, highly recommended.

First time in Thailand for me and what a pleasant shock Sukhumvit was, busy , bustling, chaotic and thats just trying to cross the road at lunchtime :). Seriously very elegant in parts and seedy in others, depends on your preferences really, but would not hesitate to return.

Of Sukhumvit which is huge I have been on the lower part (from soi 2 till Ekamai).
The area is very well connected as it has the Skytrain, the tube and several bus routes. From Sukhumvit it is very easy to reach a lot of points in Bangkok (bus station, airport, railway station...)
This is also a great place for shoppers as there are some shopping centres along the huge Sukhumvit Road (Platinum, Terminal 21 etc...)
At night it carters for all tastes and the neighbourhood is full of places to eat ( from street food vendors to Arabic restaurants to more chilled out places that will serve Thai or Foreign food) and you have all the price ranges.   The same applies to party, in Sukhumvit you can find everything (beer or sport bars, Gogos, nightclubs...) and the hotels (you can find everything from cheap hotels, although not as cheap as Kaho San Road to luxury rooms).

Right place for staying, shopping, eating, having fun... Excellent communications by public transportation (BTS) and everything you need. Even a couple of art places worth the visit are close by, like Jim Thompson house and museum.
My favourite area of Bangkok hands down. Best restaurants & the best streetfood for me. Obviously its a bit smelly but that just adds to its craziness

"Sukhumvit" is the name of a very long road. It's called just "Sukhumvit" - no "road" or “soi". In Bangkok it is a six-lane, with very few crossing bridges, most of them skytrain stations. Crossing the actual road on foot is an offence except for the Soi 4 junction.

Walking towards Cambodia, even-numbered roads are on the right, odd ones on the left. The streets are numbered. "Sukhumvit Soi 7" is the seventh road off Sukhumvit, on the left.

Each soi is supposed to have a sign on a post, at the road edge of the pavement, near the entrance. It’s blue with white writing, including the soi number. Soi 7/1 is the first small lane after Soi 7 and before Soi 9. The signs have six sides, like rectangles with pointy arrow ends. They are often hidden amongst poles, cables, trees, etc., or missing altogether. Major roads have long unpronounceable names, but are included in the numbering system.

The "odd" side between Sois 3 and 23 is the westerner's (farang's) red light district. Plus Soi 4. You will see the world famous "ladyboys" (katoeys) along this part of Sukhumvit.

There are two local Skytrain stations: one called “Nana”  (E3 after Sois 9 & 6), and the other called “Asoke” (after Sois 19 & 16). The entrance to the Asoke MRT (underground) station is next to the Skytrain station

The skytrains stop at 23:00, and you are then at the mercy of the taxi drivers. Use a local hotel. There is a complete range to choose from along both sides. 

Street vendors start to set up shop on odd side after 10 am. Many sell tourist souvenirs and food. It’s safe to eat food from the street vendors, provided it’s piping hot and untouched by human foot. At about 11 pm, a lot of the vendors pack-up, and are replaced by food vendors and pavement bars (police permitting). All except the food vendors and bars pack up after the go-go bars on Soi 4 (Nana Plaza) and between Sois 21 and 23 (Soi Cowboy) close at 2 am.

There are lots of 7/11 shops on Sukumvit and the odd-side sois. Some of them are open all night . Robinsons is a western-style department store just before Soi 19, next door to a McDonald’s, and a currency exchange shop. There’s a western food supermarket underneath that has two cash machines which you can use to withdraw baht from your UK bank accounts. There is a vertical shopping mall called “Terminal 21” just past Soi 19, which also has a western food hall in the basement.

Trouble:
The legal and policing systems in Thailand are very different from farang ones. Thais don’t understand “fairness”, “equality”, “justice”, “human rights” and all the other current farang fantasies. Do you understand “karma”? If you get into any sort of trouble with the police, you will be given the opportunity to buy your way out. Always do so, even if it costs you your home, car, and the shirt off your back. Negotiate - yes, refuse – never, ever. Thais think that conditions in the “Bangkok Hilton” are bad. If a farang survives, his heath will be ruined for life. If he survives. ALWAYS PAY. Forget your farang innocence, it’s irrelevant in the Thai legal system. Just concentrate on healthy survival.

The nearest hospital is “Bumrungrad” on Soi 1. It is very good, and very expansive!

Great area to stay in Bangkok. Plenty of shop's, resturant's, bars and hotels all around. Handy to the Sky Train and Metro Subway. Also many taxi's and tuk tuks available. Soi Cowboy here for some fun and excitement too.
A major thoroughfare through the city -plenty of shops, restaurants, hotels and in the evening -market stalls.

This was my favorite area of Bangkok. If you like modern mall shopping with all the best designers boutiques and tons of restaurant choices, this is the place for you! This is the hipster high end part of town, more modern and sophisticated.

We stayed in a suite hotel off of Sukhumvit road. Side streets are quiet but the main road is mad busy, area is very varied from good class hotels to cheap and cheerful hostels. Lots of eating options and loads of small supermarkets and some street vendors, mostly at night. Traffic and sky train can be very noisy but make this arear good for transport

Summit is probably the busiest steel outside the city centre.
it goes on for miles east to West and is access to everything from fabulous hotels and malls to the bars of the nightlife.

A great area to stay in if you are visiting Bangkok, it is central, has great access to both the MRT and BTS transport systems as well as shopping and everything else you might be interested in in Bangkok

review_title: Big street. Each place different characteristics
review_text: Sukhumvit is very big street, so, when someone offer you to stay there, take some reference around which place you should stay.

review_title: It's a long street with one of everything
review_text:
This street has everything and then some. If you walk along at street level, you will see all of the stores, restaurants, street vendors, and get the full flavor of the city. But you can also walk above on the pedestrian walkway, and look down on the traffic and everything that is going on below. Above the street, you will also have access to the train. Nice.

Sukhumvit is a busy street, and a lot is going on. Walking the street at night is not for everyone. It can be a little disconcerting, to say the least. Warning given.

We stayed around Nana station (Soi 11), well  it's pretty interesting route to walk around, you can do walk between Nana and Siam station to reach the Siam Paragon, or the other way (Nana to Asok) to reach the Terminal 21 Shopping center. simply whatever you need to shop. night is the best time to visit.

Sukhumvit road in the center of Bangkok is a chaotic traffic jam. Suggest to use the skywalk. Super efficient and much safer. The road is busy and a lot of life especially at night. Plenty of nightlife especially around soi 4 to 17. 

But expect chaos

review_title: Its just a Street, but its big!
review_text: This street goes forever, so its hard to pinpoint what to actually write about. Its the main road along the centre of Bangkok and as such has many attractions along the way. Restaurants, movies, shopping centres, small shops, it has them all.
Long street connecting to all of down town. Busy all the time. Can get the feel of Bangkok in one interesting ride. Has the subway, sky   train and taxis every where

review_title: High end clubs, rooftop scenes and great nightlife!
review_text: After khao san road and chilled crowd, had to expore Sukhumvit for it's famous clubs and nightlife. Very different but very interesting. Octave was a great rooftop lounge, many other pubs around. Great eating options, many markets in the street, very happening in certain streets/soi's. Must spend a weekend here to explore fully.

If in Bangkok visit Sukhumvit it has everything and more, bars, restaurants, street food, shops and of course the girls. Found it fascinating walking around taking in the sights and smells, it is a very lively busy area.

review_title: Lively - food, shopping, transport.. it's got the lot
review_text: We stayed in an AirBNB here for four nights in February (soi 11 to be precise). The transport links are excellent, the street food was great, there was lots of shopping and good night life too. There were a fair few 'Westerners' in the area so many of the Thai's spoke English. The only down side for me was that it got a little seedy at night time. After 9pm there were girls lined up on the streets and sex toys being sold openly on the street markets. Not a big issue for some but not the best if you're with with your wife and kids!
There is everything in this place. There are shopping opportunities as well as lot of street side Thai food.
If you looking for hotel or shoping this is the best spot. Plus plenty of new bars and pubs. Must see!

Well almost everything, it's a bit short on Royal Palaces. But if you like eating out, hanging out in bars, seeing shows or just hanging around in shopping malls it has all of that plus lots of hotels in all shapes and sizes.

Sukuhmvit owes most (all?) of it's success to the BTS line running above it making it possibly the most accessible of Bangkok's many attractions. If you're looking for food from a particular country you're almost certain to find it on or just off the main road.

Criticisms? Not many, it's not the cheapest place to shop or eat, Taxi and Tuk-tuk drivers are waiting about to rip you off, places are constantly being pulled down to be replaced by yet another shopping mall or smart hotel and some areas are seedy beyond belief. Still if you like vibrant or exciting, this is where you'll find it.
This is a very long road and it comes to life at night. Plenty of restaurants and a good night life.This is a tourist attraction and lots of tourists at night. Had a fun night out.

Advised to visit Sukhumvit for a bit of last minute shopping before our flight back home.  I haven't been to this part of Bangkok before so wasn't sure where to go shopping but my husband and I did stop off and get some street food - it was hard to find a place free as we went near rush hour and lots of people were eating at the time.  However after having our meal, we were walking past the other stalls and I noticed a rat (nothing special in Bangkok, but then I noticed a black bin bag moving full of rats and the more I looked the more rats I saw hiding in the grates under the kerb.  I can honestly say I haven't seen as many rats all in one place before and I was glad I'd eaten before I saw this because I wouldn't have done so otherwise.  I love the street food in Thailand but I have to say the streets in Bangkok seems to be more greasier and dirty than other places I visited - I'm afraid I wasn't impressed this time.
Most happening area there. From night eatery stalls to roadside vendors of all kind of goods. Bumrungrad international hospital. Hotels. BTS train station. Good area.
The main area for shopping and eating for tourists who visit Bangkok - it is excellent and people speak better English than in other parts.

The moment U speak Sukumvit-those who have already visited bangkok will have smile on their face by just listening to the name Sukumvit..as this area offer
ALL 5 Star HoteL
Everything in Shopping
Superb Restaurent
On Road Clean Food Vendors and sitting restaurent Too
Very Good Residence Hotel
Famous Thai Message
Cheap Shopping
Night Shopping
Cabre
ALL kind of AlcohoL
I mean what not?? i just need to remember area is awake 24 hours and has a great connectivity from all place and one take BTS Line which connect entire Sukumvit..
whwnever I want to go to bangkok I prefer to stay at Sukumvit..

Spent a day wandering around Sukhumvit. If you go inside the shopping centres and awesome hotels it is fine but the streets are dirty and getting a bit run down. Not sure why as it was always a lot better than now.

review_title: A Well Heeled Khao San Road
review_text: I know it is a bizarre title but 32 years ago I was on Khao San and when I ventured to Sukhumvit I thought they were so rich.  Maybe my 15Baht per night room was more tolerable then but now I need a bit more comfort I guess.  And access to live music, finer dining, higher end shopping with air con, etc.  I must face it: I'm old and want creature comforts...

Sukhumvit is best place to stay in Bangkok for easy travel / night life / shopping. 
Why ?Center Spot , Easy access to all other tourist spot by MRT Sukumvit Station and BTS Nana/Asok station. Walking distance to COWBOY STREET / TERMINAL 21 / NANA PLAZA.

The suburb of Sukhumvit is a great area to stay in - plenty of bars, restaurants,3 big shopping malls, eateries and of course lots of nail bars, little cafes, tailors and the like. There are heaps of decent hotels in this area too

not my favortie neighborhood. Its too corporate and residential at the same time. You dont get good promotions like chinatown, you dont get good typical food, more like mixture with european and you dont get all the great monuments like in the center. Its great if you live here but if youre visiting, I wouldnt stay here...

Sukhumvit is effectively the main road through Bangkok city centre. There are lots of shopping malls and restaurants but these are all very tourist orientated. 

At night the street is lined with vendors selling everything from street food to Viagra. It is all a bit sleazy. Some of the sois (side streets) off Sukhumvit are very sleazy at night, with sex shows and strip clubs and "working girls". 

It is worth a visit but not my idea of fun. There are much more authentic places to visit.
Clean rooms, comfortable beds, great breakfast that was included in price. Quiet street and friendly staff

The longest road in Thailand, both it and its many adjoining 'sub-streets are poor on traditional tourist attractions but rich in of-the-minute shopping, eating and nightlife. The many fashionable restaurants, bars, air-conditioned malls, luxury hotels and hip clubs give the area a distinctly modern, cosmopolitan flavor.
Nothing of interest from a historical or cultural point of view. Restaurants, shopping centers and a lot of traffic.
Sukhumvit is a hot tourist destination for travelers. the place is so colorful in evening and night. lots of bars and food shops and hotels.

We stayed in a hotel in Sukhumvit.  It was convenient, because the Sky Train was there, which made the entire city fairly accessible.  There were grocery stores, so we were able to buy fruit, bread, and snacks, etc., which saved us money by not having to eat breakfast at our hotel.  There were all different kinds of restaurants, including restaurants which served western food for those who need a break from Thai food.  There were bank machines everywhere, which was convenient (the bank fees were expensive, though).  We were able to find everything we needed for our stay in Bangkok.  We didn't do a lot of exploring at night, though, as we have young children.
Probably are favorite neighborhood in Bangkok. Our hotel was very cool and it was easy to walk wherever we needed.

This was truely a remarkable place to visit. The location as central and everything is avaialble in walking distance. You can find food, malls, taxi's, etc. Basically everything you need can be found in Suhkumvit.

review_title: Cool area by night, not so much in the day
review_text: This area is best for nightlife. I loved some of the rooftop bars which give you amazing views over Bangkok. Also, there are apparently some cool international nightlcubs which you can go to, but the area on the whole is quite big and so it's relatively quiet and not too touristic or party focused.
One of the best place to hang around be it shopping, eating or entertainments all are near by including BTS station.

Sorts of characters,superb public transport.anything is within reach...Sukhumvit Road actually stretches 400 km all the way to Cambodia but the main prime Sukhumvit area is divided into few sections...starts from Chidlom where mostly medium and hi-end malls located namely Central Chidlom,Central Embassy,Central World Plaza,Gaysorn,Siam Paragon connected through sky-walk BTS Chidlom and Siam station...you don't need driving,once you're on top it links to all the malls. Ploenchit is mostly where you'll find commercial banks and Eco-friendly office blocks and modern looking apartments,condo and hotels. NaNa is the busiest and noisiest among other Soi (street)...I called it The Little Pattaya where the curiosity kills the Cat...rows of nite GoGo bars,Isan girls,lady boys,Africans and Mid-eastern joints and the other side is Farang society cheap looking bars.
If you're late nite party goers...this is your kind place. Asoke is always busy with mad traffic...all sorts characters..you could easily find many mid-budget type three star hotels located within the vicinity.
Phrom Phong is the quieter part of Sukhumvit where the famous Emporium Mall is and the up-scale Sukhumvit 24 residential neighborhood and Japanese communities and restaurants are every where...some of the finest apartments like The Marriott Residences,The President Park and the popular cut-throat Seafood Market...Its not advisable to even think about having your dinner here...you pick and they cook right away according to your taste or chef recommendation..nothing cost less than Thai Baht 2000 per KG for any seafood...never ask a Taxi man where to go for seafood and he has 25% on top your bill...haha
Thong Lo the neighborhood of wealthiness and hi-end real estate,expat communities,some of best restaurants and Hi-So Thai clubs as the locals called it. Phra Kanong and On Nut is more towards local kind neighborhood and some of the best Thai style charcoal fire buffet offers relatively cheap and you can eat drop-dead with full choices of seafood,meat and loads etc...you could adjust to anything if you know how to look for it in Sukhumvit...just don't litter your cigarette bugs on the street and the fine is about THB 2000 goes to the cops...
Sawadee Krup...

A good area to stay in Bangkok as it is near to many interesting attractions, hotels and restaurants, Rail is also near by to get to other parts of Bangkok conveniently. The number of people in this popular area of Bangkok I found off-putting, although the big disadvantage was the elevated rail line, built out of concrete, that dominated the sky around the Sukhumvit.

review_title: Nice area of Bangkok, especially due to the Emporio mall
review_text: This is a central and good area of Bangkok, with a lot of hotels, restaurants and nightlife. The Emporio mall in particular is a must visit.
if you come to Bangkok for actions and nightlife...save travelling across town and book an hotel in sukhumvit, you will save hours of transport headaches !!!

Like to wander in a new city - and found this area an eclectic mix for sure.
Nice eateries and hotels amongst street food, cheap bars and night market

First time in Thailand and ended up in Sukumvit.
I just loved the easy access to the train, the mall and all the near buy restaurants. Will stay there again during my next trip

Sukhumvit area and road connects to the widest range of attractions imaginable. Not only is it home to multiple shopping malls, restaurants, hotels and bars but the many BTS stations which allows you access to many, many more sights. Not far off the main route is the infamous Cowboy Soi.

this neighborhood is close to everything, the rivers, canals, busy streets with shopping, Thousands of options for food ,from street vendors selling scorpions to eat to Italian cuisine to Pad Thai, anything , many different kind of massage parlors , from traditional to .......... well you see for yourself, but something remains true, people are still welcoming,
Took their metro in Bangkok to several locations, and they were close to malls and hotels....but my least favorite part of Thailand was Bangkok---just like SF or NYC.  I can see the homeless at home!

review_title: Preferable zone to observe citylife in Bangkok
review_text: Maximum renowned hotels of Bangkok are situated at Sukhumvit. The street foods are awesome here. Many world class restaurants and food shops are available here like - KFC, Starbucks, Burger King, Pizza Hut, Subway, Mc Donald's etc. There are many message and spa shops available on road side. Sukhumvit is also popular for its nightlife.

review_title: Great place to stay and shop
review_text: We have now stayed in Silom area and near National Stadium but Sukhumvit area is by far the better area to stay all it missing is a few nice pubs with live music which aren't seedy and maybe a few cheaper shops

review_title: Best area to be on a short stay
review_text: Central, lots of activities, good hotels and restaurants, and excellent public transport. The only drawback, as elsewhere, is the chronic traffic pollution. My wife lost her voice the first day because of it , but despite that we spent four wonderful days here, and will be back again before we leave Thailand. Hotels are excellent, and nightime a real adventure for the eyes and tastebuds!

Sukumvit is a very long road (it goes a very long way around the country as well as in central Bangkok). the Sukumvit area in Bangkok is a great central area to stay and very handy with the BTS (skytrain) Sukumvit line and many interchanges with the MRT (underground). I first stayed on Sukumvit in 1975 and have never wanted or needed to stay in any other area since then on all my frequent visits.

review_title: A convenient and exciting area to stay
review_text:
There is so much to do in the sukhumvit area of Bangkok, and excellent transportation should you decide to go further out or in. We were able to see, eat, shop and enjoy many places within easy walking distance of our hotel. That said, I do not worry about staying within the confines of any given area as most of the city is filled with adventure and sightseeing and shopping opportunities. 
A moneysaving tip: if you have expensive medications they will almost certainly be much less so in Bangkok.
My wife purchased 90 day supplies of hers for much less than she pays for the insurance co-pay in the USA. There is no need to have a prescription with you. She did bring the US printouts, and in some cases the original box, of the medications she wanted. Pharmax is a good place to get them, it is less costly than some of the other pharmacies.

Sukhumvit area of Bangkok is the modern hub consisting of modern shopping malls, hotels, restaurants and entertainment centres. The area is serviced by BTS skytrain in the centre, MRT on the side and is neat and clean. It is the heart of modern Bangkok.
It was good to experience another part of Bangkok. Not too far from all the shopping malls. One could walk without having to use public transport

If you have been to Bangkok you probably were on Sukhumvit Road and didn't know it. If your in the city your on Sukhumvit.
Hotels, restaurants and shopping

review_title: Expensive with high end shopping centres
review_text: While the area is modern with high-end shopping centres such as terminal 1 and central world, the prices are also comparable to back home, therefore defeats the need to lug it all home.  Having spent time in the old district khao san road, you tend to miss the street pad thai's, night markets.  The area does have a lot of indian restaurants, tailors and a muslim-halal food theme.

review_title: Sukhumvit is one of THE main roads full of life
review_text: When one plans a trip to Bangkok the map of this enormeous city tends to puzzle. However there are a few main roads and Sukhumvit is one of them. It is also the longest road in Bangkok. Walking down Sukhumvit together with its side streetsoffers you a good impression of local life. Sukhumvit, SiLom Road and maybe Kao San road(although this last one becoming less attractive) are worth the visit on top of the main attractions.

review_title: Comfortable Safe Area to Stay or Spend Time
review_text:
I stayed for 4 days in Sukhumvit and found it a clean, safe and vibrant area well connected by rail to the airport and other parts of Bangkok that tourists might visit, Siam Square for example. 
Plenty of good hotels here, lots of restaurants too and good street food and life. 
Found it very safe and friendly.

review_title: Central and happening place in the city
review_text:
This is main place in the city where all malls,markets and attractions for kids are there. well connected through BTS.
if you are planning a visit to Bangkok stay in here only. the place has all the hotels around.
It has good connectivity to rest of the Bangkok, via BTS Sky train stations. It also has some great shopping malls, if you are a shopping freak.

The centre of action in Bangkok, a great range of shopping from Terminal 21, Siam Paragon, Central World, and Emquartier (and more), a huge amount of restaurants, bars and entertainment. Easy to get around using the BTS system, and occasionally for fun a Tuk Tuk or Bike.
Sukhumvit is a busy but very nice place to visit. Here you find all the traders selling different items from traditional medicines to clothes and households items.
This neighbourhood is close to both the skytrain and subway, making it a very convenient location. There's also a beautiful but expensive mall there.
