I lived right off this street and it can get really busy with traffic in both directions. The area is interestingly nice because you can find a lot of things to do and see from traditional items and buildings to new malls and international neighborhoods.

Basically a street where you can visit malls and do business. It was okay when I needed to go  for a meal in a shopping  all because I could easily tram over and back to the business sectorhotel a small tour I took booked for me.

review_title: One of the city centers of Bangkok
review_text: Lots of bars, shops, and shopping malls can be found along Sukhumvit. It is easy to move along Sukhumvit by the skytrain (BTS).

This place was dirty, smelly and disgusting. So many girls selling "massages", and stalls out in the open selling medications that normally require a prescription, as well as steroids and an array of sex toys. Definitely not family friendly. 
Would not return.
Nearly all restaurants were Indian, so not many options available for dinner

Just awful, everything that is bad about Thailand or Bangkok ends up in this area. It's like the sewer of Bangkok. Day or night it's unpleasant.  Noisy, rip off, prostitution,  fake stuff, dirty and sexy tourists in soi cowboy.

review_title: Check what's going on before you try to spend too much time visiting.
review_text:
When we visited (and stayed for 2 weeks) in Sukhumvit it was a lot of fun, but it drastically changed due to military/police regulation changes.  
In Jan/Feb 2016 the neighborhood was bumpin' at night.  It was full of street markets, vendors and shoppers.  It was obviously good for shopping but it was also good for goofing around and people watching.  Upon returning for another 2 weeks in Dec 2016 regulations had changed; there was about a quarter (if not less) of the action we saw at the beginning of the year and a lot of the space has been taken over by ladies for hire.  We still enjoyed ourselves and made the best with what we were dealt but a lot of the action has moved to Khao San Road.

review_title: Smelly dirty and noisy, but good fun!
review_text: Sukhumvit road is huge! From soi 4 up to soi 17( I think!) Is basically girls and bars. Few nice restaurants scattered in between and some great bars. The night market along the pavement doesn't seem to be that big and more, and has been replaced with people selling Viagra and sex toys. Further up are some very expensive condos and some more shops. A great night out, but don't bring the kids!!!

Busy street, full of interesting people and things to see and do. 
A little bit dirty, but ok in general :)

review_title: I like it but there is Rush
review_text: It's nice to visit on road and its safe on roads , but there is difficulty with muslim foods also. i think more qualities muslim food is needed there
Sukhmuvit covers Ashok, Nana and Silom area which are the main attractions for shopping and eating for locals and tourists alike.  BTS and MRT are available
this is a touristic place with restaurant, bar, clubs and girls but local people are more honest than patpong (tourist trap). It's my favorite place in BKK. Good 7 days a week

I was mesmerised with this place when i travelled to this location for the first time. Bcoz this is one place where you have SOI COWBOY(Strip clubs) and NANA PLAZA(World's Largest Adult Playground-Strip clubs).These are the 2 places which you should visit only with friends or alone never with family.Wat a place ....but on top of it you should have your wallet full if you really want to enjoy the best time of your life. On Sukhumvit you also have MALLS and road side shopping streets in order to enjoy your day. Not only this, the best place for pubs, discotheque and one of the costliest drinking hubs ....!!!! Not to miss out on this.....

Many people like Sukhumvit, but I despise the area. Might as well be in NYC. Slick expensive shopping malls and hotels. A universe away from real Thailand people and culture. 
Don't be a victim of consumerism and a world of uniformity. Explore real cultures of Asia.

review_title: After a week our favorite neighborhood.
review_text: Spent the week in Bangkok and visited many of the neighborhoods several times. Without a doubt this was our favorite and one that we went back to many times. Next trip we plan on using this as base camp and using the metro and taxis to get around. There is a selection of top notch restaurants, street markets, shopping malls and nice hotels. Mixed in is also a lot of private residences and the neighborhood shows it. Everything your heart desires.

Great for people watching and getting into the hustle of Bangkok. So many different little sois to go down that each have their own vibe. Most restaurants/pubs on the main road are a bit overpriced but you can find a few little hidden gems.

review_title: A day at the Sukhumvit Road
review_text: A swarm of cars and motorcycles crowded during the massive Sky train-structure running parallel to the street, and the warm air was thick with exhaust fumes.A natural feature of the Bangkok street scene. Emporium at the Skytrain station Phrom Pong is one of Bangkok's most exclusive shopping center.Fantastic opportunity to try street food Thai style.Several of the major hotel giants are represented here, and public transportation is good.

Sukhimvit is my home in the city of Bangkok. I consider this neighborhood as my second home. Sukhumvit area is popular for shopping and nightlife. It is also home to some of the backpackers who visit Bangkok.
I stayed here and it was very convenient for everything. Skytrain has stops as MRT. There are some good shopping malls.

many fashionable restaurants, bars, air-conditioned malls luxury 
.hotels and clubs 
Making exploring its staggering length incredibly easy is the Skytrain (BTS) that runs through most of its downtown stretch.

Sukhumvit is a long way with lots of shops, malls, eateries, restaurants, sky stations. Always busy with traffic. One can feel the vive of city life of Bangkok. The pavements are OK but one has to be careful while walking as they are not even. In places, there are small potholes or damaged. Our friend fell down as he did not notice uneven path. It is fun to walk and see different activities on the street.
I found this place a good place to stay and the big plazas I enjoyed going thru them & buying different things for my family
A central place to be in a fun area best thing is it is on sky train route so quite easy to get to all the places you want to see

review_title: Vibrant Enjoyable Cultural Diverse Area of Bangkok
review_text: When I travel to bangkok 2-3 times a year, I always stay at a hotel on or near Sukhumvit Road in Central Bangkok. The BTS runs along this road, and makes traveling a quick and efficient way to get around Bangkok. Sukhumvit Road has all one needs in terms of sightseeing, restaurants, shopping malls (6), great hotels for both mid and upper range prices. Great views of the city from hotels along Sukhumits. And this area is very cultural of Thailand and offers a great opportunity to interact with the Thai people and other people of various countries around the world as well.

review_title: My favorite area to go to in bkk
review_text: This is my favorite area to go to in bkk. Yoou can find ANYTHING you want, there is a nice crowd of people and you will experince a good bit of Thai culture in a short period of time
helpful staff, i was lost here but staff help me, even they couldn't speak in English.. nice and challenging experience haha

What do you want in Central Bangkok?
It doesn't matter, it's probably here, unless you want a nice quiet time, in which case you had best stay away.
HOTELS, there are many, from the grandest to the lowliest hostel.
SHOPPING. From the glitziest world leading malls to the street hawkers. Lots of cheap clothes and watches etc etc with wares displayed/hawked street side.
WATS & SHRINES. Wat Pathumwanaram is on Sukhumivit. There are many shrines in the close locality including the Erawan shrine on Sukhumvit.
RESTAURANTS, from the worlds best (expensive) to the cheapest of street foods, always excellent. 
Cuisines from around the world are available including Western offerings such as KFC, McDonalds, Subway, Burger King and Starbucks plus others.
BARS, from the swishest lounges to dingy dives. Spend as much or as little as you want.
GIRLS, if that's your thing, as many as you want, go to Nana or Soi Cowboy. It is true that there are some street girls and gatoys but they don't hassle you.
TRANSPORT, so well connected via the BTS or MRT. Any number or Tuk Tuks (be sure to agree a price before you set off). Taxis are everywhere (make sure the meter is on, if not skip to the next taxi), really cheap transport especially if there are two or three of you.
MONEY EXCHANGE, better rates than you get at home. Try Superich at Asoke BTS on route to Terminal 21. Don't forget your passport.

I can't think of anything you cannot get here except a quiet time.
If you love going in and out of malls, selling all sort of goods, then book a hotel on Sukhumvit, the heart of Bangkok's retail district.

Our hotel in Bangkok was located in Sukhumvit - soi 15, the area is a nice place to walk about, its easy enough to navigate around and had lots of bars, food places and some street stalls 

Well worth a look when in Bangkok

All the smells, colours and day to day passings of Bangkok, a must walk day or night to take in all that Bangkok offers from street markets, street food and street life. Entertainment for free...... watch Bangkok pass you by
Good place to live.  Loads of cafes, restaurants and bars.  Great shops.  Feels very safe.  Lots of hotels... all price ranges.

Busy, stinky, dirty, are some of the adjectives that apply to this dirty highway. Seems like all the people in Bangkok decided to have a party and they converged on Avenue Sukumvit to create a giant traffic jam.

Sukhumvit is one of the main tourist areas of Bangkok - we stayed in here and spent most time here and there was everything we need for shopping and eating - but most tourist attraction for take photo were in a different area.
this is an area in bkk, it is nice for shopping during the day time in the shops and mall, at night the carts come out, you will enjoy

review_title: I wrote about "Sukhumvit" a few months ago and it was deemed too controversial to include so here goes again
review_text: My review was basically was to say this subject is a bit unclear or perhaps too wider area and needs to be narrowed down to parts of the Sukhumvit area. I assume most people are more interested in the tourist area which is at the lower end of Sukhumvit Road starting at Soi 1 through to say Asoke (Soi 21) or is it further down to Soi 24. But Sukhumvit road itself is the longest in Thailand so it is a bit of a challenge. Well what I can say is the tourist area is now greatly changed the crowded pavements of street vendors ready to strike you a bargain which they had just bought from the local markets and now selling to you at twice the price or more, can now only sell on the pavements in the evenings, so more pressure to make a killing. The older building and bars are also disappearing slowly and the character of the area is definitely changing, if for the better is your choice. The stream of polluted water from Soi 3/1 (affectionately known as soi Africa) has been finally been fixed in Thai fashion. You will still be pestered by the LB's if you are male for some fun massage around soi 7 but hold on to you valuables! I find the best places to eat are on Soi 11 and across the street at Soi 8. The choice of hotels on Sukhumvit is outstanding when 4 or 5 star is your choice, and still some good bargain hotels are on the sub Soi's. If other types of entertainment are your thing then Soi Nana 4 and Soi cowboy 21/23 are the places to head. Bars up to today i.e. the 15th November have been restricted in what entertainment that could be shown due to the Thai Kings recent death. But just noticed live bands are now allowed. Local TV can now show Thai soaps as long as not controversial - I know you will love them.

review_title: Awesome central location; ตั้งอยู่ในทำเลที่น่ากลัว
review_text: A great place to visit with lots of malls to visit for window shopping and to find good bargains. Lots of restaurants in the vicinity with Western and Asian cuisine to choose from. You see massage parlours around every corner. The best thing is to visit here when it is not too crowded.

very lively street with variety of shops and restaurants and local food outlets.
select your hotel nearby and enjoy

On Oct 7th I walked from Asok to Nana; I was suddenly stopped by the police, extremely polite and fluent in English. “Sit down” they asked me...”enquête”.....
Lots of small stores and foodstalls, on the footpath were removed by the government. They wanted to know my opinion. Agree/Disagree and why? They asked me to write down my opinion about it; and they needed my name and nationality....When I left they saluted me as if I were “Clinton”, huhu.

Sukhumvit isn't the same anymore: less vendors, less stores, impeccable clean, even no cigarette buts in the street anymore...less prostitutes... less beggars.

Is the new look of Sukhumvit positive or negative? Can't give you an answer... I can only say that it is not the same anymore, at least not between Asok and Nana.

review_title: A happening place to stay in
review_text: One of living places on earth, where you can get the most of nightlife. Easy access to markets, MBK, Siam Paragon and a night market. No one can get tired of staying there.
Some years ago, I did not like Sukhumvit very much, but now it is the place to stay for me. Central and some nice; e.g. Terminal 21!

Well then, first night in Bangkok and we went for a stroll from the Thai cultural centre to sukhumvit road and along, never felt more at ease. Just be aware of surroundings and the locals will not be a bother. Of course there are girls looking for trade but that is the same in any major city. 
All in all, a great place to be.

Time of your visiting will depend on what you see or don't want to see, plenty of stalls, and shopping , great eateries and easy access to other regions of the city, night life can be an awesome experience, just be aware where you are and watch your stuff, a good time can be had,
Anywhere close to lumpini  Park  Sukhumvit area seems to be good spot easy to walk to night life eateries etc close to Sky train as well.

All around Sukumvit there are numerous good quality shopping malls with all the top brand names from around the world. In addition all these malls have extensive areas just for dining out. From Mac's to fine five star dining. Be warned, DONT shop on Saturdays. All th youngsters from out of town flood into Bangkok on Saturdays to do, well, what they do.
If you are thinking of going to Thailand at this time(November 2016), again, be warned. Since the death of their King everything to do with entertainment has been put on hold. No singing, dancing, or generally making merry. A national football game was held in October where the crown were told not to cheer!!
The Thai's will mourn their King for one year.

review_title: Indigo Salon Gateway, Sukhumvit, Ekamai BTS... One of the best salon service
review_text: Indigo Hair Salon at Gateway Ekamai Shopping Mall, right at Ekamai BTS station.  Providing good ambience, hight quality product used, experienced hair dresser.  Especially the service is superb at very reasonable price.  It is one of the top salon with relatively cheap price.

One of the most happening places with active nightlife is Nana. It has lots of clubs and bars. Lots of good dining options to catering to tourists from various countries.

Also beware its a place where lots of unwanted things can happen too.

To avoid the severe prostitution area keep away from the Nana BTS train area. Most tourists and their 'guests/clientele' will be getting off or on from heres -some from the night before.

Sukhumvit is a very long road but Phrom Phong BTS metro station is more pleasant and respectable because there is a large upmarket department store/centre with classy restaurants and shops with prices to match.

This area is alive with activity and offers shoppers the chance to find everything imaginable. The Emporium has been revamped and is bigger and even more luxurious on inecstretch then getting closer in tontown you have so many shopping malls  to choose from it is mind blowing, Central World and Siam Paragon two of our favorites. We loved this area.

Sukhumvit is slowly becoming a strange , maybe even dangerous place . Ive been going there for about 5 years and it seems to be deteriorating each visit . There seems to be large numbers of African people congregating along  Sukhumvit rd  , the males offering drugs and the women offering themselves to men walking along the footpath  , both being a little aggressive in the way they approach and pester me anyway . 
There is also a seemingly ever increasing presence of Moslem people in the area and it seem they are creating a world of their ow particularly in Soi 3 & 5 , and at times become rude and aggressive when walking along the road in large numbers .
The demographic has made me reconsider staying in Sukhumvit in the future , which is disappointing as there is some good night life venues and food places  in the area . If your going to go I suggest you don't carry valuables and avoid walking the streets alone at night . Shame .. but that's what's happening.

Clean and modern city. People are friendly and if yo are travelling with family, an ideal area to stay. All the facilities are available, including shopping malls, MRT, Taxi services etc. But don't forget to visit Local market and buy stuff from them. You may have to bargain, but they are friendly. Food is clean and tasty.

review_title: This is where all the action is
review_text: This area is center of night clubs and entertainment; Nana plaza near the Nana station and some night clubs near the Asok station has more crowd. As far as food is concerned, you will find all kinds of cuisines around. The Indian and Arabic/Lebanese outlets are mostly in the Soi3
one of the largest road in bangkok many shopping malls n area of attraction sky train mostly pass ol along this road
An array of shopping/tailors/eating places. Noticed that the area has been cleaned up and no side hawkers stalls remain, which gives the area a safe feel.

This is where all the action is and it is within walking distance. Nana is a great stop more stuff going south and on either side of the road lots to see and lots to do. So many eating establishments and the back roads are fun to just walkabout. even a bar or two.
this is a great area to stay  it is the centre of town and has sky rail and also underground rail too shops malls eating places

review_title: Sukhumvit  Area is good for Shopping and also good Asian Food
review_text: Very nice area to live... Its good for shopping and also good for Asian Food on little bit costly side.

review_title: Great Restaurants, Shopping and Hotels; Expat Central
review_text: Sukhumvit is home to great hotels, restaurants of every kind, the fancier shopping malls and many massage parlors and spas. The Sky Train makes it all convenient and easy access. Just be sure to visit the more historic parts of the city. Most days, traffic from Sukhumvit to the more histtoric areas and temples/wats can be a bit of a bear, so expect additional travel time. One thing I loved about Sukhumvit is that there are so many vegetarian restaurants nearby. And it's easy to navigate the streets.

Seen as the tourist centre of BK and it pretty much is where most expats congregate. Lot of bars and shopping around and pretty much anything you want you can get it. Streets are busy at night time but much easier to navigate now that the street vendors are gone. Lot of good places to play pool also.

This is a great area to base yourself.  We always stay a few minutes walk from Nana station, and there are plenty of market shops in the evening.  Massages as well as Cheap Charlies and plenty of places to eat are here. We always feel safe, even with the working girls along the street we stayed in.  They always said hello.
The only criticism I would have is that the traffic is a nightmare for arrival in a taxi, or even departing for the airport in a taxi.  Slow slow slow.

This is an area that can be a lot of fun.  But I would say it is an area for familiies as it has a lot of night life.  But there is also some great shopping with Termial 21 right there and the rail system.  Food here for the most part is very good.  I suggest being bold and just eat at the sidewalk eatery's.  I have never got sick.  The food is excellent and only a 10th of the cost of restaurants.  Jump on the rail system to get around and see so much of what Bangkok has to offer.
Sukhamvit is the mai road through Bkk , lots of hotels shops market stalls I love it there , there's nothing you can't get , movies bars, shopping malls its busy but good
This is Bangkok. This is the center, the suburb. You have to go here to know what's Bangkok. I stayed 4 days here last July. Around Soi 7

This place is bustling 24 hours per day!!!!!  Lots of high end shops, massage parlors, and great eating and drinking establishments!!!  I feel very safe in this area!!!  but it's so crowded!!!!!!!!  I'm staying for the month at the Sofitel in Sukhumvit!!!!!

review_title: Sukhumvit is the center point in Bangkok.
review_text: Busy, clean, Walker's delight. This road that sprawls about 2 miles touches on all aspects of tourist's time on the streets. There are majestic hotels on the main drag and more budget hotels on the streets leading from it. But be careful of friendly females who may rub you the wrong way and steal your wallet. At night time, street vendors set up shops and compete with the established stores. Bars and restaurants abound. I met my goal of losing weight by venturing out on this street at all times of the day or night. Explore the stores below the stores and above as this is a shoppers' paradise.

You can find everything in Sukhumit. Shopping, good restaurant, Spa, night life. Just name it! Good place for tourist.
But if you go by taxi, there are always traffic.

The night market/stalls have it all - and they are open whether dry or - as in our case - lots of rain at night. But, it is still fun to walk the street and see all walks of life - if you alone as a male you might get asked whether you like some company - but as a couple or family it felt very safe and was very interesting.

review_title: The heart of bangkok with all the happening events.
review_text: The place is the biggest road of thailand there are blocks called soi all around it has a skytrain line across both the sides having hotels and many shopping malls NANA is the famous place for the nightlife of bangkok with bars hotels restaurants all around this area has many apartments and high rise buildings with famous places like siam center,madam tussad , ocean world .On the road side there small shops and hawkres selling various items name anything you wont find here beware of pockets.Nice place to shop around and stay even.

review_title: I would suggest one stays in this area
review_text: Loads of great hotels, eateries, mall, the BTS train. It is very centrally located to go anywhere. You can just walk around a discover a lot.

Sukhumvit was the first place I was introduced to when I said I wanted to buy a few things for home... and wow ...  talk about choices from the stalls - from watches, to food, to virtually anything you can possibly think of... its all there. Quite crowded though but worth the visit...you might get a bargain or two.
most happening place with malls and star hotels. You can find many eateries lined up by evening in this area.soi cowboy is in sukhumvit

review_title: Busy with plenty to see & do
review_text: Plenty of shops and outlets to visit. Sukhumvit is a big place so you would need a bit of time to get around. Great central location to stay if you want to be near the action.

review_title: Easy access and full with great place to eat and very near the train and metro station
review_text: Just really enjoyed the Area very much. Got here on my arrival in bangkok and decided to come back to the same area after coming from the Island located south thailand!
On Sukhumvit you will find everything, from Nightlife, nice Streetfood, local Shops and market Dealer, also big Shopping Center.
Fantastic place for travel ,shopping, good shopping malls, nice resturants , good abeance and most of very beautiful nice cultured people.

review_title: Main road with lots of shopping opportunities
review_text: This very long road is a great tourist attraction.  It has many shopping malls, restaurants and shopping stalls.  It is busy 24 hours a day.  The traffic is usually very heavy particularly in the evening. The BTS service that runs along it to be recommended.

Sukhumvit road is the gest location to stay while in BKK. It provides a great number of eaying, shopping and entertainment venues. All is very easilly accessible either on foot or BTS or taxi.
More cultural attractions ard in a short range as well.
Sukhumvit road is the location with a huge offer of hotels and apartments, from budget to luxury, to plan a stay in BKK.

long long street with all what you can think about
many resturants all kind of food
many Spa
many coffee shops
lovely street

Sukhumvit is the place to be in Bangkok. Future, modern developments of Bangkok will continue to be established in Sukhumvit. It is served by the BTS which ensure smooth and convenient access to Sukhumvit from elsewhere in Bangkok.
Have a few hrs out shopping ,nice aircon to cool you off .Food stalls to stop for a drink or food , it's nice for a relaxing few hrs .
One of my favorite locailties in Thailand , great buzzing vibe and the perfect place to stay to if you want to enjoy some great pubs, food and access to transport

review_title: Heart of the city - the best locality to stay
review_text: It has everything you need - shops, restaurants, parks and malls.It is easy to reach and has good connectivity.

You can find everything you need on Sukhumvit. Shops, restaurants, parks and malls.
Easy to reach by BTS and taxi.

review_title: One of the best locality to stay
review_text: Sukhumvit Soi 4 to 30 is very good place to stay with decent locality, good connectivity to metro, or taxi plying through all the time, good nightlife and lot of restaurants nearby. Would recommend this place for any couples or family

review_title: Its the heart of the city and place to be there.
review_text: Sukhumvit is an area and has quite a few good restaurants and is a shoppers paradise. A walk down the sukhumvit raid and you can get street shopping at good bargains. There are a couple of malls too.
affluent, clean, great shopping, nice people, great area to stay in and easy to use the skyrail to get around

review_title: Perfect Way to Get About Without Getting Lost
review_text: I love the Sky Train (BTS), it is so easy. Takes you to the popular places and there are excellent guides. If you do a lot of travelling in one day then get a day pass. You can also get a smart card to load money and just tap in and out. If you buy just a single journey ticket you must use the machine and there are long lines in busy times.

A really beautiful area full of clubs, restaurants, bars, massage shops and with many people coming from many different countries. 
Here there is a BTS: easy, safe and cheap transportation to move yourself in Bangkok.
You can also find many good hotels, for any pocket.
Very nice place for a great vacation and also for living..full of life, day and night. 
Really amazing!

I love this place very much. Central location for all the tourists. You will get here everything you want: restaurants, 3-5 star hotels, shopping malls, bars...If you like Bangladeshi food you can visit the restaurants at Soi3.
Good area to stay in - a lot better than the Kho San Road - best to avoid the area around Nana and Cowboy
Great area to stay while on a visit to Bangkok. Centrally located. Lots of shops and the sky train has easy access.

Sukhumvit is basically Downtown Bangkok. All the Luxury Malls,5 star Hotels, Restaurants etc are situated here. Surrounded by skyscrapers and dazzling with bright lights, this is the most happening area of the City. Our Hotel (Westin Grande) was situated here.
You can just walk around all day and not get bored. But the place really comes alive at once the sun sets. Bright lights, cars, crowd and skyscrapers everywhere. This place is busy all the time.  
But the traffic gets really bad here during the rush hour from 3pm to 8pm so avoid taxi and use the BTS instead. The Asok station operates for this area.
Many top shopping centers and hotels here. BTS Skytrain makes getting around possible despite terrible traffic on the street itself a good part of the day.

We stayed in a hotel in the heart of Sukhumvit. This is the 'happening' street with high end shopping malls, five star hotels, entertainment outlets and trendy restaurants and bars coexisting with push carts selling street food, wet markets, sundry shops and family run eateries with tables on the side walk. Traffic jam the street day and night with the BTS running along this stretch providing relief from the daily traffic chaos.

WOW! This place really is the place to visit in Thailand, great atmosphere and all located all in one place. Great place for tourists to do some shopping, however please be aware of street sellers; selling anything from selfie sticks to zap zaps also known as an electric stun gun torch. These may seem as novelty items, here in the UK they fall under section 5 of the Firearms Act. Please do not fall victim to this!!

This road is a beautiful amalgam of everything Thai. The high end hotels and shops scattered with street vendors selling authentic Thai food. The road is filled with traffic and people one can see the spirit of Bangkok here.

Billboard pub at level 3, Plaza Nana, Sukhumvit Soi 4 is a rip-off.

My friend and I went there last week, ordered one drink for ourselves and one lady drink for a girl each, totaling 4 drinks. After 15 mins, the bill showed 9 drinks costing 1,400 baht. We pointed out the extra drinks and told the waiter to reduce the bill.  However, the bill ballooned to 2,500 baht with 6 more drinks added to the bill with 2 bouncers looking on fiercely. We have no choice but paid up and just walked out, never to return to this place. 
To all patrons, never enter BILL BOARD ! They are scamming and conning tourists !
This is THE road in Thailand. Everything you want to see or find and everything you didn't want to see or find is all on this road, especially between Soi 5-21

This is the busy heart of Bangkok with lots of shopping and food options. Glossy high end mall sit comfortable beside street vendors pushing fab road side fare and buskers touting a few Bhat. Take a tuck tuck or the metro. Its an easy area to navigate. In this area are loads of tourist activities so plan in advance to make the most of your day

This is a shopping, entertainment and hotel area. MRT and BTS converge here. A lot of traffic converges here. The SOI Cowboy is close by and there is a variety of establishments. Tourists can find all sorts of items from the high prices to the bargain basement. Follow the locals is a good tip to identifying good, well priced shops and restaurants.

Don't miss the Sukhumvit area during your stay in Bangkok for a taste of real Thai culture, from the great shopping choices, to the custom tailors and great food. Some of the restaurants have tables right on the side walk, where you can people watch as you savor your delicious meal.

review_title: A very long road with a lot of stuff
review_text: Sukhumvit most commonly refers to the area around the MRT stop Sukhumvit, which is the same as the BTS stop Asok. There are tons of bars, restaurants, malls, and hotels in this area. If Bangkok has a downtown, it's probably within a mile of this place. Sukhumvit road is actually about 20 miles long, and there are tons and tons of things on this road.

review_title: Good area for shopping and drinking
review_text: You will find everything you need here. Sukhumvit has become the go to for expats and tourists alike. Very busy at night.
The area is very good and lively but needs to be cleaned up a bit.The good thing is everything available around this area this place seems to alive 24/7 specially with the Foodland supermarket there.

review_title: The traditional area for first time visitors
review_text: This area is not the best area for long time visitors knowing Bangkok or Thailand.  Crowdy and loosing the Thai characters.

review_title: Not a great attraction any more.
review_text: the street market was big disappointment.they are rude and not interested to talk to any Brown/ Asians. Quality of products is also very poor. Not recommended at all.

review_title: Very Busy shopping area with multiple centres, good transport links
review_text: Rather chaotic busy and dirty streets, but many shopping opportunities with local as well as international brands, at 1/4 of the West's costs. Some security concerns with bombs in this area within the last year, but generally feels safe if not chaotic. Still popular among tourists, though i would stay off this main busy noisy area if you stay in a Hotel.
Sukhumvit is the heart of Bangkok where all the fun is. Close to malls, X district (which some people consider part of the sight seeing), a lot of bars and markets.

review_title: A must see for one evening in Bangkok
review_text: This area of Bangkok is wild and wooly. Loads of bars, shows, market stalls, restaurants and other things. Not a thing for the whole family but good adult fun. The people are friendly and will take no for an answer.
I always choose to stay in Sukhumvit area because it's filled with hotels with reasonable price, restaurants, shops, bts stations and spa.

We stayed at Bangkok Marriott Sukhumvit during our stay and the place is very convenient. Just next to the BTS and so many shops, restaurants, massage places that you can check out , all by walking :) . Where we stayed was at Thong Lo and it was just next to Phrom Phong where the Emporium shopping mall was exactly situated, at the Phrom Phong BTS station! Amazing.
This is place where walking is fasting than taxi.  You can enjoy a lot of street food and shopping here!

review_title: Melting pot of culture, food, shopping, nightlife and sleaze!
review_text: Absolutely loved walking up and down Sukhumvit, being offered everything from elephant pants to viagra and blowjobs whilst looking for breakfast on my first morning was eye opening! A truly diverse area!

review_title: SkyTrain puts Sukhumvit at your fingertips
review_text: From two of the most amazing malls you'll ever visit to hotels and the Jim Thomson outlet, they are all on this one street and running just above it the Sky Train will move you from one to the next. Paragon Siam with its Rolls, Bentley, Aston Martin and other high end car dealers in the mall, and the Emporium/EM District with its helix restaurant building provide upscale and beyond upscale shopping. Aquariums, IMAX theaters, nightclubs all on one street particularly if your hotel is on this same street

Sukhumvit is a long major street that runs east-west. The are numerous malls, hotels, restaurants, pubs (where you can hear live music), apartments, and stores along the street. You will find hidden gems on the side streets. It is mainly a touristy area of the city, so security is good. If you get tired, you can catch the sky train above.

This main artery of the city is a series of different shopping malls, most of which are high-end for affluent locals and tourists. The road is very long... 10ks... but easily navigated on foot (the various malls are mainly interconnected) or via the SkyTrain that literally runs above Sukhumvit Rd from Siam Square through to Ekkamai and beyond...
Sukhumvit is famous tourist place and well connect to city and Airport, lots of hotel and restaurant around, good night life all street
Sukhumvit hosts of some great Indian restaurants and road side shopping.You will get dirt cheap clothing and other stuff. Terminal 21 mall is also in the vicinity.

review_title: A good area to roam around
review_text: Centralized location with good connectivity with various attractions. Very busy place. Too many hotels.
Go hear at night to catch all the little stalls and smells of the cooking. Nice stores too. Just the traffic is bad so go early.

review_title: Sukhumvit is long. Visit the side streets (sois) and revel in what's there.
review_text: I would say daylight hours are great for the malls, which are interesting even if you don't like malls. Terminal 21, Central Embassy, Emporium and more. For a bit more excitement try the side streets after dark. Pretty astounding the difference between night and day. A bit of everything from high class restaurants to low class massage parlor so. Nana soi 4 (x rated), Soi 11, and many more all the way to Thonglor BTS stop. And yes, the BTS sky train can take you from the beginning to the end of Sukhumvit. But take good walking shoes cause that is your best way to see the real thing.

Everywhere you look in the Sukhumvit area there is a huge variety of culture. from "Little Africa" to areas populated by westerners and Korean and Japanese expats alike. Sukhumvit is effectively the "hub" of Bangkok. Theres something for everyone, nightclubs, bars to restaurants and shopping malls.

We stayed near Suhumvit just to experience the excitement of the nightlife. Sometimes it was a bit seedy but overall it was just fun and always had stories to tell every time we walked home and people watched. I wouldn’t bring little kids here at night but during the day it was ok.
Always stay in this locality during our annual vacation.Ideal for gastronomic treats,street shopping as well as malls.

review_title: Central location, the artery of Bangkok
review_text: Great place to hang around. Sukhumvit offers some for all tastes. Shopping, nightlife, eating and relaxing.

Sukhumvit probably the best area for eat & fun. Plenty of malls, but by far my favorite the new TERMINAL 21. Great people viewing, too. Nightlife for men mainly at Nana-Plaza & Soi Cowboy. But plenty of nice restaurants and hotel bars around, too.

We liked the shops and the shopping malls along the Sukhumvit road area such at Central Department store and further along the road at Terminal 21. So much to see and many good deals to be discovered. The day was filled with fun for the family. Along the way we met so many smiling faces.

review_title: Always a great p[lace to wander around
review_text: Sukhumvit Road is a very long Road but what I view as the Sukhumvit area is Soi 1 by the JW Marriott hotel up to Soi 33 on this stretch there are many Bars, Restaurants and stalls along side the road for shopping of all sorts of gifts. Do enter the Sois that go off Sukhumvit as there are many great hidden gems of restaurants and eating places down these little Sois
We stayed in Sukhumvit, its got everything on your doorstep, shopping (if you like that sort of thing) great restaurants,street food, good massage parlours and all within walking distance.

review_title: Nice place to go for shopping
review_text: Easy to get here by Skytrain or BTS. Lot of shopping and restaurant. Terminal 21 is a very modern shoppingcenter. You may get everything here.

The sukhumvit region is really big.Lots of skies Scraper are here.Only big building, corporate office,Big hotel, hospital and Mall.The night time whole alley is becoming silent and so less people in road.I do not like the night time in sukhumvit..

What an amazing experience this area is, the night life, the restaurants that appear in the middle of the street at night. 
This is one of the must see places in Bangkok, walking the streets feels incredible, you are safe, and with so much to see and taste it is incredible.
Thank you Bangkok!
Excellent shopping malls, restaurants, bars, and anything else the heart desires. This place has them all and more.

If you are looking for high-end shops with Western brands and Western Hotel, this neighborhood seemed to be the place. It had an interesting sprinkling of local Thai touches, but definitely not old Bangkok.

review_title: Ther are better places to visit
review_text: too crowded and lots of buildings. Not nice to walk through, but there are lots of restaurants and malls around.

This is by far the best street of Bangkok. most of the good hotels are on this very long street the sky train the BTS is traveling on this street. Many shop malls and restaurants are located along this street.in the evening along some of it there are vendors that will sell you the next bargain
Busy and bustling neighbourhood, lots of shops, people and traffic, variety of restaurants and massage

review_title: Seems to me to be heart of the city.
review_text: I love this road and there is so many soi's that have so much to do. Lots of options to travel this road. Many shops to have many things to do, and many places to eat great food of all kinds.

One of the best areas in Bangkok considering restaurants, shops, malls and other important eminities. Most of the hotels in this area are loaded with foreigners, so its much easier to converse with the shop owners as most of them understand English.

Sukhumvit is the higher class street. Here you will find most of the Embassy`s as well as shopping, dining, bars and more expensive hotels. You will of course also find a lot of budget hotels to. The BTS goes all the way through Sukumvit and stops at all the great places along the street. Here you will find shopping malls like MBK, SIAM center, Terminal 21 etc.

A review on Sukhumvit?  What?  The subway station?  Ok.  Hey, so all the stations have security checks: metal detectors and checks of bags.  All stations are designed unremarkably.  Nothing to write home about like the subway station in PyongYang or Moscow.

review_title: This area is better than Silom or Sathorn
review_text: Sukhumvit is getting better and better each time I visited the Big Mango. From Soi 1 with the Arab/Middle Eastern enclave to Soi 5 the Indian enclave to Soi 49-555 the Japanese enclave, this area is so lively and varied. The BTS makes exploring this place even more accessible. And with Emporium and Em Quartier here, you can get almost everything you need and shop to your heart's content.
You can see all BKK in this part.. but wish if it is cleaner and more organised. .  But still intresting .. I dont recommend this area for observatory families specially with children ..

Sukhumvit is very near to the Siam if you take the Sky train its one station away.
Sky train is the best option to reach the center and avoid the traffic.
easy going area of Bangkok, good for transport and has parks if you  want to escape the hustle and bustle
Sukhumvit is home to the red light district in Bangkok and is a fun place to visit.  Great markets, food, and late night fun!
this is one place where your tour operator will make sure that you are left for half or may full day . walk around and shop if you want
A fun area, lots of nightlife and bars. Easy walking distance to Skytrain. Especially liked the bars with Happy-hours food and cocktails!
It is huge and encompasses the night districts to the business districts some very good some nauseating and some average so it's all their you have to select what you are there for Nd enjoy

review_title: “One of the best area to stay in Bangkok”
review_text: We stayed at Sukhumvit 13 in the Radisson SuitesBangkok Sukhumvit Hotel. Definitely a great and interesting area. Many restaurants, bars, massages, and hotels available in this area. Street food and night markets spring up in the evenings. Very convenient area for transport as the BTS is very close (nana metro station) and you can access the main areas of bangkok very easily. You can reach this area via the Airport link. Many Tuk Tuks and taxis also available.I would definitely stay in this area again in the future.

The longest road in Thailand named in honour of the person who built it has quite a number of  malls which cater to of-the-minute shopping, eating and nightlife. In addition to the malls there are many fashionable restaurants, bars, air-conditioned malls, luxury hotels and clubs which give the area a distinctly modern, cosmopolitan flavour. Also near the Phrom Phong BTS station a new tourist attraction for both Kids and adults alike has been opened known as Dinosaur Planet theme park.

Some of the city's most sinful spots, red-light districts Nana Plaza (Soi 4) and Soi Cowboy (off Soi 23) are also found here. And little, stress-busting pockets of calm can be found in the sweet cafes, atmospheric restaurants and lush day spas that hide down many of its sois. Making exploring its staggering length incredibly easy is the Skytrain (BTS) that runs through most of its downtown stretch.
very busy road, if you have kids make sure you have them on your shoulders of holding your hands its very very busy

review_title: One of the most happening place in Bangkok
review_text: This one of the most happening areas in Bangkok. everything is accessible from here Nana Plaza, Asok, Soi Cowboy, MBK etc. Nice place to explore the night life of Bangkok.

The business center very crowded and lots of people from Arab and India (Soi 5)including the seller too. Can find nice Indian and Arabic food here. A lot of Bar and Massage but difficult to find great Thai food. Some of building still on renovate. Comfortable with transportation ( BTS, Bus or Motobike )

One of the main attractions for European or western tourists. Famous for clubs and night life.You can find street market as well as big malls and shops in this area. Variety of restaurants are available in this area.
Highly populated and densed business houses are the most common feature of this place.Lots of hotel from cheap to luxury make your acomodation confirm as per your budget.

The concrete of the sky train is always above you and shopping is the order of the day. Many businesses and offices in this area so to many this is Bangkok but it isn't. To be fair there are some nicer places along some of the sois (alleyways) but I prefer other areas of Bagkok that seem less of a concrete jungle.
It is also an extremely long road.

It's simply one of the main roads through Bangkok; lots of hotels, street food, the BTS, markets, shops, massage, you name it. If you are coming to Bangkok its definitely a good place to stay along whilst here due to the BTS so you can beat traffic.

review_title: Bangkok main street for shopping and night live
review_text: This is a very long street, but the crowded area is around ASOK BTS station with some shopping malls, bars and Soy Cowboy.

This part of Thailand suddenly doesn't feel Thailand, more like Singapore or any other city. Buzzing with Malls and restaurants. Terminal 21 and Emporium is a good visit to pick some best brands and trendz. At night stop by at the ART BOX the flea market near holiday Inn.
Various activities can be done in this area. There is literally something for all tastes. Various local shops during day time and a steaming nightlife when the sun goes down.

Definitely a great and interesting area. Many restaurants, bars, massages, and hotels available in this area. Street food and night markets spring up in the evenings. Very convenient area for transport as the BTS is very close and you can access the main areas of bangkok very easily. You can reach this area via the Airport link. Many Tuk Tuks and taxis also available

Sukhumvit is perhaps the main shopping and business district of Bangkok, offering many modern international shopping opportunities, great places to eat, hotels and (toward one end of the area) access to Soi Cowboy and the Nana "entertainment" complex (sex tourism/party areas) both of which are easily avoided if you'd prefer. My wife and I went to a gogo bar and a ladyboy bar briefly one night to see what all the fuss was about but we'd both prefer other places to spend our money like with one of the many street vendors offering up coconut ice cream, etc.

There is something truly unremarkable about Central Bangkok, as if you're waiting to see something marvellous round the net corner, but all you see are dingy, dirty doorways and endless streams of headlights, as everyone tires to get from A to B (or wherever) at the same time. It's a daunting, polluted, ugly sprawl of concrete shopping centres and sleaze. Best kept clear of, unless you particularly like shopping in huge department stores that reak of perfume

There are many neighborhoods in Bangkok worthy of a visit or two. Sukhumvit comes to mind as the great provider. It has everything you come to Bangkok for and is accessible to everywhere else. 

Excellent Hotels, Fantastic Restaurants and a knockout Night Scene is what Sukhumvit is all about. 

I've been trotting up and down Sukhumvit for the better part of a decade. I actually don't remember the 60 bars cleared off for the little park. I bet it was great back then!

Sukhumvit has changed a lot since my first wide eyed stroll almost a decade ago. It's comparable to going to Times Square in the late Seventies and going back now. Every rule is changed. 

Last Christmas I witnessed one of the infamous Police Expat shakedowns, replete with urinalysis right on the street. I didn't even know what I was looking at until I read about it in the Bangkok Post the next day. 

Soi Nana is too much for me now. I will never get used to full on "gunslot gown" Muslim women roaming an area loaded with drunk expats and Go-Go Bars! They're really moving in solid on the other side of the street from Nana. Lots of restaurants and hotels catering to the Halal crowd. In a place like Nana it's an accident waiting to happen! This neighborhood goes against everything they believe, and it goes against it HARD! Don't be surprised if this turns into a "thing".

No, I stay down by Asoke now. The scene at Nana has been stripped out for a few years now, only hardcore guys, Arabs and first timers are over there. I still enjoy Soi Cowboy. I recommend that part for the world renown night life, it's a little more like what I'd expect. There's a lot more to Bangkok but this is Sukhumvit.
In the heart of the city this area has everything. From shopping up to a moving nightlife. Hotels are a bit more expensive but much more comfortable.

review_title: Many hotels, bars, restaurants and shops
review_text: Verry convenient location if you like nightlife and nice hotels. Also close to the BTS and MRT (Asoke).

Great location.  Very easy to get around with bts stations, tuk tuks and taxi.  There is also the underground but we didnt use it.  Good shopping and food. Lots of massage places and some seedy areas but easily avoided.

Probably the best area to stay in Bangkok. I along-with my family stayed here for 4 days in The Continent Hotel by Compass Hospitality. Major highlights is Asok BTS covers almost the whole city with different intervals. 

You can also find halal food as Usmania Thai Restaurant and Kashmir Restaurant are two good venues to dine in and take away.
Nightlife is also an important feature of this area. SOI (streets) are integral part filled with SPA and dance bars.

Most of the prominent shopping malls are here as well including Times Square Building, Emporium, Terminal21 and Nana Plaza. Access to whole sale shopping malls like Pratunam Indra, MBK and Chatuchak is also very convenient from here.

All in all a very good place to stay in.

A good area to stay in, many hotels with mid range prices, lots of shops, near by many shopping mall, best seafood restaurant in soi 24, many cargo and travel agencies, best exchange rate shops in soi 3, many Arab restaurants and coffee shops.
You can found anything that you want here. Eat, mall, good hotel, street food. They have very good mall called terminal 21 here. Very recommended. It good to visit all the time.
You are basically in the middle of everything and with the BTS can go anywhere fast and easy. No  matter where you are along this famous road you are accessible to everything you can every imagine.

center of city.... all big hotel chains..... streets ...... bts rail.... available.....
gardens ... shopping streets....
Malls, restaraunts, funky bars, hospitals, hotels, galleries shops that sell just about anything Sukhumvit has it all. If you are looking for something naughty then head to  Nana or Coi Cowboy.

This is a must see for visitors to Bangkok. Though it is best seen in the evening when office workers are done and the street comes to life in a real way. It's dirty... seedy... and generally sleazy. This is the quitessential Bangkok. Love it.
The most happening area on BKK. Must stay and taste food and hang out here & there on Sukumvit. The zone never sleeps and have all the options to chill out.

I've stayed here 6 times now, good jump point to other places, BTS train, Main airport just a 45 minute taxi ride, great night life and lots of restaurants, pubs, clubs plus famous Soi Cowboy and Nana Plaza, the night markets good fun too.

Sukhumvit Road is the main road thru center Bangkok which also follows the BTS elevated train route.  You can literatly find anything...Shopping malls, Entertainment, Tons of restaurants and bars, the adult playgrounds of Nana Plaza and Soi Cowboy.  Great area for your hotel as a base to explore this vast city of eight million.
In this district you find many mall types bazaars that cater for the tech savvy bargain hunter. You will find anything from computers, cellphones, repair specialists and many more.

review_title: "The good and safe place to go!!!!!!!!"
review_text: This area located in the middle of Bangkok City. Many facilities can get here especially public transport and restaurant. Shopping complex and hotel are awesome and feel like rich people when walking along Sukhumvit Road.

When I visit Bangkok I usually stay in Sukhumvit area. This is the pulse of Bangkok with food stalls, tuk tuk, metro Nana and Asok and many other interesting shops. High ranking shopping malls like Terminal 21 and many hotels are located on Sukhumvit. Embarrassing are only the many bars with in certain places.

review_title: first time in the 3rd time in BKK
review_text: Went to Khaosan all the time and this was my first stay in Sukhumvit. Very big and you can get lost where to go but once you know the address, I thinks Sukumvit is better for people looking for clean and quiet stay. Also, it's fun to explore and find out nice cafes and street food. Stayed two nights and another night after coming from Laos. Recommend the street seafood tables near Winsor suite hotel and convention in Soi 20. The best dishes in BKK we had.
Good high end luxury malls and good transport available in the area. Some nice entertainment options like dinosaur park also are available here.
very nice place to visit , many shopping malls, food areas, entertainment and many more you can enjoy this place
If you're in Bangkok you have to visit this area. Great crowds, malls and shisha bars. People here are rolling and really friendly.

review_title: Definitelt stay here when in BKK
review_text: One of the most central, perhaps touristy, but very convenient areas in BKK where to stay. You literally have the whole city at your feet: BTS and Sky train public transport, taxis, millions of shops, bars, restaurants.
we had our stay in this area in Marvel hotel. this area is a very busy street. its always full of traffic.

this area is great to be able to get around and see the BKK nightlife. The MTS is crowded, but it makes it easy to get around. Lots of traffic and tourists, but this is where all the action happens in BKK

review_title: I usually stay in Sukhumvit area when I am in Bangkok
review_text: There are two metro stations in Sukhumvit area: Nana and Asok.  Sukhumvit area is convenient for tourists, you can hop onto a tuk tuk or take the metro.  There are many hotels in Sukhumvit.

For the last few years I have loved staying in the Sukhumvit area. There is everything to do here. Shopping, Sight Seeing, Shopping, Entertainment, Drinking, Eating and much more. You name it and Sukhumvit mostly has it.

review_title: Nice Malls and a major shopping place in Bangkok
review_text: Siam square and Siam paragon are two shopping malls that are there in Sukumvit which have Siam world ocean and Madame Tussad too. A good shopping experience and kid attractions. You would require an entire day to cover this. A must see for all.

Sukhumvit is one of the heart in Bangkok. It has every levels department stores , restaurants, bars , disco and pubs. You can limit your budget for having trip around there. If you love hanging out or shopping , it 's the place that you should;t miss.

review_title: Everything you need is in Sukhumvit
review_text:
Sukhumvit area or should I say the areas surrounding the Sukhumvit Road, one of the 4 major roads in Bangkok, can span so vast that you can have practically everything under one "roof", from street food, cafes, restaurants, shopping complexes, night entertainments, spas & massages, museums, temples, parks, various accommodations to anything you can think of under the sky. It's endless.

Currently, after staying in many districts in Bangkok, I would personally think that the areas surrounding Soi Sukhumvit 12, 14, 16, 18, 19, 23 would be the best places to stay as you can utilize the conveniences of the Bangkok Mass Transit System (BTS Asok) and the Metropolitan Rapid Transit (MRT Sukhumvit) for all your traveling needs. Moreover, Terminal 21 shopping Complex is just next to Soi Sukhumvit 19 and Soi Cowboy is just off Soi Sukhumvit 23. 

It would be best come year 2029 when Bangkok would be fully linked up with more interchanges available for all your visits. By that time, Don Mueang Airport and Suvarnabhumi Airport would be linked up too~ :)

You would love this place if you are a shopaholic!
The BTS is just around the corner, all types of food options are easily available. The night market is wonderful. The biggest malls of Bangkok are easily accessible.

It's a lot of life/pulse in Sukhumvit. There are places that are not seedy just nice and still lovely for couples and families and it has still have the beat, good food , good music, nice people. Actually it changed for the better since last time we were there.

If you want to experience the shopping area of Bangkok, than Sukhumvit is the place you want to visit. Best is go with the BTS skytrain station Siam and there you are right at MBK One of the biggest and world famous shopping malls in BKK.Siam Center and Siam Paragon some big shopping malls are also close by. Take a Taxi to Panthip Plaza, very good for all kind of electronic devices.For the nightlife the famous Nana Plaza and Soi Cowboy is also located in Sukhumvit. Not everything or everybody there is what it seems to be ha ha. Just remember, if it is to good to be true it very likely is not true.,

review_title: Love sukhumvit, busy and interesting and so easy to get around using the BTS
review_text: This is typical Bangkok, so many shopping centres, stalls, restaurants and bars, the BTS makes it so easy to get around Sukhumvit
Its my personal place to stay on Soi 11 .as its close to all the party area ,high end mall ,street shopping . Theres everything for everybody  .And the Skytrain is best to beat the traffic

review_title: This is the touristy exciting area.
review_text: This area is known for it's red light districts, but what not so many people know is that there are a couple of really good pubs there that have live bands playing with cheep beer.

review_title: High end shopping street worth visiting
review_text: Shopping along this street is a must for all visitors. Best to use the skytrain instead of taxi as traffic jams are common along the street especially during morning and evening rush hours. With the hot spell currently experienced in Bangkok, it's not a bad idea to cool down one self in the many shopping malls along the street.

Sukhimvit is full of streets - Sois. We stayed on Soi 15 which is close to Soi 11 - one of the good places to party at nights. Lots of bars. But very very crowded and full of traffic.
Sukhumvit has some good malls and restaurants, and the streets are full of vendors selling everything from clothes, to bags, to belts, electronics and food.
The BTS and underground both operate along the length of it so very convenient.
I did not enjoy Bangkok as I expected because everywhere in the streets they offer you sexual services and you can not even walk freely...

Sukhimvit is the longest street in Bangkok and considered to be a main thoroughfare. Traffic is heavy at all times of the day so it is wise to take the BTS Skytrain if you wish to go anywhere along this street. There are many hotels and lots of shops that line this street. There are street vendors selling Thai food and souvenirs of various kinds. There are numerous sois (side streets) that branch off Sukhimvit. Some parts of the street are safe and charming and others are areas where one should avoid. One should try and walk down parts of the street just to absorb the flavor and culture of Bangkok.
Great to go out at night especially if you are staying in the business part.  Some good bars and restaurants.  Market stalls and an array of shops catering for most things.

review_title: Crowded Streets, Nice Bars and Great Street Food
review_text: Its kind off a mixed bag. There are malls with designer brands and swanky restaurants as well as Go Go bars and street markets. My favourite part was the street food. You could sit on a table on the street and enjoy the best of thai food, satays, grilled meats etc with a cold beer.
Sukhumvit is a good location for the centre of town, Sky Train, BTS, shopping, bars, nightlife. Plenty of good value hotels

This area is lively and acts as a hub for everywhere else in Bangkok.It does mean that the place is always very busy, but at least it is served by both the underground MRT and the Skytrain. Shopping malls abound and Terminal 21 is at the centre of it.
If you have a BTS metro pass you will make the most of it...good nightlife...many good places are near by.

As a first timer to an unknown territory you would love to be a place from where you could easily reach all the major locations at any time. especially as Bangkok is known for its night life your would definitely prefer to stay around the most happening areas at night from where on u could easily have a walk or get a automobile to your residence,from both these point of view i preferred Sukhumvit more specifically The Westin Grande Sukhumvit  hotel which is situated at the center of sukhumvit opposite to both BTS and MTS.
If you are a shopaholic then u would easily get malls around it with all the necessary day to day amenities with in your reach.
these are all good points about Sukhumvit.the only bad side of it i found is if u r on a budget trip or looking for a low cost hotels to stay on then its a bit difficult to find because everything has its price and as you r getting every other things at your reach so easily so you have to compromise you wallet to avail this
If its about the security,i would say i never found anything to be afraid of more importantly the whole area is like awake during the nights so its not a concern in my opinion

The ideal location to stay, you are close to most things. There are numerous Hotels and good restaurants in every Soi as well as the main road. The best shopping is here, MBK, Siam Paragon, Central and Terminal 21. Of a night there are numerous street stalls selling great food, Tee shirts, fake everything, an interesting walk through the area. If it is your thing there a couple of red light areas in the area. If you want to get anywhere there is the Skytrain or hundreds of taxis. You can get to almost anywhere for 100Baht

review_title: The sights, sounds and contrasts of Sukhumvit
review_text: Sukhumvit road covers a very large area and encompasses every thing from 4 star hotels to shanties, high rise shopping malls to back street sleazy bars and open air food & produce markets selling everything... yes literally everything. You can get everything from a full dental replacement to a boob job... if you want one.. It is exciting , we felt reasonably safe and experienced no problem getting around... especially on the overhead rail which is fast and convenient.

review_title: Best Place to Stay In Bangkok
review_text: One of the biggest roads in Bangkok it's filled with Hotels every where.You will get the best street food and great eating places here.There are so many Soi's[streets] in sukhumvit and each soi has it's own identity.We stayed for 2 days here and explored some streets and it's got a highly cosmopolitan Mix.After 9 Pm you will see girls hanging outside most of the Hotels.Terminal 21 is the best mall in this Road.Best Place to stay in Bangkok because of the BTS service which connects most of Bangkok with this Area.
My hotel was a two minute walk from the Nana Station. I found riding the BTS to be enjoyable and inexpensive.

This is a looooong road with loads of shopping malls spread along its length.  Starts with everyone cheap and cheerful choice MKB near the Siam exchange station on the BTS.  This Mall has a little of everything at knock down prices, but if you are looking for a more upmarket experience there are loads of other Malls to chose from.  All, or nearly all seem to have food courts and cafes of various types, even Costa is in one for a great cup of Italian coffee with a UK twist.

Some of the Malls also have Cinemas, if you need a Hollywood fix while you are here, but don't forget to stand for the national anthem at the beginning of the show.   The BTS offers a good way to get between the Malls if you can't stand the heat of the day outside walking.  Traffic seems to be slow along the road nearly all day as it is so busy. 

There are two areas for "Gentlemen" if you need a beer with company or beer &  entertainment, which are around Nana (Nana Plaza) and Asoke (Soi Cowboy) stations.
 
So if you don't want to eat, shop, go to the pictures or get some other "evening entertainment" you can give this place a miss .

It is impossible to describe it because there are many places not recommended and may recommended. I f you interesting the way of living of the poor take taxi an make a good plan, it is interesting , a place to take authentic pictures, ask the locals if they agree to be photographed   .
.

review_title: Upscale Area with Great Hotels, Restaurants & Shopping
review_text: We booked the JW Marriott Sukhumvit for our vacation in Thailand without knowing anything about the Sukhumvit area. We hit the jackpot!  The hotel was incredible. The restaurants in the area were fabulous and there was upscale shopping all around. We intend to do another trip to Bangkok in the future and we will definitely stay in this area again.

review_title: Like NYC in the 1980s - weirdos all over
review_text: Like Time Square in 1985 - filled with weirdos, dirty old white guys, folks walking to work, bad smells, good smells, dangerous intersections, great food. Worth a walk down.

One of the main areas of bangkok for shopping and alot of hotels and guest houses,it can be very busy when walking on the street ,so try and use the overhead walk way when you can.
You will find many shopping malls to suit all tastes,but it can be very busy with cars and buses,so if you go there take the Skytrain or walk,many people seeling everything on the streets and street food is good in the area.most people go here so prices can be alittle high
Not a place to hang around at night.  Eclectic mix of night markets, good street food but overt prostitution

review_title: Place with availability of everything possible in this world
review_text: Sukhumvit, is the name that is there in my heart permanently. I cannot forget this name inmy life. This is a perfect area of Bangkok which has everything to offer to all kind of tourists. If you are a single traveller or you are travelling with Family of friends this locality is perfect for you. People are amazing in this area and you can shop as per your budget and lovely hotels at cheap prices are available at Sukhumvit. Foodie lovers must visit Sukhumvit- Soi Arab to taste the Arabian food.

Ideal for travelers on vacation as this location has a lot of hotels, good restaurants having Indian & Bangladeshi food, lots of shopping malls including the underwater aquarium (huge attraction for kids) and wax museum. Also you find Mac Donalds nearby.

High end retail tourism.  Among the glitzy glamour there are many nooks and crannies oozing grass roots but if you prefer the facade there is and endless stream and well presented. Excellent life style on all levels

review_title: Busy part of the city with lots of shopping
review_text: Sukhumvit is full of local vendors at night and malls during the day. The streets are busy and oftentimes intimidating to cross! However there is plenty to do, especially via the tuk-tuk or skytrain.
A wonderful place for nightlife and foods. It is easily accessible by Skytrain. Avoid going alone and drunk after 1am as u may be robbed.

A must see neighborhood, iconic, original. A low budget destination indeed, but with the hidden diamonds. We actually stayed in a low budget hotel, during the night in some alleys you could see the rats chacing the cockroaches and the other way around, but.. that is part of the feeling you have to experience. This is what you expect in low budget areas, low costhotels, street food of unkonown quality, teh atmoshphere of old bankgog. We did not feel any danger when there, but of course one should be cautious of low lit alleys, as in every part of the world.  Clear your mind and dive in one of the most iconic neghborhoods in Bangkok
I love that it is just beside Asoke BTS for easy transfer. and in a great accessible location. One of my most frequent station that I visit
Cheap shopping , good bargains, search the entire market before you actually buy anything to get the best rates.

I stayed in an apartment in Soi 11 in Sukhmvit and it is the perfect location. 

- It is by the Sky train so easy to commute in Bangkok (my station was Nana)

- Loads of shops, bars, restaurants, massage parlors, very lively during the day and at night scene 

- lots of street foods during the day and night

- about 1 hour from the airport (depending on traffic, 30 minutes to 60 minutes), By taxi the cost is.... up to £8 (everything is so cheap!)

- quite far from the old city where the palace and some attractions are but do-able. A tuk tuk or taxi or boat ride (it was so hot when I visited in April that walking around, which is what i prefer and like to do, was just out the question, the heat was unbearable). A taxi to Wat Arun (which is on the other side of the river) was only £7.

- very close to all the nice (ie designers) shopping centers (WorldCentre, Zen, etc

- To get to MBK shopping centre (the reasonably priced one) it is 2 stations by sky train (20 baht per journey, which is around 50 pence)

review_title: best place to live or stay in a hotel
review_text:
i have been living in sukhumvit for a few months now and its just fantastic! so many good restaurants and bars nearby! also the bts and metro are meeting at asoke, which is quite convenient! 
for tourists some of the hotel chains have sukhumvit and river locations. sofitel for example or redisson blue!

review_title: Good visit in the Sukhumvit area
review_text: Many nice hotels, shopping centres and upscale residential buildings in this area.  I shopped at Big C and Tesco for the whole afternoon!

review_title: Whatever you want, whenever you need it!
review_text:
Fantastic area, street food,bars,tailors,massage,tattoo,nightlife,
girls,ladyboys,shows you name it and it's available in suhkumvit.
Not everyone will like it, but we love it.
for budget hotels to five star hotels, shopping from street vendors to luxury shopping malls, restaurants, nite shopping and entertainment, its all here.  Something for everyone

We have used Nok Air many times for domestic air travel in Thailand.  When booking tickets on-line accept the charges in baht on your credit card because the company uses an unacceptable rate of 28 to 1 for US dollars which is as much as 25% less then the going exchange rate.  Lately Nok has had some purported labor issues resulting in cancelled flights which is unsettling.  I suspect that part of the issue is really company consolidation of partial flight loads into full plane flights.  We had our 12:30 PM flight cancelled as well as an earlier 11:30 AM flight which all left on a scheduled 1:30 PM flight without any apparent problem involving who was suppose to sit in what seat - all very curious.  Don Muang Airport has upgrade its domestic terminal which is very nice; but, overpriced for food and other services which are now under review.  Taxi service to and from the airport is about the same and remains problematic.  Set taxi rate in Chang Mai from airport to hotel at 160 baht is refreshing.

Has everything you need for a night out, whether by yourself, a couple or group of friends. Huge variety of restaurants, bars, clubs, street food and little street vendors. 
It does have a slightly seedy feel to the area, but that can be avoided and could still take a family out.

An easy spot to head towards the airport link north of sukhumvit or the NANA line nearby. There's so much delicious foods and nice hotels nearby. I also went to the massage parlor after researching on Trip advisor for a good back/shoulder and foot massage. My friend and I walked from Terminal 21 to Nana to see the street market along Sukhumit. There are many areas to shop for fun things for cheap!

Sukhomovit is very attractive and peaceful Where  any body can walk and talk
specialy skhomovit is  one  of  the best and beautiful place to watch 
bangkok is city of  peace and love  
we can meet  peapole worldwide 
Bangkokis diffrent city where we 
Never feel alone all citizens are friendly 
I will prefer to bangkok
Great place to stay so central and loads of fun easy to reach both sky trains as well as the underground trains a must stay location

Sukhumvit is one of a kind... from high class hotels and eateries through to English and Dutch pub food, heaps of Japanese eateries and all the major international fast food chains. Literally any number of drinking establishments,through to street vendors of all persuasions down to massage and dens of iniquity.. it even has something for the kids!!!! (Just noticed a new Dinosaur World which wasn't there six months back)... there seriously aren't to many places on earth quite like it.  Start at Soi 1 and simply wander down to soi 33 and beyond ... don't be scared to wander the alley ways and back streets ... all safe and lots of hidden surprises. You could spend a week here and still not experience it all ... There is more on behind the odd numbered Sois than on the other side of Sukhumvit Rd.

review_title: My usual haunt when in Bangkok
review_text:
When In Bangkok I stay in Soi 6 or Soi 7/1 Sukhumvit.
I like the area,lots of street food stalls,other stalls selling goods,shopping centres,foot massage parlours,resturants and so much more.
Always a buzzing scene going on with ltos of sights,smells and sounds.
While there is a lots of "adult" venues in this area it is also welcome to familes and those not out for "adult action"

best location to be when visiting Bangkok for the first, try to get a hotel near Terminal 21 as they have a food court to suit every taste at very reasonable cost. Asoke skytrain and Sukhumvit subway station will takes you eveywhere.
I stay in Soi 13 Sukhumvit for 2 to 3 weeks each year..I love the atmosphere,shopping,restaurants,bars and night life..It's a very happy atmosphere,and close to everything you could possibly want..
Sukhumvit is a good area for shopping as well as restaurants and local eateries. It will be wise to book a hotel in this area, however the local transport system is very effective.

There was a lot to see and some really good places to eat. I found there to be lots of creepy people even in the day. Stick to day time, at night (like 6pm) someone grabbed my bum and I walked holding my young son's hand. Disturbing. Stick to day hours only
Such a long street with markets, food stalls and people everywhere. Watch out for tuk tuks and motorbikes but ohh myyyyyyy goodness so amusing watching all the different people and sights.
Nice area in Bangkok but office time crowd is heavy.All types of transport facilities available throughout day and night.
Recommended place to stay as this is one the center locations of Bangkok with all facilities for transport, food and shopping

review_title: Comes alive at night. Not much to see before 9pm
review_text: Besides mall, a bit crappy in the daytime. Comes alive after dark though. Good variety of pubs, bands and great atmosphere.
great area of Bangkok, great place to buy clothes also right in the middle of the night life two BTS stations also are helpful.
Would recommend that you get a hotel near this place. Connected to other train stations and near a lot of attractions.
Ex Pat area and you can find some needed American food.  Good high end shopping and hotels to visit and plenty of night life!
I don't have much to say about this place. It was ok and all, but I mainly exchanged my cash here, lol.

review_title: A place for good food, good hotels and bars
review_text: It's a good area to stay when you're in Bangkok.You find good restaurants,bars,hotels and also a good options when it comes to nightlife.
sukhumvit its a palace in bangkok where you can find good quality sea and thai food. there are lots for good hotels for stay and locallity is also too good.

review_title: Busy, vibrant all in one place.
review_text: Heart of  Bankok. All your needs can be found here within walking distance. From affordable night markets or cheapest street food to fancier shops l and restaurants. It will meet your expectations. Very very high traffic area ...

we stayed the first 2 nights in country in this area and while it felt safe, we discovered as our trip went along it was quite pricey in comparison to other areas we stayed.  But the food and restaurant quality was good.

i lived in hte area of sukhumvit 23, at fantastic 1Sabai hostel. This area is nice, giving a big range of food and accommodation from budget to very expensive. lot of night life and good connection, trough MRT and BTS to the rest of the city. I like to be in Sukhumvit.

review_title: A road with many hotels shops and dept stores
review_text: A visit to Robinsons dept store is worthwhile .  There is good  food hall in basement. Next door is  Terminal 21 shopping mall. A visit to Erwan hotel is a must for afternoon tea.
lots of hotels, shoppingmals, restaurants, massagepearls, central located easy to get around with MRT,skytrain

review_title: GREAT PLACES TO STAY - CLOSE TO THE CITY
review_text: Fantastic area to stay in Bangkok - close to the city and a short ride on the Skytrain to most attractions.

The Emporium quarter is stunning if shopping is your thing.
Best place to get mobile SIMs. 
Good design and great for rainy day.
