The best location of the city,close to the CBD and the nightlife area.Almost all the attractions are walkable or can be reached by Sky rail.

It is true that one can relax in of the most busy road, avenue. Street life is so vivid that one can pass day and night there without getting bored. Busy traffic, lively street food markets, street shops, super malls, everything is there to entertain the mind.

review_title: Nice area real close to street food and dancing
review_text: The sky train makes this area great. Plus we walked everywhere it was way easy....lots to do and see!

review_title: What was once great now mostly gone
review_text: Ten years ago I would have given this street a 5. Now they have torn down most of the fun, unique little shops and bars, and have replaced them with enormous, cookie-cutter shopping malls. Officials were bribed, and the quaint shopping, eating, and drinking districts mowed down, their charm forever lost, buried beneath an ever growing row of boring glass and steel monoliths, shining shrines to corporate greed. Moreover, the local cops might just make you stop to take a urine test for drug use, your reward for spending your tourist dollars here. There are still nooks and crannies of the old, fun Sukhumvit left if you look hard, but their days are numbered, so you better go catch a glimpse now before they are gone forever. Another tragic loss of the things that used to make Bangkok special.
A very central and happening place..everything from food to pubs to shopping is out here...A good place to be in irrespective of your travel type(family, friends, couple etc.)

If you want to sample the street markets then come late evening, maybe after dusk when the stalls open selling clothes, gifts, shoes etc. It's crowded so keep your wallet safe and it can get a little warm but it's a great experience. Late at night there are also lots of small bars and food stalls. Don't forget to haggle when you're shopping!

Sukhumvit is a tourist and Expat hotspot, Many people living in Thailand, so called Expats and business people living around Sukhumvit or meeting here. It is one of the older streets with a lot of tourist souvenir shops, restaurants and bars. 
:
Unfortunately the Bars are more and more history and gone. 
What made Bangkok once so special had to move for skyscrapers and shopping centers. 
:
Lots of good and not too expensice Hotels around Sukhumvit. One of the biggest and best known Hospitals in Thailand the Bumrungrad Hospital is in Soi 1  ---- my favorate foodstore in Soi 5 FOODLAND ... and check out the food on the counter there...its just phantastic and there is always an expat for a chat

review_title: Sukhumvit is where the best nightlife in Bangkok can be found.
review_text:
There is no other destination quite like Bangkok. While the heat, traffic and street noise can be an assault on the senses, there is plenty to smile about. The warm and friendly faces, food on every street corner and an energy that soon gets under your skin. The sprawling city of angels is packed with things to do from visiting historic attractions to shopping and nightlife. Sukumvit district is where the best nightlife in Bangkok can be found. The huge road has so many side streets or ‘sois’ to discover. The trendiest nightclubs are around Soi 11 such as Bed Supperclub and Levels Club & Lounge. Thonglor and Ekkamia are where the cool urban crowd hang out and for those that prefer dancing girls can head to the seedy neighborhoods in Soi Cowboy and Nana. 
Octave Rooftop and Lounge is more than a place for watching the sunset or having a romantic meal. No rooftop bar in the city has a 360 degree view of Bangkok that matches this, and the top floor of this three level venue is the highest spot in the city for a party. If you visit Bangkok and you intend to party, then Sukhumvit Road will be the centre of your world. This main thoroughfare hosts most of the finest clubs and bars that the city has to offer.

Sukhumvit offers everything from soup to nuts as they say...It has
night entertainment, some of the best hotels in Bangkok, has the BTS that stops along the road and there are numerous places to eat Thai and western fast-food.

I for the past 15 years always stay in the Sukhumvit area. Why? Well it is convenient for the BTS Sky Train, has pleant of hotels from economy to luxurious, is very close to the Expressway to the airport so less chance of getting in traffic gridlock like some other areas which can be disasterous if you are in a hurry to get to the airport.
Yes there are plenty of girlie bars and street hookers if you are into that.
Some reasonable shopping can also be had and also very convenient if you want to go to Robinsons Dept store near Asoke BTS or any other shopping area via BTS like Central Dept Store, Paragon, Siam, MBK or just about anywhere

A busy, bustling area with great shops and at night it comes alive with street sellers of all types combined with the nightlife to suit most people. There are all types of bars, restaurants and night clubs where you will meet locals and all nationalities. It is safe

i stayed in the Soi Sukhumvit 15 which is near the Terminal mall and Asok BTS.  The hotel I stayed is nicely designed and pretty cheap.  Has a nice pool and bar at the roofdeck (U Sukhumvit)

The vast area of Sukhumvit encompasses a lot of the 5 star hotels, big malls, apartments and Expat areas.  The place is always energetic from day to night.

Areas that can be accessed easily will be Sukhumvit 11 for the nightlife, lots of bars here plus the posh malls and other attractions of Bangkok.  

Soi Cowboy can be accessed as well but this is not my thing though.

The Soi I stayed is a nice quiet place to stay in (Soi Sukhumvit 15) if you want to stay in a peaceful and quiet part of Bangkok.

review_title: The area to be based out of while visiting
review_text: This is a central location that is close to everything. You have access to the public transport system, restaurants, clubs, bars, etc.

I stayed at Prathunam during my last trip and I didn't like it.
So, this time I booked a hotel in Sukhumvit. 
The place is centrally located in the city and has got many attractions. 
BTS and MRT is available. It is close to Nana where you can find nice nightlife scenes in Bangkok.
Also, you could find a lot of shopping malls and street shops around this place.

Sukhumvit is a suburb of Bangkok, and is well connected by the MTR (subway) and BTS (skytrain), and thus a popular area for tourists to stay. Always energetic, always busy day or night, it is a nice area to feel the real urban energy of Bangkok.

The suburb is vast, and encompasses lower middle class apartments as well as top end five star hotel accommodations. With restaurants for literally every world cuisine within walking distance, it is a foodies paradise, not to mention the street food which is awesome as well. 

Lots of shopping malls which basically have walkways attached to the Skytrain and Subway stations for ease of access, and the malls range from a bargain hunters paradise to exclusive palatial designer brand locations. 

Good central suburb to stay for long or short term.

review_title: This was where we stayed, a shopping district or road.
review_text:
Thanon Sukhumvit or Sukhumvit Road is long, like a never ending road.  If you are fit, and take your time to walk, you can skip several BTS stations.

I would call it a shopping walking tour.  At Phrom Phong BTS station, there is the super huge EM Emporium, then further up one BTS station away, there is Terminal 21.  At Siam BTS, you have Siam Paragon, Siam Square and all.  Then, it's Phleon Chit and Mo Chit BTS where you can get to The Amarin, Central World, Big C, and Pratunam Market.

Along the way, there are also many fascinating smaller mom and pop shops and various cheap restaurants and street food at side alleys (sois) to cater to the budget consicous.

Sukhumvit is a must visit in Bangkok. Can be reached by metro and there are a lot of malls to be visited there, even the famous Robinson s is there.
You can spend whole day there around.

We stay in Sukhumvit whenever we are in Bangkok.  There is always plenty to do in this area and we make good use of the Skytrain which connects with the other mass transit systems throughout Bangkok.
There are also a good variety of modern hotels to choose from, not to mention the ever popular Australia Bar.
This area is a popular one. The BTS station here takes you to many  other well-known places. Superb locality around.

It is one of the most touristic and most popular expatriate districts of Bangkok.  Bars, apartments,  good and luxury hotels and traffic hubs. The area is plenty of local shops, tailors, but plazas, too.  This is also one of the well-known red light district areas of Bangkok (Nana area).  In the evenings it is completely different than daytime, street vendors and food stuff everywhere; even a simple walk can be difficult due to the crowd. A lot of condo is there where you can easily rent flats, even for some days via airbnb.  Sukhumvit is very well served by Skytrain, MTS, or taxis, or tuctuc (the latter is to be avoided).  I advise to try the regular boats in Saen Saep Canal (from Nana chard pier or Asoke-Petchaburi pier) to travel to inner-city, (eg. Siam center, Golden mount).  The trip will be very much contrasted with the modern, skyscraper-like Bangkok. Extremely cheap for a great experience, don’t miss it.   Some aspect of the area, especially around Nana district can be "dramatic" and sad at night. There is an Indian (around Soi 11-12) and also Japanese neighborhood (in the area Soi 21), both with good restaurants, but try instead fantastic street food, especially hot pot.... And of course, huge traffic jams, so if you can, just avoid taxis. Some big shopping malls are also at your disposal (eg. Terminal 21.)   Benjakiti park is also in the area, with dedicated cycling and walking paths, a nice lake, colorful flowers, fixed exercise equipment and several monuments, playgrounds for children, fountains.   Cheap rent a bike service (50 bhat/hour). It is really close to Terminal 21. If you need a rest, it is the right place.
Not so much for the nigh life but for the shopping and the restaurants. Thanks to the BTS (sky train) everything is reachable in little time and in comfort.
Centrally located, this station can become mayhem in peak hour and shoulder periods. If you are in a group make sure you have a meeting point, as you will be separated without a doubt.

review_title: Nice area for shopping and restaurant
review_text: Sukhumvit is a very large area. But in general it is a popular area for shopping and eating. Many big shopping centers are in this area. It is just a matter to know which part of Sukhumvit to stay.

review_title: Always worth a stroll along this crazy street
review_text: A busy hassle at day and night, there is always something going on. Shopping, hussling, eating and drinking late at night, never a dull moment. Be careful of pick pockets and beggers and do not listen to husslers, be careful. Great fun in wild crowds.

review_title: Not as bad as the taxi driver may me think!
review_text: Few minutes after I gave the address of my hotel  to the taxi he couldn't find it. Then he stopped in the highway, may a phone call. I asked he knew where we were going he said that now he knew. Then after like 20 minutes we started to approach Sukhumvit area and the drivers asked me if I knew the area. He made me think it was bad and unsafe. But it is not as bad as the taxi driver may me think! You are close to the action!

This is a very long street where the modern and traditional meet. It is literally a shopping district where you can find everything from clothes to colorful dildos. Yes you read it write. No need to go to the eye doctor.
This is also the scene for much partying and club hopping. So if you are into clubbing and bar-hopping this is the place to be.

One downside thou is the near constant traffic jam in the area but as a remedy, you can always take the BTS which run along this street. You can also walk along this street to get a better experience and perspective. Either way, there is much to discover here for the curious and much to buy whether you are a luxury shopper or a hardcore haggle champion.
This is similar to Khao San. So, if you are doing that, you can skip this one. Otherwise, a must do.

review_title: in the heart of the nightlife
review_text: this is the area to stay in for shopping and nightlife.  it has access to the skytrain so you can easily get around

review_title: Too busy street, but with MRT/BTS easy to do
review_text: Sukhumvit is a ver long road with many things to do. Famous are Soi 8 (Nana) and Soi 21-23 (Cowboy), but also shopping at Terminal 21, and EmQuartier (BTS station Phrom Phong). But also Sukhumvit 55 (Thong Lo) is famous by many expats.

It's fun to walk down Sukhumvit along booth after booth of items for sale. If shopping with children, be aware that they may see more than you bargained for. Not uncommon to see a booth with rainbow colored dildos next to a booth with silk scarves.

Sukhumvit is crowded, noisy and jarring. It is also modern, fun and convenient. You should know what you're going here for. Go for the nightlife, restaurants, spas, shopping. Don't go if you're looking for a quiet time or are looking to get off the tourist trail.
Clean area with many hotels, nearby malls and shopping places. Street food is easily available and is very well connected with the Sky Train.

review_title: A great area in Bangkok for nightlife, restaurants and shopping.
review_text: This is my favorite area in the city, a one stop shop if you will of entertainment and luxury accommodations.  I highly recommend it for solo travelers, families, and friends out having a nice vacation.

I travel around the world to experience new things, see something different, and deal with the locals. This neighborhood is very westernized and does not meet my travel requirements.

Except for the BTS train, needs of travelers are not easily satisfied here such as presence of travel agencies, cheap shops and restaurants. I stayed only one night here and decided to move to a hotel near Khason Road where all my needs are met.

Traffic is horrible, worse than LA, USA, Manila Philippines or NY NY. Travelling one block by car could cost you 15 minutes so it would be faster if you just walk. Some shop have good deals though. It is better if you change your dollar in Bangkok as it gets cheaper the further you are from Bangkok like going to more remote islands like Phuket, 
Koh Yao Noi etc.

The upscale'ish modern'ish side of Bangkok

It's not fully westernized. In fact, it's not westernized much. But it's s Thai fusion of modern Thai. 

Lots of action, people, food, restaurants, activities

Please give this review a thumbs up

I lived in Bangkok for 5 years and still have not been to half of Sukhumvit Road.  There are so many great soi's leading off the road. It is super accessible as the BTS Sky Train runs right through the heart of it. You have great street traders toward the lower end of the road just up from Soi 4 on both sides and then the higher end shopping centers like Emporium which has it's own BTS stop, that has exclusive brands like Louis Vuitton.  It is a street that has a vast range of anything for everyone.  Bars, pubs, clubs, shopping, street markets, food stalls, convenience stores, plenty of ATM's and banks, Western Union's, great world wide restaurants catering to every kind of cuisine, and it seems to go on forever.
Cool place to stay if you're travelling and want to get away from Khao San Road! It's a very modern neighborhood, with access to skytrain. Lots of restaurants!
Very expensive area with many chain hotels. I don't think I would waste my money to travel to this area again

This famous street is the hub of the city with numerous sois running off to the left and right of it !  Shops and hotels of every possible rating abound, as do street and night markets, though one day per week is now reserved for cleaning and stall holders are not allowed to set up, usually on Mondays.
Stayed in Sukhumvit area, many malls, good restaurants and spas around. Well connected by Sky train and metro. Many street stalls at night.

Stayed in a hotel close to this place. Some of the shops are not for people on a budget but doesn't stop you looking. The food stalls all along the side of street, what a variety of smells, have a good walk along this street and see Bangkok locals.

review_title: Good area to stay, easy access to sky train airport.
review_text: Good area with sky train and airport shopping centre connections. Plenty of hotels and restaurants. Mdk shopping centre indoor market easy to get to and worth a visit.

We stayed in one of the hotels along Sukhumvit road. It is one of the busiest streets in Bangkok. There are a lot of shopping places, delish (clean) street foods, massage parlors, 3-5 stars hotels, bars, convenience stores and restaurants/fast food places. We are located near Asok station where Robinson's Dept store and Terminal 21 are situated. If you book tour packages, you dont have to meet the driver/van in a specific place because if you are booked around Sukhumvit area, they will pick you up and drop you off your hotel. 

I will make sure to book my hotel around this area when I go back to Bangkok.

This is one of the main cities in Bangkok and is extremely busy!, if you are not used to large crowds I would avoid this area. 
The night markets appear here which are interesting and fun.

review_title: Shopping by day, lots of fun by night!
review_text:
Longest road in Thailand, full of hotels, shopping malls, bars and restaurants. You can enjoy fine food in exquisite restaurants, but also enjoy local food on the street; you can shop for famous designers and also for local ones. 
But it's not the road that is interesting , it's the alleys(soy) that make it famous (Soy 11 is full of bars and restaurants, soy 4 lights up at night and is famous for the naughty nightlife and so on...).
You can move along the road by BTS Skytrain, very easy and avoiding the traffic jam. 

It'sa must!!!

review_title: You want it?? you can get it here
review_text:
Flowing with street vendors selling most things, busy with people standing alongside the many shops selling (ummmm) - it is certainly not for those who want to stroll gently with children as there is the statutory motorbike buzzing along the pavement to be avoided lest you 'get in their way'

Bangkok at its best or worse here dependant upon what you seek.

review_title: A hub for tourists; easy and accessible
review_text:
I lived in the Sukhumvit area for 18 months and loved all the conveniences close to hand. I can see why it's popular with tourists as the BTS line extends all the way through, and there is an interchange station at Asok where you can hop on the MRT (underground train). Basically if you use Sukhumvit as your base it's fairly easy to get anywhere. 

Most of the major shopping centres are on or near Sukhumvit Rd - Terminal 21, Emporium, Central Chidlom, Emquartier, Central World, etc. There are also some good hotels with great rooftop bars and restaurants.  Soi 11 in Sukhumvit is chock full of bars and restaurants, and with the Arab Quarter from sois 1 to 5, and Nana opposite that on the other side of the BTS line, there is never a dull moment. Your only dilemma will be deciding where to eat!

At night time the street vendors set up market, and you can walk, eat, drink and shop.  SO many things to do - if you've never been to Bangkok before, this is the perfect area to stay in. Aside from the big 5 star hotels which are usually on Sukhumvit Rd,  there are countless other 3 and 4 star hotels down the many sois.

I am not sure why anyone has put this down as an 'attraction' as it is simply the name of the main road in Bangkok.  As such, it is lined with shopping malls and restaurants - but not many 'attractions'.  
By all means, take a taxi down on the way to somewhere else but it is not really a place to visit.  It's a bit like Oxford Street in London except there are not as many shops and there is a fair walk between them.

review_title: Interesting by day, vibrant by night
review_text:
BARS, shopping malls and top quality restaurants and clubs are all within walking distance at Sukhumvit while you can easily travel further in the city on the Skytrain which has a number of stops in the area.
Traffic can reach gridlock proportions, but if taxis can't move then tuk tuks can turn on a sixpence and get through impossibly tight gaps while manic motorcycle taxis know all the ducking and diving tricks of the road to get you where you want to go.
Food is a paradise of taste, colour and smell from the blue flash of a street vendor's wok to the fine dining of top class restaurants such as Cabbages and Condoms.
Daytime reveals the city without make-up from sewer aromas wafting up from fractured concrete culverts to piles of rubbish, the roar of vehicles, hoardes of people going to or coming from work and the endless buzz of street businesses where you can get a suit or a soup made to order with equal ease.
Once night descends and the neon make-up is applied then Sukhumvit offers a kaleidoscope of characters and entertainment from booming clubs and bars to street markets and massage parlours.
Just sit and relax with a beer at any one of the vantage point bars on Soi 4 Sukhumvit and the world and all its faces will come to you, fabulous people watching entertainment.
And where else can you buy a combat knife, viagra, T-shirts, fried Tarantula or a fruit smoothie within a few metres?
Sukhumvit may not be the greatest free show on Earth but it is definitely a contender.

review_title: Plenty of options to eat and shop
review_text: Centrally located, plenty of options to eat and shop. I'm a vegetarian so I could find some vegetarian restaurants, dominoes and Pizza Hut
Lots of bargains to be found when shopping in this area and closes late for the night shoppers. Delish fruit juices to be had at the food trucks.
Very central place!  You can do all your shopping and eating over here ! Probably the best place to book your stay as well!

I stay here month at a time when visiting Bangkok, have it all, good food, street food, fine dining, cheap eats, great hotels, good shopping, great clubs, pretty much anything you are looking for you can find here!

review_title: Its a street - but the heart of BKK life
review_text: you have to wander up and down this street -  from international shops to cheap stalls and a few adult entertainment zones as well

Sukhumvit is where all the fancy hotels are located. But it is also where much of the sex trade is highly visible. It was disgusting to watch all the older, western men, strolling with the very young Thai consorts. It was horrible to watch knowing that many of the young ladies had been sold into sex slavery before they started menstruating and will die of AIDS by the age of 20. Absolutely horrible.

review_title: Everything you can imagine - available here
review_text: A whirlwind tour of Bangkok before moving on to Chiang Rai, Chiang Mai and Krabi - I would like to go back to shop - no time.

After eight trips to Bangkok, always staying in the Khao san area, I decided to stay in Sukhumvit for a few nights. Very lively and in your face. Street venders selling, well, just about everything. A lot of nightclubs, bars and restaurants here of all varieties though more expensive than other areas plus just about everyone adds service tax on top here. A beer here will average 160 Baht. In and around Khao san it will average 60 Baht. If you arrive in Bangkok during peak hour, hang back, as the traffic is incredible and a short journey can take you over an hour. This area is good for BTS travel as is any area where you are close to a BTS station.

In sukhumvit area you find people from all over the world, and everyone gets what is asking for . Fun , medication, massage , shopping , eating , drinking, sleeping , money exchange, dancing,and more and more unbleavable things .

I walk out of my hotel on Soi 11, there are multiple Thai massage and barber shops, I often stop for a brief massage and I love getting a shave and haircut, I haven't even made it the 200 feet to Sukhumvit Rd and I feel like new man, ten years younger. As I turn the corn onto Sukhumvit it's a flea market of stalls and food vendors, I eat street food!!!! Love the grilled chicken and spicy papaya salad (Som Tom) with sticky rice! There are plenty of actual stores with good deals on gold etc, I am not good at negotiating but they do bargain and expect a little negotiating of prices. There is Robinsons mall with a movie theatre and bowling alley. I went to park where we all did Tai Chi together with the locals. For adult entertainment there is Nana Plaza and Soi Cowboy, where there are girls, girls girls. Some are pushy but most are respectable and you can have a fun time and enjoy
We stayed in the Sukumvit area for the first time on our last trip to Bangkok, and I can safely say we are coming back. Eating, shopping, relaxing and near / or easy to get to "everything"

How you will like this area depends on what you are looking for. If you want a  crowded, wide-open touristy area then this is it. The unescorted male cannot walk far without hearing touts from the girls wanting to sell a massage (but, really, something more). Noisy bars everywhere. Restaurants in every price range. Street food like crazy. Tons of street vendors selling everything under the sun.
located in the central of Bangkok, easy access to anywhere around Bangkok, through BTS, Tuk-Tuk and the focus place to get hotel too.
A lot of shops and good venues for a drink. All conveniently reached by BTS Sky Train. This is where east meets west.

review_title: Not the best part of Bangkok for tourists
review_text: Many tourists visit Sukhumvit because it is where their hotel is located. Jump on the BTS or take a taxi to visit the more traditional areas of the city.
Main road Sukhumvit is one of the place has many attraction, night seller, food, clothes with cheap price. if you wish to buy cheap souvenirs, this is the place you should go.

The street is a must visit for people who love food, shopping and beer. You will find everything here. Clothes, electronics, accessories, bags, toys, Sitcom DVDs, artifacts, Chinese lamps, mobile covers, cheap gadgets, street food, barbeque, happy pubs, foot massages. The street also has a lot of Indian restaurants. My suggestions would be to try Maharaja Bhog and Balbirs..
Do bargain.

Been to Bangkok four times, and stayed in Sukhumvit area in all my stay, but at difference locations and hotel. The easiest place to get to any other locations in Bangkok due to ample transportation services in the area.

review_title: Buzzing, lively and many places to visit
review_text: If you want busy, loud and hectic then this place is where to stay. All the main clubs and bars are located here and all within walking distance, Visit many shops, malls, restaurants and street food carts also. Really fun place to be and where it all happens!

What an amazing place. You can virtually find anything you want or need all the way along Sukhumvit, from all types of cuisines to any type of product. The markets are brilliant and worth a good look but only start to bargain with the street vendors if you are serious about purchasing their goods. Unbelievable night-time atmosphere. You can find nice quite restaurants to the loudest with good food and great entertainment. If in Thailand a must place to visit.

review_title: nice area with bars and restaurants
review_text: Nice area with bars and restaurants, the only disturbance is the sex tourism there (lady bars and lady boy - nail polish shops), also the Arab quarter is at Soi 5, 7 and 10 and worth walking through if you are there anyway

Fantastic touristy place and you can find it all here. It is crowded to the max but i will hsve to say thst it is safe. Even in the wee hours. Food, bars, shopping and food all over again. Do try the freshly squeezed orange juice that retails for 20baht a pop. Bloody good. The only issue is the humidity. Thats dorts stifling. And not great with kids.

Sukhumvit is a really really long road.. Very touristy, lots of places to eat and shop but shopping isn't very cheap because they really charge you a bomb for everything on that street. The same things near victory is way cheaper. The perpendicular roads are all called soys. Eg. Soy 11, soy 12 etc etc.

While the traffic jams do get to you, the road is lined with attractions for all - night markets at Nana, street food at Soi38, malls at Siam, BTS to go up and down. A good place to be based out of for your stay.

review_title: Alive with the buzz that is Bangkok
review_text: Sukhumvit is a great place to visit to experiance the buzz that is Bangkok. Easy to get to by public transport you can experiance shopping, food stalls, restaurants and at night the area comes alive with colour. Worth the visit.
central area of bangkok . have many hotels and massage salon. you will enjoy to walk sukhumvit street

review_title: This is where the night life begins
review_text: Ok... If you are into pubs, here is the place to hang out at night. There are a number of pubs around Sukumvit area and do remember to pub crawl...you may find all sorts of different pubs around bangkok!

review_title: The longest road in the world .......
review_text:
Sukhumvit is a district of Bangkok, Sukhumvit Road is also the longest road in the world ..... it starts in Bangkok and it finishes close to the Cambodian border. The choice of hotels and restaurants is endless, as is the shopping - and close by is one of the red light districts of Bangkok, Nana (which seems like a safe area to walk around). 
Above the road runs the Sukhumvit Skytrain line which gets you to your destination faster than a taxi which is likely to get stuck in traffic. The side roads (sois) are misleading: on one side are the even numbers on the other one the odd numbers - looks good. Unforunately the odd and even number side streets are not necessarily opposite .... and if you get it wrong, it is a long road ! Have a look at a map before you start to see where you want to go (it may be further away than you think). Having said that: there are plenty of coffee shops and restaurants to rest tired feet.
Managed to eat langar at the Gurdwara here but be careful. It is only served early like 8-10am if I recall correctly. An Asian will feel home here as there were lots of friendly faces around.

review_title: An ends to a means but not really a good "destination"
review_text: Not worth its own trip as this street has many elements you will find in other more noteworthy destinations.  But if you are stayinh in a hotel on Sukhumvit, do take a walk between Nana and Asoke BTS stations to watch the buskers, night life and ladyboys.  Parts of this neighborhood are not for the faint of heart later at night,  but I have never had any security issues walking the streets - use normal precaution in guarding your wallet or purse as you would in any big city
Sukhumvit is a great area with many eateries, cafes, restaurants and shopping.  It is a long stretch road with many different shops, massage place and tailor shops.

I preferred to stay in Siam Square area as was travelling with family (2kids). I was not very comfortable staying at Sukhumvit area with kids around. but did go there for half a day for shopping and just to get a feel of Bangkok. Get off from BTS at Asok station and walk right into Mall Terminal 21. It is an amazing mall with lots of shops and good eateries. Loved eating our lunch at Sunrise Tacos..Food just too good. Street shopping outside is also good for buying Souvenirs but beware some shops were charging exorbitantly.. bought a few Souvenirs like magnets and fancy scarf pins from a big shop named Pink Pvssy. These were very reasonable priced. but I would still prefer Siam Square area to Sukhumvit though when in bangkok.
Sukumvit stands for everything Bangkok is known across the world. It is not about museum or cultural attraction but the stupendous nightlife option that can be accessed from here

review_title: Only way to get around in Bangkok
review_text: Using the sky train is a must if you want to avoid the gridlock traffic. There are many routes/stops to the city's attractions. It's inexpensive & routes are easy to work out. The only downfall we found was in lining up at a teller to buy tickets only to be given change to use in ticket machines which we lined up for again.

review_title: Best area to live for family holidays !
review_text: We have chosen this area for our 4 days stay in Bangkok . The area have lot of shopping markets , clubs , pubs and restaurants . SUKHUMVIT is connected to all bts station .

review_title: Lots to do, eat and shop and plenty accommodation
review_text: A very touristy area with a number of medium to high end malls, lots of food outlets and entertainment; though probably not the best for families. A large variety of accommodation including guest houses/airbnb from budget to high end. Stay in properties in the sois/lanesy if you want some peace.
This area is in the middle of diwntown allowing you to get around quickly particularily on skytrain. Beautiful shopping centers as well small markets. Great for business or pleasure trip.
This place has everything you need. From shopping, restaurants and bars plus it's pretty easy to get to and from with the BTS or the MRT trains.
Many clubs, bars and restaurants really popular with tourists, and therefore prices are a bit above average in this area, however its quite lively day and night, especially at night !
Sukhumvit is in the central part of the city. Good place for shopping with good stay options around with all budget ranges.
Worth staying in sukhumvit full of tourists lively place through out the day lot of malls, pubs, restaurants and spas.
I stayed very close to the Sukhumvit Street and had the opportunity to stroll through this lovely street during my travel around Bangkok.

We stayed in this area.  It has bars, cafes, restaurants.  Choose wisely with your hotel as we initially stayed in a horrible hotel, but managed to find a great one for our last two nights in Bangkok.  The Skytrain and MRT are walking distance.  You also have all the local market stalls.

review_title: Sukumvit is a nice place with family!
review_text: Sukhumvit is a nice place to spend time with family,  what is amazing is the window shops from Terminal 21, one can see good and cheap Souvenirs almost for half a kilo meter.  Pl check the prices in 2-3 shops before you buy.  Most of the shop owners are friendly however you would also find out some staff behaving rudely if you ask for the price and do not buy.   Pl choose the shop person who is looking friendly.  My experience bargain, bargain and they will only call once you start walking.  Best time to go for shopping is during the closing time as they also tend to give good discounts at 9-9.30 PM.  Saturdays/Sundays it would be heavily crowded.  Be watchful of pick pocket specialists

you can't go wrong staying right off Sukhumvit. All the best shopping is on this street whether indoors or on the street itself. The skytrain can take you anywhere you need to go on this street since the traffic can get really bad!

This area has it all , shopping , skytrain , food ,street markets from a tourist point of view it's got the lot .We stop at soi 11 and there are plenty of hotels and food places within walking distance ,all at reasonable prices .If your thing is the more exotic ,then there are soi's for this as well ,it's your choice
Our latest visiting is on Dec 2015. This is a big area,consist few BTS station, many luxury hotels, shopping malls, restaurants. We love to visit Sukhumvit every time we traveled to Bangkok.

Sukhumvit is the Beverly Hills of Bangkok. There are fancy hotels, large luxurious malls with expensive shops and fancy cars whizzing by. There are some reasonable hotels on the back roads, if you want to stay in this area of town.
The sukhumvit road is and exciting place to visit or stay at one of the hundreds of hotel in the SOI lanes. The Road has number of custom Tailor shops and plenty of Souvenir shops.
Major department stores like Emporium, EmQuartier, Central Chidlom, Terminal 21 are all on the Sukumvit road where you can reach by BTS.

review_title: Area is very clean and nice
review_text: Unlike many other places of Bangkok, Sukhumvit area is a bit posh and much cleaner, less traffic (mostly). Dominated  by diplomatic houses (and luxury cars), this area is a bit different than a typical Bangkok city area
you need a place to stay, good food, markets, malls... then this is a right place to choose. all are in walking distance. nice!

I have stayed in the Sukhumvit area so many times I have forgotten how many.  I love the area firstly by arriving by the BTS at Asoke or the MRT at Sukhumvit station.  I stay in a hotel in Soi 19 about a 5 minute walk to Terminal 21 mall and Asoke BTS or a 2 minute walk to my dentist on Asoke road and 4 minutes to Sukhumvit MRT.  The area is full of so many restaurants and shopping to satisfy anybody's needs.  You also have the famous Soi Cowboy which may not appeal to a religious person but it truly is a gem and hidden away enough that a prude would miss it.
Stayed in this part of the city for 2 nights. The place we stayed is very convenient and can easily find food and near to train station.
if you want to see sukhumbit see NANA and the bars around..i m sure the market ,street food and lights would turn you on

review_title: So many shopping, eating and people watching opportunities
review_text: The Sukhumvit Road is a great place to stroll during the day or at night. This strip never seems to sleep as late at night it’s packed with street vendors selling goods, food and even mini-sidewalk bars. It’s really a lively, happening place with so much to offer. You’ve gotta’ spend some time along this strip of the city!

In this area you have everything, many hotels (some expensive) great restaurants or food stalls. Many bars so for nightlife you are doing well here. Not much to see but take the skytrain to the malls or the river boats to go to the temples

review_title: A road that has something for everyone!
review_text:
Sukhumvit is a very long road; as highlighted by some other reviewers. But the main bit for most of us who live in or around Bangkok is the road itself in Bangkok, and the roads that come off it.

If shopping is your thing then you have so many shopping centers along Sukhumvit; my favourites are Siam Paragon, Terminal 21 and Emporium. There are so many restaurants  about and you have such a wide choice of food, one of my favourites is Hemingway's which is across from Terminal 21.

You also have plenty of night time activities going on, with music and lots of lights and glamour available in Soi Cowboy. You also have a selection of night clubs, be they large or small.

Getting about on Sukhumvit is easy if you use the BTS, most of the big shopping centers are connected to BTS stations and if there places not connected to BTS stations you can walk. The traffic jams can be bad and it can take ages to get to places; also taxi's may try to charge flat fee's instead of using their meter.

If you are walking you have loads of 7-11's and Family Marts should you need to hydrate. There are quiet places to go to chill, I like to go to the park next to Emporium, Benjasiri Park, where you can have a walk and enjoy the peace and quiet.

Once night arrives Sukhumvit changes with street markets and bars appearing in certain areas. Remember that if you are shopping in the market haggle, don't just accept the first price they give you. If you are walking about at night I would suggest just be careful and keep valuables safely tucked away. 

In all Sukhumvit is the heart of the city and if you stay on it or near to it you have access to so many different things.

This area is always bustling and changing.  It runs the spectrum from high-end luxury to quite rough.  It is continuously changing.  Depending on the hour it can be a very different area.  It can be a little rough late at night and should not be considered kid friendly in the evenings.
If one stays at the hotel in this area,he will have everything he wants,nightlife,restaurants,shopping all are within walking distance
I like staying on this street as there are many places of interest to visit. The night-life on this street is very vibrant, plus the transit train (BTS) is on this street.

review_title: An interesting area for a walk
review_text: This part of the city is bustling. It's a good place to take a walk, although the walk during the day is much different from the walk you would take at night. Be a little careful at night, but basically safe. Lots of things to see.

The street is not clean,crowded,some addicts or insane were sleeping in the street,
Transportation is very good available all the times,drivers were quiet,quit, friendly even when they don't speak English.
A truly batman town. Streets are alive at night. All of the thai market stuff u can find here. Especially if you into sex antics ;)

A nice roar, with big hotels and apartments. Definitely you must visit the street for dinner and drinks. It has good restaurants, coffee shops, skybars. You can reach the street with skytrain or taxi(I recommend not tuk tuk because is more cheaper by taxi)
The place that has it all. From street food to Irish pub, from local souvenirs to shopping centres. This is the heart of Bangkok for all travellers.

I love walking up and down Sukhumvit and all the side sois. The diversity of restaurants and entertainment is staggering. From massive Malls like Siam Paragon, to the street market that comes alive at night. A truly mad place, again only let down by the famous Bangkok traffic. Ideally you want to be in a hotel around Soi 11 (not the ambassador, please read my review of that dump) you can then walk to most places and those that are further afield you are right nest to the sky train.
Great place to shop particularly ladies hand bags, shoes, clothing store. Night shopping along street side at Soi 11, Soi 9. Street foods having delicious taste with alluring colors.

Best place for meet tourists, shopping and eat outside. Best for Japanese, Indian food. 
Terminal 21 is good for shopping.

review_title: Best area to stay in bangkok
review_text:
Personally, I wouldn't stay in any other area when in Bangkok.
You've got it all.....BTS & MRT easy access. All the shopping you could possible want. Great choice of food from fine dining hotel places to really excellent and cheap street food. Also has Easy access to the canal system for more authentic way to see the city.....and it never seems to sleep

review_title: Great for shopping and late night activities
review_text: We travel to Thailand on early of Nov and spend 3 days in Sukhumvit, Bangkok. Our activities was shopping in MBK & Siam Paragon center, visit 3 temples to exposing the cultural of Buddha religion, we also visit the patpong night market & hang out for some late night activities in a very exciting bar. It's was really enjoyable, Great place to stay in Bangkok!

Sukhumvit is where it all happens ... or in very close vicinity! Always busy, always on the move ... the best place for clubs, hotels, food, nightlife, etc. A great central hub location to base your BKK explorations!
A good place for shopping. Alot of people here speaks English. Always remember to bargain. Sometimes you even get 90% discount if you bargain hard.

This bustling ever active hub of Bangkok, sets the pace for this city that never sleeps!  Hotels, high rises, shops and eateries abound, and it is in fact the longest road in Thailand, but it is the section within that attracts, from 5 star hotels to Terminal 21 and other shopping extravaganzas !

review_title: Great location for business or pleasure in central Bangkok
review_text: This is a great place to stay for both business visitors and tourists. Great central location on the BTS line, plenty of pubs, clubs (without being in a red light district), shopping and entertainment options. Plenty of good hotels to be found here. However, if you arrive in Thailand via DM airport, be sure to leave the airport for easy and cheap transport options outside the airport. Catch a bus to the BTS for just 30B or even catch a taxi with meter. However, DO NOT order a taxi at the Taxi Counter inside DM airport. They charged me 820B, including toll fees that the driver next even used. Even the guy that will walk you outside to put you in a taxi will demand a TIP for doing so. Transport is easy to secure by yourself right outside the airport. If you get your own Taxi you will pay less then 300B to any city location.

review_title: Sukhumvit is one of the most busiest streets in Bangkok.
review_text: One of the longest roads in Thailand, the most attractive part is definitely in Bangkok. From food vendors to cloth stalls during the day and early evening, changes Sukhumvit his face completely later at night and becomes a big party place. Many small, temporary, beer bars right on the street, gives you the first row seat of a cinema. Watching the people walking around, have something to eat or drink or just enjoy chatting with locals and tourists is definitely a highlight. It is a must for every tourist, who want to see a part of Thailand, a different one, not the one in the regular travel guide. Everything your heart may like is available 24 hours / 7 days a week.

review_title: A place of wide social contrasts
review_text:
Situated in central Bangkok, this area is saturated with hotels of all standards, making it an ideal location to explore Bangkok's attractions from.

Whether it's a taxi / tuk tuk to the Grand Palace or one of the major Buddhist Temple areas, shopping at either one of the super exclusive shopping emporiums along Sukhumvit Road or the amazing BMK (Bangkok Market) this is the place to stay.

Nearby are also the bars, night life and ladies of Soi 4, Nana Plaza or Soi Cowboy if that's your scene. 

If it's your first trip to Bangkok, this is the best location to stay and explore.
Couldnt find much bargains. The prices are almost same as in Bangalore. The location is awesome. That's it.

A great place to get dropped off and stay if you've only got one night in bangkok.
close to nana plaza and clubs. lots of night life happening.

If going somewhere in particular, make sure you get off the skytrain at the right stop!  It has shops along the whole stretch. There are also some malls. You'll find Bangkok in microcosm: tailors, souvenir shops, massage parlours, 7/11s, etc. And from the afternoon and in the evening, stalls are erected selling fake polo shirts to sex toys!

The Sukumvit is a great area to stay when on holiday. Good bars, nightlife and modern shopping plazas galore! There are also also lots of delightful little restaurants of many different nationalities to be found in the numerous streets "soi's" of the main road. The BTS sky train also makes travel to other areas alot easier.

Great place for shopping with nice evening market.
lots of good restaurants and bars.
always something happening.
easy to go with skytrain

review_title: Good... And bad... But really good in a bad way
review_text: Well what can u say about sukhumvit.  Your gonna like it... Or hate it.  If your the prim proper type... Well firstly why art you in Bangkok?  But secondly stay away from here.  But if u wanna see some down right crazy stuff that just dousnt happen anywhere else in the world... Here is the place to be... Grown up play ground.  Go here... Spend all your baht... Get checked for diseases when you get home... Enough said?

The Sukhumvit area is buzzing with activity and has great yet safe nightlife. Has Nana square and Nana station within the vicinity. Our hotel being in this area, we had access to everything such as Indian restaurants, pubs, money exchange, massage parlours, shopping etc within 5 minute walking distance. The place is alive and buzzing till dawn.

Tourist hub and paradise. You can get almost anything here. Open till late night (almost all night). Even just for people watching this is a fabulous place. Lots of hotels and eating place from different culture around Sukhumvit. You just have to go and experience it yourself !!!
I have been coming here for many years - probably 30. The change is amazing but it does  look like most modern cities.

The Sukhumvit Soi Nana area, where the Bumrungrad Hospital is located is a cheap and very safe place to stay. Its central and within walking distance to major Malls and nightspots. Halal restaurants abound here as majority of visitors are Arabs. Hotels range from 800THB to 1500THB for decent accommodation. The First Tower, the Park Phloenchit and the Ruenrudee condominium offer furnished apartments for long term visitors and are adjacent to the hospital. Expect to pay around 6-10 for a Halal meal. The MRT stations are about a five minute walk. The MBK mall, Tokyu mall, the Central Mall, Pratunam shopping area are all within half an hour stroll, a 28THB Metro ride or a 100THB taxi ride away. Stay safe, have fun.

review_title: The place to stay at Bangkok
review_text: The most happening place of Bangkok to stay. Easy access to all public transport, affordable hotel and great food.

review_title: Lively Sukhumvit - the heart of Bangkok
review_text: Sukhumvit 20 - our stay at GM Tower with the Emporium and Terminal 21 within walking distance. So much to enjoy like one-hour massages for as little as Cdn $10, street food vendors, coffee shops with the best capuchino - highly recommended.
A major route through the city, from the touristy spots and shopping off New Road, to the quieter upscale neighborhoods near the Emporium. Great shopping everywhere!
its got everything.............good hotels, malls, art gallery, great restaurants......cool pubs........great night life...........24x7 u never get bored

I stayed at Soi 3 which is the most happening place in Sukhumvit. It's great for street shopping, food and night life. Nana Plaza located near Soi 3 and Soi Cowboy is Soi 23. There's a lot to shop on the streets. Whatever they quote, say 1/3 of the price and start walking. You will find almost everything that you need in Sukhumvit.
Sukhumvit roads in Bangkok and pataya were given us the feeling of hope. even if you lost find this road first as it gives the access to all the things around. sukhumvit has it all'

Sukhumvit is an area of downtown Bangkok. It is accessible to public transportation, friendly, safe and has lots to offer a western visitor.It is an exceptionally good place to use as a base. There are loads of restaurants, shopping, locals from many countries so authentic food is easily available and people with at least some English to help you find your way can be found without too much trouble. I wish every review would also mention that Thai people are exceptionally polite. If you are lost everyone you ask will try to help you. Sometimes that means directing you the wrong way because they just can't bear to disappoint but they will always make an effort and be absolutely lovely about it.  Keep asking, don't be shy and don't lose your cool. They have the same communication problem you have and they want to help.
Sukhumvit is a wonderful spot both day and night. There is nothing you cant do or want to do as a busy business/shopping/eating/partying place in Bangkok.
The highlight for me was that it had halal food. Other than that it's ordinary. Everything other place is quite similar.
Fun airport, lots of people to view, places to eat, shopping options, etc... Just be sure you know what terminal & gate, b/c they have long hallways, haha.
Anything and everything is on this road so a good place to be  plus some awesome street food and of yeah foot massages..

Place for night street shopping and street food.. You also find a lots of Arabian food stalls and restaurants. Soi 11 had very good street side sea food vendors where as Soi 4 is the typical red light zone with few a go go bars.
This is a great area in Bangkok to stay when visiting.  I felt very safe and tons of good restaurants and shops.

review_title: One Road which has everything for everyone
review_text:
not many roads in the world can boast of having everything for everyone - food, hotels, shopping malls, small shops, street food, high end restaurants, budget restaurants, etc...you name it this road has it. 

From budget to the expensive and especially for a visitor, this is the place to stay...

Great place to experience BKK! MRT Sukhhumvit (or BTS Asok / BTS Nana) will bring you into the center of Sukhumvit - Soi Nana and its Indian / Middle Eastern food, numerous Western food outlets, live seafood restaturants and the night markets!

review_title: Has gotten more crowded and seedier
review_text: This is the place, if you like crowds, sidewalks crowded with stalls, and office women hooking at the bus stop. Two highlights. - Asoke station area/Robinsons now has a nice, new, big shopping center, and the Middle Eastern restaurants around Soi 3 are terrific. Some folks are commenting about scantily clad girls- that's at Asoke and Sukhumvit, on Soi Cowboy, a block from Sukhumvit. Yeah, you can drink and look and more there, but it's kind of sad. Sometimes in the winter you can see an elephant on Soi Cowboy, and you will also see a bug (to eat) vendor.

review_title: Very busy street, though easy to access to anywhere in the city
review_text: The sidewalks are uneven due to wear and tear and city services not upcoming maintenance of the stone walkways. Some areas are loaded with sidewalk vendors which can make it tough for walking traffic. Car traffic and pollution is a daily problem. But with the Sukhumvit BTS line running right above the street, using the BTS allows for quick movement across the city.
You can easily go around with BTS around Sukhumvit area. A lots of shopping mall and night market along the streets.

review_title: Good place for a look and a beer.
review_text: We went with a bit of apprehension after not enjoying Patong but this was a much more pleasurable expierience.  Sure ther are still plenty of bars and plenty of scantily dressed girls but it just seems much more fun and not as sleazy as other similar places.  You should make a visit if you are in Bangkok.

It's the place to stay if you want to shop, enjoy nightlife and experience Bangkok's notorious traffic jams.
It's full of life n the best malls are close by and can be accessed by sky train/metro

review_title: Best area to stay in Bangkok
review_text: Be sure to book a hotel in this area.  They have many things to do like  soi cowboy and nana plaza. There is many things to do.

Extremely busy but orderly if you are on foot.Access to many shops and sidewalk vendors.We discovered a delightful little cafe named O La La which was perfect for breakfast and an awesome place called Best Beef which we visited twice for supper.Very good value for money and packed out by locals every night.
Pretty a crowded place as. It has a BTS Skytrain Station in here too. A good place as side visit while doing transit to somewhere else.

If you come to Bangkok for the 1st time. 
If you want to explore Bangkok life. 
Or simple come for business.
You can find everything you want here in Sukhumvit area. Dining, Enjoying, dancing, shopping, night market, club-ing etc. all in 1 spot (or walking distance).
No matter what the time you can find virtually everything here. From shopping food or bars from the store front or street venders. Come and try your bargaining skills.

review_title: Worth a visit to see the real Bangkok
review_text: Area is very busy with street stalls selling all sorts of goods, plenty of massage parlours and the traffic is crazy but it's a place you need to go and experience for yourself.

October 2015 . I visited the Sukhumvit Road the first time after 21 Years and i was a little bit shocked . It is not like before , the small Hotels , where i used to sleep are vanished , but instead they built a lot of 5 Star Hotels and Shopping Malls and Fast Food Restaurants . And there is a lot of Traffic . But they are still a lot of small Shops for Clothes etc.
I am not so crazy about the strip.... But it is a fun place and the stalls are interesting.  It just has changed so much over the years

You have all on this street.
BTS, all known malls, markets and a lot of cars smoke.
Can't be in Bangkok and miss this street.

The area has exceptional public transport. You'll find good malls like Terminal 21 here. And there's a couple of parks like the Queen's Park where you can relax. Lots of prostitutes hanging around even till 7 in the morning.

Everything placed well. Good place to stay, very convenient transport system, variety of shopping methods and also good place for fun loving night life for adults.
However, at night (around 9) most of shops are being closed.
Clean, easy to get food, many places open for foot massage and still can go by walk for shopping area
If you want nightlife and party in bangkok you can go to khao san road, but you can also go to these area here is there not so many young tourist so if you little grown up i think better

I really don't care for Bangkok, and the street is one of the reasons why.  The traffic is terrible, making crossing the streets on foot hazardous. The sidewalks are taken up with vendors forcing you to walk in the dangerous streets, and the vendor are extremely aggressive and sometimes dishonest.  I know there are a few indoor malls on Sukhumvit which would be better,  It it were my choice I wouldn't go to Bangkok at all.

Sukhumvit between Soi 2 to 63 has an extensive collection of hotels, restaurants, shopping plazas, movie theaters, bars and entertainment areas. Everything in the way of life support is there. The BTS (Skytrain) runs above Sukhumvit and cabs are readily available.
Don't bother visiting this area during the day as the street markets are now restricted to evening opening times

Ladyboys.  Prostitutes.  Hustlers.  Shopping Malls.  Indian Vegetarian Restautants.  Street Stalls.  Sukhumvit is vibrant, exciting, and seedy.  It is the Bangkok of popular perception, a thriving part of town.

Sukhumvit spans a large section of Bangkok with a few different public transport stops.  The most notorious is Nana which seems to cover some of the seediest parts of this place.

Tourists abound in Sukhumvit.  So many farang and so much for them to do.  This is not really the place to bring a family though and definitely not at night.  

Night is when Sukhumvit wakes up.  It seems to go on until quite late in the morning.  Perhaps that's why there are surprisingly few places to get a breakfast meal.

It can be quite an effort to dodge the persistent and assertive ladyboys.  Some may well feel very uncomfortable here.  It is not the cleanest place and with all the street stalls out can be a bit close.  

There are dozens of bars and places to go.  It could be intimidating and difficult but for those who are prepared to accept it, Sukhumvit is quite the experience.

review_title: Good for specialty shopping, but busy
review_text: Sukhumvit is actually a road that goes all the way down to the border of Cambodia, but the part in Bangkok gives very much a business like experience. Brand shops here, but also cheaper places in Soi leading away from it.
Very good night life but during the day its a nice place too..her you can find a very good market.many cafe and many many bars with huppy hours.
Best way to get here if you can use the BTS, traffic can be a pain during peak hours. A must see if you have the time, lots of variety of dinning options.

Sukhumvit area is a life area which does not sleep day ant night, any time you need shops, restaurants, pubs, massage.... just go out the hotel and you will find. BTS is a great way for transport, easy and cheap.
The best place to shop. Lots to look at not that expensive.  Can batar. People always frindly. Very local easy transport
go to chequers pub in soi4 excellent pub australia,london,indian, and other countrymen visit this place very often

review_title: Party Time! Walk time (it's long) and Shop time!
review_text: You can get all you need here... Shopping, Walking, Partying, Massages and yes the rest Bangkok is known for...  Long cab ride though if you are staying by KhoSan...
We stayed in a hotel in the Sukhumvit area while visiting a doctor at Bumrungrad Hospital. Sukhumvit had many dining options, small stores and amazing mall shopping all within walking distance.

We went down to Soi 38 to have dinner, which was fabulous..we were of course, the only Americans that we saw..the choice of foods were abundant, and the mango sticky rice was the best!!
Walking the street later, is fairly busy, to say the least, and happy to get in a cab back to the quiet hotel.
Everything imaginable available on this road! The traffic is a giant undulating serpent of cars and motorbikes...but when you are there...listen....almost no horn honking!

review_title: Soi Cowboy right next to metro station
review_text: Walk out of the station and the 1st street is Soi Cowboy, get off at Sukhumvit and right there, all the go go bars in one street. They have some specials for happy hour which ends around 8pm.
A nice area to stay in while in Bangkok.  Hotels are within walking distance of night shopping, bars, restaurants and mall.

review_title: a lot to see, a lot of traffic
review_text: a good location, many hotels, many things to see. but always traffic. i stayed there for one week. if you want to be close to everything then you should come here

review_title: Nice Place in Bangkok for Pubs & Food
review_text: Great place where so many pubs available for chilling also can found nice food for mostly Indian, Thai & Chinese food. For Enjoy must visit with friends.

review_title: Sukhumvit is the name of the street
review_text: Sukhumvit is the name of the street about 10km long from the heart of Bangkok to outer Bangkok, always deadlocked with traffic. Best way to travel is by meter taxi, although services by public transport you need some Thai language to survive. Excellent shopping Malls are all the way along the Sukhumvit, rainy days make even slower.

review_title: it have everything a tourist/travelers want
review_text: Its the heart of Bangkok. it have everything a tourist need/want during their visit.cheap to high end restaurants, clubs, night life, street food, branded shop, super mall, hospital, BTS, money exchange, spa, just name it and you will get it in sukumvit. it can be say that, If you haven’t been to sukumvit, you didn’t see Bangkok.

its relly enjoyble arya & swa  some difrent  power ful man woman
 we take picture .missing  just indian culture

review_title: Interesting, fun and great people watching with a beer
review_text: We got off at Nana on the BTS and strolled the Soi's of Sukhumvit. Lots to offer in the way of bars, restaurants, shops etc. During the afternoon it is great to bar hop and watch the goings on of tourists, locals & the street food carts. During the evening things heat up and become even more vibrant and exciting and busy. Definitely a must see when visiting Bangkok if for no other purpose than strolling around and soaking up the atmosphere.
Excelent trip if you are in Bangkok.. On the list "must be visit". Good destination for a nice day..

It's a good place for travellers, tourists .. and those who seek night-life.
Plenty of eateries, shopping .. good local transport. The tuk-tuks are great.

review_title: A great street with everything on it.
review_text: Sukhumvit is a very long street and we walked a large part of it. It has everything on it depending on which area of it you are at. The sites range from the red light areas with clubs at Nana sky train stop, to pubs and bars, massage parlours and different kinds of food from all over the world to high end apartments, houses and high end dining with great restaurants. There is a wide variety of street food and a night market. There are luxury malls with high end designer products and food courts with amazing gourmet food. Lots of street food at Soi 38.  The sky train runs along this street which makes it easy to get around. There are interesting streets running off of Sukhumvit. Take some time and walk part of it.  Sukhumvit has it all.

review_title: Great food and entertainment plus heaps of accommodation options
review_text: If your looking to slip the backpacker seen in Kho San Rd then maybe try Sukhumvit, from Soi 4 to 13 is a heap of accom , eating and partying options just depending on your budget and preference , great bars , restaurants and hotels in Soi 11 and if your feeling a little riska then head to Nana Plaza for a look...

Sukhumvit is right in the middle of the city where you can find anything you'd wish in Thailand... Restaurants, Pubs, Nightlife, Shopping... the place is centrally connected with public transportation & is the epicentre of tourism in Bangkok... I'd recommend to stay in this area as many attractions will be closeby.

review_title: Bangkok to the Cambodian border at Koh Kong
review_text: One can walk, run, drive, bus, cycle the whole way to Koh Kong in Cambodia along this famous road if one wants to. However, within Bangkok along with Silom it is probably the best known road/street for visitors. It is possible to buy anything here from a Rolls Royce costing in excess of THB 35,000,000 to a plate of Pad Thai costing a mere THB 35-50 and everything in between. Only hassle, but one most people seem to put up with, is that footpaths are more-or-less street stalls. There are many things that could be said about Sukhumvit that are best left unsaid but 5 minutes on that road and most people would understand my point.

Yes is in the red light district but I've stayed in 2 of the hotels so far and not had any problems. As in most things if your looking you will find it otherwise enjoy the full on experience of amazing food stalls, cafes, restaurants and bars that is the heart of sukumvit the BTS is within walking distance but there are plenty of taxis, tuk tuks and bikes if you prefer

review_title: Could have been the AVENUE of Bangkok
review_text:
I live on Sukhumvit Road and this is the place I like to live at. Walk to Subway and skytrain within a few minutes. Restaurants, some of the best restaurants you find in the world a few minutes from my condo.

But the market on the side walk is an eyesore. And they only sell crap. Asking 3000 Baht for a LOLEX that would cost around 300 Baht. As soon as they close down this KNICK KNACK market it will be a pleasure walking up and down Sukhumvit Again.

I used FOODLAND for all my food purchases and I used to love to walk there. Now it has turned in to down town Lagos with people asking if I want to buy cocaine when I leave with my milk. Now I have found myself avoiding the area up lower Sukhumvit and I miss it as it used to be back in the days

The night life, well, I go to other areas. The obvious RED LIGHTS and the not so obvious RED LIGHTS. But when you come in to a place with only foreign males and local girls. Yeah, you can be pretty sure the girls aren't there because they like the interior design. I suggest you head up to Ekkamai or Thong Lor instead

This is the red light districts and part of the city's DNA. It's always busy, noisy and full on but as a tourist it should be experienced. The street market is great at night, there are many eateries although many are overinflated, and plenty of westernise shopping, alongside the sleazier aspects.

This a must visit street. Lively place to be. Food, Bars, Live Band, Best foot massages at very cheap rate. Massages of all hue and colors. Famous night life around the corner. Everything in life are available !

review_title: Most happening place of the city
review_text: You can find Soi Cowboy and Nana Entertainment plaza along with other activities in this area. Avoid going at night alone as you may come across with some lady boy with mischievous activity.

review_title: A busy area with transportation available and plenty to see
review_text: A busy area with transportation available and plenty to see. This is a major intersection of the Metro and Sky Train. Plenty of shopping fro Malls, stores and street vendors. A major center for restaurants. Theaters, bars and attractions all in a few block area.
There are many areas in Bangkok but Sukhumvit is a major and very good place. Nice hotels,shopping,restaurants,sky train, you can find everything there.
The longest street in Thailand, going up to Pattaya. Main way in the city and a guaranteed traffic jam at all times, day and night.
Great location to venture out - go to all the high street shops, movie theaters and bars around while avoiding the Khao San road crowd.

review_title: its of course the famous red light area
review_text: Sukhumvit  changes from a busy area of people by day going about their business to by night to a busy area of people going about their business   with a difference  all very interesting. But however can be a bit sleezy

Very busy road as lots of streets . Entertainment starts around 10 at night till 5 am in the morning . Good bars , disc like levels ( one of the best in bbk ) , climax disc ...... Nana Plaza with bars and go go bars . Shopping and street food . Well connected with MRT and other local transport . A must visit if you are in bbk .
If you love the busy rush, seeing different kinds of people and activities, bars and restaurants, shopping, this street is awesome at night!!!!

Sukhumvit is one of the main roads running north and south thru Bangkok.  All kinds of businesses and restaurants are located on both sides, and plenty of stops for public transportation.  It's always busy, and if you need to find a taxi at 4 a.m., it's no problem here.
it's in the downtown, close to the BTS u can find anything here shopping food, massage, hotels, if you r coming to BBK u should book in sukhumvit.

Lots of good tailors, shopping malls and excellent public transport to get around the rest of Bangkok.  A street market in the evenings is good for bargaining.  There are lots of massage parlours along the main street plus street food.  Take a look at the fish restaurants  close to the Sukhumvuit Soi 1 - during the day these are clothes or food markets but at night turn into fish restaurants - street food really but excellent value and great food.

Sukumvit is a well connected with Road,Water, MRT, BTS (Underground & above ground Metro Rails) transport from all places in Thailand. Its quite busy during day time due to road traffic but still keep running due to its advance MRT/BTS Rail system. 
Its a hub of cafes, restaurants, hotels, pub, bar, live music and other stuff's on the other hand its a very high secure zone due to Embassies around this area.

All the High rise apartment & condominium are well maintained and mostly have their own recreation areas like Zym, Swimming pool, Squash, Tennis court, Basket ball extra. 
Soi 1 to Soi 63 are main division of this area. Sukumvit is good for roadside night shopping. It is worth visit to Highly designed shopping mall Terminal-21 & Robinson on soi 21.
Sukumvit is a balance between a fast moving life, advance technologies, food and luxuries.

Great place to holiday especially now that the winter season is upon us. Cheap, friendly people and the food is international. I have lived here 5 years and only vacation in the US. I am a retired US Army Officer and a former GS 13 (DOD employee) Great golf close by as well.

review_title: Something everyone...especially if you have some ceep pockets.
review_text:
Nearly forty gears ago, you could travel up Sukhumvit and think that you were headinto the country side. Now however, it has become a nightmare of concrete and traffic.
There is an eclectic array of things to see and do. Those who have the money, may care to visit Central Embassy, those not as generously endowed can try the many other shopping centres Central Chitlom, Siam Centre and Paragon etc. we even visited a Home Pro Store today...on Ploenchit...who would thought!!!!....a hardware store so close to the British Embassy and the home of so many chic European Brand names. 
At night, there is the night market starting at approx Soi 4 all the way through to Asok. There are the Indian tailors, the street walkers, the porno and souvenire black marketeers, the fruit carts and sidewalk bars that open way past midnight. And of course, there are the girlie bars on Nana and the Arab Quarter surrounding the Grace Hotel. 
To some it is Sodom and...to others a playground. For others, their livelihood both legal and lllegal. It is disgusting and eye opening at the same time. However, l still remember the old mansions, the Ambassador Zoo in the forecourtand the greenery at the far end of Sukhumvit. It has changed a lot, not necessarily for the better but that is supposedly progress.

review_title: Sukhumvit Soi 11 is The Party Street
review_text: Sukhumvit Soi 11 is where you will find some of the more popular clubs in Bangkok. A very hot and popular club is called Levels. Another famous bar is Cheap Charlies' at the entrance to the Soi. The street comes alive and is filled with party goers in the evenings, makes for great people watching.

A central commercial area,  everything you could want,from,modern mallso to smaller specialist shops. A great place to find made to measure tailors but be sure to haggle the price down. There are a few ladyboy "massage " places that should be avoided unless you really want to catch something unpleasant in a dirty sweaty darkened room!

This is a great area to stay and from which to go and visit Bangkok if it is your first time. Very well connected by BTS Skyline with your fix of hotels, restaurants, bars and ... some of the shady stuff too.
Bars, clubs, malls, everything you need and thing you don't need, you can find on sukhomvit. Soi 11 is recommended.

It is at heart of Bangkok and accessible by both MRT and BTS train. This area has modern plazas, night market, different cuisine restaurants (esp hilal food).
Tip for Hilal food: only 300 meter from Nana BTS station is Arab street which is heaven for hilal food.
One of the most favorite and key shopping and entertainment zone of Bangkok, had good time with family and friends.  Been quite often to this place and love it.

This is at the heart of traditional shopping Bangkok. 
You find lots of variety shops,  small malls,  hotels,  late night opening shops and off streets. Restaurants and cafes as well as street food vendors. 
Sky train stops here and you never get stuck for a means of transport.
One of the old road in Bangkok.  Busy with traffic anyway is handy to catch BTS,  Lot of food and goods for shopping.
Nice tourist place, but the prices increase many in six month, it's better to paid your items in a shop without tourisme area

review_title: The place to be for all your needs
review_text: A good district to be with all amenities within easy reach by good public transportation.  If you seek more, the 'red light district' Nana Plaza is located here for some entertainment and laughs.  Do be mindful not to be ripped off as tourists.

review_title: FUN place to stay,  but watch your back.
review_text: I love staying in Sukhumvit when I'm in Thailand because it is so convenient. The train is right there at Asok. Great location for tourist to start out from. There's a mall, hotels, or rent an apartment, clubs, restaurants, street shopping ( here's where you better watch your back, as my $600 cell phone was just nabbed in March 2015), tailors, internet, ATM, McDonalds, grocery stores, 7/11, exchange, telephone shops, postal services, and a whole lot going on all times of the day and night. I'd stay here again but my phone will be well out of reach. In fact I just bring my cheep nikon from now on. It's not a smart phone but it takes an international sim.  I heard of 2 more people who also got phones nabbed in this neighborhood. Just pay attention. Especially when you're with friends,  not on your guard, or had a few drinks. That's when you are being watched.
Definitely a district to visit. Go to the hotels in Sukhumvit, it's a great area. Feels really safe to walk around as a tourist.

Sukhumvit in my  mind is at the heart of Bangkok and comprises the very beat of the city, from hotels, shops of every description, restaurants, Internet facilities, postal facilities, tuk tuks everywhere, massage if you want it, Terminal 21 and other spectacular retail malls.  Not to be missed however short your visit to Bangkok may be !!

review_title: Great area to rent hotel or condo.
review_text: This is one of the main streets in bangkok where most business, restaurants, and hotels and condos are located. I alwaays try to stay near this area, but not this time. Skytrain travels some of this route.

A bustling safe place with lot of hotels eateries and ease of getting around due to presence of sky train and underground all length of the road. Found the place to be very interesting with lots of road side eateries and shopping malls etc

I like to come at Sukhumvit area. because, get more shopping place. The convenient travel A train service along the route The famous shopping Although the road has a traffic jam. But not an obstacle for my travel. There is also an option for me is to travel by subway. And train on clay
This is the main place in Bangkok to get bargains and barter a deal with all the shop keepers, You can get designer label shirts and all sorts along with weapons and paintings, you name it its here
Sukhumvit road will take you from central bangkok with it's nightlife, mega shopping complexes and offices through commuterland all the way to Pattaya. Someyhing for everyone

review_title: Fascinating area but...the traffic. Oh my, the traffic...
review_text: Sukhumvit is an area centred on westernmost portion of the infamously congested Sukhumvit Road. Most of its sois (side-alleys) are fascinating and full of surprises. Bangkok-style surprises. Lots of decent hotels, from affordable to lavish, lots of restaurants with many cuisines available. Sukhumvit also hosts two of bangkok's more...uh...colourful entertainment precincts: soi Nana (soi 4) and soi Cowboy (between Asoke-Montri Road aka soi 21, and soi 23). And in Sukhumvit, you're never far from a 7-11 or a massage shop. A BTS (Skytrain) lines runs down the centre of Sukhumvit Road, and this is often the best rransport option, as the traffic is often painfully slow.

Sukhumit is a a delightful area of Bangkok with all modern and Thai stores with excellent shopping. It also has all the tourist pleasure and attraction places. Excellent restaurants of different types of food. In the evenings the sides of the Main Street converted into a miles long bazaar with small stalls selling handicrafts, electronics,clothes etc etc.a real fun place. Must visit

review_title: Great for shopping lots of noise and hustle and bustle
review_text:
Remember to haggle if your buying something from a street stall.

Sukhumvit road runs forever you can stop off at many of the large shopping malls from Siam Centre, Paragon, Central World via Chidlom, then you have Nana for entertainment. Most of the shops are linked now by a walk way.
The new EM Quarters is looking great.
