Wherever you stop off at on your journey  there is plenty to do and see.

Lots of Thai food stalls at cheap prices.

Never ever try to cross the roads here always use the up and over stairs.

review_title: Great for shopping and easy to around.
review_text: VERY much a hub of Bangkok, shopping everywhere, Food stalls run by Thai's are cheap and great quailty. Remember to bahter and it is central to the trains.That have easy maps and speak the stations in English as well Thai. Short train ride to MBK

One of the best shopping area in bangkok, with bts Nana, Asok along this road , major hotels including Sheraton, Westin and Centre Point, shopping mall Terminal 21 and the latest EM district which includes the new Emporium and Equatier was definately a shopping, food and entertainment paradise. Very chic , new and cheap hostels like The Blocks Hostel on soi 11 and One Day Pause and Forward hostel will be great choice for back packers .
If you plan for Halal food, luxury shopping, nice Middle East perfume or just a special night life explore, visit here. The best way to hang out is BTS sky train and MRT.
This is the main and central place in bangkok the best part is that this area is the central of bangkok

I love this place and i will always if possible stop in Bangkok Sukhumvit on my travels in Thailand. It takes around 45 minutes to an hour to get there from the airport by taxi, but once you are there it offers so much. You will find it a real assort on the senses, you see life in real colour. I always forget how hot it is so make sure you drink lots of water. Sukhumvit road stretches for miles all the way to Pattaya. I would recommend staying in or around soi 1- 26  this offers all levels of accommodation along with an unbelievable choice of places to eat, not to mention the culture, shopping and also adult entertainment- Nana PLaza soi 4 and Soi Cowboy soi 21-23. After all that if you need some peace and quiet head for one of the parks to relax like Benjasiri park which is next to the Emporium shopping centre nearest sky train Phrom Phong.

Sukhamvit area is burstling with life.There are many hotels from various chains in this area.Top end shopping mall Emporium is here with a choice of eateries to choose from.BTS is also available so one can go to any other aprt without any problem.

review_title: heart of the city but can be really noisy and crowded
review_text: Take a walk from Sukhumvit 1 and walk until your tummy is full. We did and tried soft shell crabs, oysters, some rumali rotis, ice creams, coconut water with cream and list goes on and on....Also amazing is the shopping here as you will find lots of small treasures. Beware though it can be a bit rowdy especially in the evenings and nights.

Sukhumvit is the Heart of the City and offers many shopping areas, hotels and restaurants.  The downside is the street traffic.  Leave yourself plenty of time to get to your destination if travelling by Taxi,

Sukhumvit is a very lively place with lot of sports clubs, bars, restaurants, massages centers. The night never ends till the dawn. People moving around, having fun, hanging around, this place is always alive.`
one of the most happening places in bangkok. if u r sitting in a taxi, u may wana get down everywhere, cause everything is so attracting.

Crowded, touristic, shopping district, massage, shows, every thing could be done here with lots of options to choose from. Sukhumvit its the heart of Bangkok and its a nice place to plan around. Easy to move with the sky train and with lots of shopping districts all over there, you can't miss it....

review_title: Sukumvit21 is the best area to live in
review_text: I have been living on this road for 10+ years. It's certainly a convenient place to stay. All I wanted is here. The banks, restaurants, shopping malls, fresh market, supermarkets, theatres, bowling and many more. The recommendation of hotels for tourists 1. The Sheraton Grand Sukumvit 2. The Westinghouse 3. Millennium 4. Furama all of these hotels above I have sent my friends to stayed before and all of them were happy. This Sukumvit21 alley is providing 3 ways of fast public transportation which is Sky train, subway and pier. All attractions in Bangkok mostly could access easily by those 3. The traffic is bad every where in Bangkok. It's the best way to get around by those 3 types of transportations.

Its a crowded and tourist filled area. There are many restaurant and hotels here! Shopping malls like terminal 21 is really close. One can walk to the nana street from there. This area has all the things a tourist needs to explore. The location is convenient for families as well! But be careful of frauds there. At the end it was a vey nice experience staying in sukhumvit

I really like staying in Sukhumvit because it is central to many things. Soi 19 is really good because the sky train and metro stations are conveniently at the end of the street. There are many local attractions nearby and the area is pretty safe.

There are many areas in Bangkok but Sukhumvit is a major one. In the 80s the place is just a busy road with many business shops but just in 30 years span it has becoming a major area crowded wiith shopping malls of international calibers. Not to be missed.

If you're looking for the Hangover Bangkok experience thats the place for you so be sure to head to Sukhumvits club alleys like Soi Cowboy and others...street bars, hookers and old guys hanging out with 18yo girls may not be something for families. Thus Soi 1 and 38 have the best street food in Bangkok city.   Besides that and the shopping malls Sukhumvit doesn't offer anything special.
This area has the the longest street and most shopping malls, trendy restaurants, night life and hotels. A good place for visitors to stay or to travel here for shopping and entertainment.
Sukhumvit road are contains a good combination of business and entertainment including the roads Ploen Chit, Chit Lom, Witthayu area.

review_title: Sukhumvit really is the centre of Bangkok life and entertainment
review_text: Sukhumvit offers the largest range of accommodation, eating and shopping experiences in Bangkok. It is easy to travel to anywhere in Bangkok from this area on the Sky Train and Metro networks and has some of the cheapest and most expensive hotels, self contained apartments and guest houses in the entire city. The range of restaurants is large and there are super markets offering local and imported food, the expected ritzy department stores, jewelers, art shops, and tailors as well as interesting hardware stores, Chinese emporiums and Asian food suppliers where the locals shop as well as the tourists.

review_title: Everything in the world in a place that is a bit unreal
review_text:
It is a bit hard to write a review about an entire area. If you are just coming to Bangkok you can stay here and I am sure you can find great entertainment, food, and shopping and probably anything else you want. 

On the other hand most of Thailand is nothing like this. If you only stay here you don't get to see the real country.  This would be like coming to America and only staying in Manhattan.
Nice Location. Nice place to stay. Sky train, restaurant and shopping site is available. From here a traveler can move any where in Bangkok.
Sukhumvit community gave us a great local feel and met a of our needs for shopping, currency exchange and fabulous dining!

When i went to Bangkok I stayed at Sheraton Sukhumvit because of the localization, nexto to a metro station and a shopping. It´s a little bit far from the touristic places in Bangkok but the price was better. The traffic at hush hours was awful, much better take the train or the metro than a taxi (be careful to no be fooled by the taxi drivers or the tuck tuck drivers). It´s a large avenue, really loud and noisy.

review_title: One of the Hubs of Bangkok
review_text:
Sukhumvit and its associated sois (side streets) is one of the hubs of commerce, hotels, shopping and restaurants in Bangkok.

There are huge air conditioned shopping malls offering every imported brand luxury you can think of, some of the top name hotels, street sidewalk shopping, and many restaurants covering most of the world's various cuisines.

The sidewalks are crowded with stalls, with hawkers calling out their wares.  There are bargains to be had, but do not expect the brand items to be the real McCoy.  They are all fake.

Soi Nana and Soi Cowboy are two bar and go-go bar areas if you are looking for that kind of entertainment.  But be careful, some of those beautiful looking girls are lady boys.

Another word of warning.   Over the past year there have been many reports of police harassment of tourists with police asking tourists to provide a urine test for drugs.  I think most of this has stopped now, but if it does happen to you, know your rights.  You can insist on being taken to a police station for the urine test and do not have to provide it on the street.

Also be very careful not to litter.   Even a cigarette butt carelessly dropped on the ground can bring down corrupt police on you.  They only target tourists for this, because tourists are the ones with money.

Other than that, have a fun time.

This is a place highly populated with many side streets where lots of people live. Parts of this place have rather seedy looking establishments like cheap hotels but in fact are full of girls waiting to give pleasure. It has improved over the years but it is still very much the same underneath. It is dirty like most of Bangkok and smelly. Keep away

review_title: Everything you could ever want and more!
review_text: Whether you have family in tow or your the Solo traveler, the Sukhumvit area is full of all types and interesting things.  From clothing to food to banking to hotels.  This area has it all.  The Landmark, JW Marriott, Majestic Suites and many other nice stays along this stretch.  Also you have Siam Paragon and Terminal 21 a short BTS trip apart from each other.

review_title: Sukhumvit is where all the action is in Bangkok.
review_text: Sukhumvit is where all the action is in Bangkok.  I love walking along and seeing what all the street vendors are selling.  Don't be afraid to bargain for the best price.  It's all part of the fun. In the late evening after all of the street shops have closed the food vendors open.  Don't be afraid to try all of the street food; it is excellent!
Whenever we visit Bangkok we always prefer this area. You get everything here. From good hotels to restaurants and shopping areas are so close!this neighborhood doesn't sleep.
If you want to be in the middle of it all, go here. Everything from shopping, to food, to nightlife. Easily accessible by skytrain.

This is the heart of Bangkok, with all the famous shopping malls and markets nearby. Using the BTS line which goes through this area, you can travel pretty much to all the temples and attractions in Bangkok. Taxies are a dime a dozen, the traffic however can be a problem during the peak time, so getting in and out from the airport takes much longer.

review_title: Avoid as much as you can
review_text: It is a prostitute area , difficult to move at night especially with kids . try to avoid staying there. rest of the bangkok is more beautiful.

review_title: Great access to all of Bangkok!
review_text: Always something to do, skytrain on your doorstep, lots of places to eat and drink, great markets lining the main streets to get yourself some bargain gifts for friends and family back home!

review_title: take the skytrain to 55 soi thonglor.
review_text: Go into Mahbookrong shopping center its full of bargains .eat in the intercon hotel great seafood buffet at lunch time.
Sukumvit is the place to stay sky train on your door step to get around bangkok , terminal 21 big shopping centre great place to eat cheap food , soi cowboy and nana plaza everythink you need

review_title: Best Spot in Bangkok to get Arround!
review_text:
From this place it is the fastest and easyest way to get to all the main things to see in Bangkok.

The markets, Railway stations, Shopping Malls. 

book Your hotel in this place and you see more than anyone else!

This area is a shopping area of interest to visit, due to a variety of interesting attractions you over in Bangkok can be found here, including enjoying the beauty of the city from the rooftop restaurant here.

review_title: To enjoy Bangkok, stay somewhere near this place
review_text:
This is the center of Bangkok, the one stop to go everywhere else. But extremely busy during the evening (for cars). Siam is on it (with all those big malls and hotels), Nana is here (yes, that Nana too), Arab town (a street filled with Middle-Eastern, African and Indo-Pak Sub-continental food and businesses), the major embassies, Soi Cowbow, 5-star Hotels, and what not. BTS/Sky-train runs on top of the road so you have access to the major parts of the city. Few stations down and you have the bus service to Pattaya. Few stations up north and you get access to Airport Rail Link. A station towards west (National Stadium) and from there you can get a tuk tuk or taxi and roam around the major attractions near the river; temples, Grand Palace, Khaosan Road, China Town.
So if you're in Bangkok only for travel, try getting a hotel near Sukhumvit.
sukhumvit is the place for nightlife entertainment.  From food to drinks and clubs you name it and its all there.  very lively place.
Nothing special, there are only a few restaurants and they are not open till very late. Not much to do except a few bars - targeted at Falangs

review_title: Street food, shopping, easy access to subway
review_text:
Not my favorite part of Bangkok but it's chock full of restaurants, bars, street food vendors and shopping stalls. Definitely a good place to buy a fake Rolex, if that's your thing. I wouldn't bring the kids to this area as it gets very sleazy around Soy Cowboy, like really sleazy, hookers, strippers and tourists looking for a good time.
It's got a few malls, parks and a subway though. We had a few great meals and stayed in a very comfortable hotel in Sukhumvit. If you're going to be staying in Bangkok for a while then it might be worth checking out, if you're there briefly I'd skip it.

there's nothing that you can get in Bangkok which you cannot in Sukhumvit. THis is literally the heart of Bangkok. From massage parlors to excellent eateries of any cuisine, you will get everything here.

Great place to visit for a bit of everything. However, taxi drivers and tuktuk drivers are massive pests and try their absolute best to rip you off. Ensure taxi drivers use the meter when you get in, not many of them want to.

review_title: Huge area - impossible to reach all ...
review_text:
I had never been in Bangkok before I met my present wife early last year.
She had been living in Bangkok for 10 years and adviced us to stay in this area when visiting her.

Travelling to Bangkok I therefor stayed in the Sukhumvit area, at Parinda Hotel parallell with Sukhumvit Soi 4. Around here there are a lot of bars, shredders, 7-11s, pharmacies, shopping centres, restaurants, massage shops and near Nana and Asor (near Sukhumvit Soi 19) stations for the Skytrain and a lot more.

At the same time this area is very noisy, and as far as I have noticed pasing by it is more quiet around Sukhumvit Soi 50 (and up?).
When the small street shops pop up in the evening the pavements become narrow. I have not heard about active thieves here, but be careful anyway. It is almost worse with the few beggers. I tried to give some small coins to one of them, and then suddenly another dump guy got desperate and wanted some coins as well.

The varity of standard for the different buildings is big! But it seems like more and more old buildings are exchanged with modern buildings. The prices at the shopping centers probably follow the standard of the buildings.

When you are in this area you should go to the small belt shop La Franché in 1st floor of Ploenchit Center (http://www.bangkok.com/shopping-mall/central-department-store.htm). La Franché also has 2 more stores, but can't remember where they are. I was told they were starting an online store for worldwide orders.

At Asor Skytrain station you can walk directly into Terminal 21 (http://www.bangkok.com/shopping-mall/terminal21.htm), a big shopping center (mall) with a lot of different kind of stores and restaurants. And even a cinema.

In general difficult to give the correct point for this area, It is so dependent of what you like. For me too noisy in the popular areas, but it is lovely to walk around an experience the pulse.

Enjoy your trip here!
Ladies this is a must go to for the shopping, guys if the ladies do get there then make sure you room in your bag.
Visit often Sukhumvit is faranville - lots of expats, loads of retail tourism quality shops and entertainment and excellent transport systems
v happening and interesting place, not a place for kids but fun to check out and people watch. a lot is going on at night if you know what i mean
Sukhumvit.road one the longest roads in thailand understand it's from Bangkok to singapour almost very busy millions things to see and worth a visit

I had booked my hotel on Sukhumvit 27 as It was very conviniently accesible to the sky train and the underground. It is one of the largest roads in Southeast Asia. Truly a great way to enjoy Bangkok. Great choices for restaurants, bars and night life. There are many street vendors offering some of the most amazing local food.

The center and heart of BKK...you have everything from the largest malls to the smallest street stalls so every moment here is unique and amazing! Fav spot: Soi 11..street food stall with Orange Bar cart, that opens at midnight.
Sukhumvit area is great. It easily gets accessed from every where. Near to shopping malls and also restaurants.
The pulse of Bangkok. The place where you can party, dine, hang around, shop, stay and whatever you can dream of in Bangkok. One of the most happening places in Bangkok
Sukhumvit street is busy with clothing vendors, food vendors and people selling a lot of souvenirs. The prices are very reasonable.

Sukhumvit area is busy and bustling all along the main Sukhumvit Road but you have only to wander down some of the smaller side streets to find quieter areas with maybe a nice restaurant or some greaqt street food to sample. 
The area is well served by public transort both the BTS and MRT and makes it an ideal base from which to explore the facinating city of Bangkok.
Two of the cities (in)famous entertainments areas are in Sukhumvit, Soi Cowboy and Nana Plaza. There are also numerous restaurants bars and hotels to suit all tastes and pockets as well as a huge selection of street food carts on every corner.
There is a night market that sets up each night along the main Sukhumvit Road (lower sois) from early evening selling a variety of items from clothes to sex aids and pretty much everything in between.
All in all a great place for the first time tourist to Bangkok to stay.
