There are also tons of places to eat and drink.  It is well serviced by the BTS.  Unless you really have to go there, avoid the areas between Soi 5 and Soi 19 - particularly in the afternoon.  There is a sidewalk market that is Rip-off Central.

There are many different shops and eating places. Street food is good and cheap while also has a Burger King,McDonalds,Ronneys New York Pitza, and Tacos. You name it there is a restrauant for everyone. Also shopping at different malls. Try street food because they buy fresh everyday. Also try coconut and drink the milk from inside then eat the white coconut itself. Jeff USA.

Most things run through Sukhumvit or just off it, the BTS (skytrain) Runs through Sukhumvit and you can get to many places.
There are Malls a plenty along the road, emquartier, emporium, Central Chidlom, Central World, Siam Paragon, MBK to name a few. There are also many many hotels and restaurants along Sukhumvit.. This is Bangkoks main road...

This is one of the best neighborhoods for tourists that appreciate the convenience of having many conveniences within walking distance. This district boasts vibrant street life paired with some of the best hotels in the city.

review_title: Not bad but didn't like the ex-pat vibe
review_text:
This is one of these areas where you will see a lot of older westerners with younger local ladies. No problem if they gain both by that but sometimes it's a bit frustrating.

Some nice bars where you can sit outside with very good beers. You just need to ignore the working girls if you're not into that sort of thing.

Nice malls and some good spots to eat.

Sukhumvit is still the heart of Bangkok, although several other areas of the city now vie for attention.  The street is lined with shops, pubs, restaurants, hotels, massage joints, and the like.  Hawkers crowd the sidewalks selling anything and everything, mostly at cheap prices, if you know how to bargain.  Young (and not-so-young) women, and men, also hawk personal services throughout the evening hours.  Non-stop, Sukhumvit never sleeps!

review_title: More Nightlife Than You Have Ever Seen
review_text: The night market is not to be believed. There are actually many, in other locations, but I visited this one. Keep your personal items close and your wits about you, but be prepared to see every nationality shopping or people watching. It Is "dense-pack" humans here, so if you come, you wil be in a sea of humanity. But it is fun, and if it is not available here, it does not exist!  :)

review_title: massive busy dirt road like something out of blade runner
review_text: massive long road full of girly bars and hotels. can get a bit clostrophobic but is a good experience. This part of bangkok is quite a way from the grand palace and khao san road.

review_title: This is the part of Bangkok you heard of
review_text: Whatever might be the reason you came to Bangkok, this is the place you want to stay. Safe , so secure, vibrant and joyful
Nice place, there is something for all tastes. Nightlife,relaxing,shopping and very accessible. From here out you can reach every part of Bangkok with ease.

The first time I went to Bangkok, I stayed in Sukhumvit. Since then, every time I have been to Bangkok, I had to be there. It has everything and access to rest of the Bangkok and to my favorite places is also very easy. No matter if I am on a business trip or a family vacation of my kid, I always prefer a few days in Sukhumvit to finish the trip.

It's got something for everybody- shopping,nightlife,bars,restaurants 
It's too crowded  with its traffic jams,sky train does offer you a way out,if you don't mind the crowds.

review_title: everything you want is on sukhumvit line
review_text:
the longest road in the world (goes up to Cambodia)  starting from the longest named city in the world (Bangkok thai name).
You'll find everything from super mega mall to expensive restaurant versus the inexpensive one, then you ll find the beach nearby around pattaya and then go on to the island the road of Sukhumvit is a bit like road 66 in US:)

This is a good central area to choose your hotel.

It's close to all the shopping malls, good street food or restaurants, and BTS or MRT stations. Yet, it's not in your face noise, booze etc.
After a hectic day trip, this is the place where you grab a beer and enjoy the nighlife. Lot's of bar and night clubs around. Streets do offer a certain bargain for shopping.

review_title: Very busy area but excellent shopping
review_text:
Was crowded have to be very very careful of your bag watch for pickpockets. 
One has to bargain. Go down to 50% and walk you way upwards
This place one of the busiest district in Bangkok downtown. You can find lot of interesting things just by take a stroll around.

The best to stay, shop, eat, night out, and many things more! If you find a place to stay in BKK, I highly recommend an accommodation in this area. It is central and close to many things you need, and makes life easier as it is linked with MRT/BTS stations like Phomprong, Nana, etc. It also somehow feels international as it is filled with a lot of western hotels, modern shopping centers and restaurants/bars. And you know what the best part is, all BKK cab drivers KNOW where it is, so hop on a cab, say an address in Sukhumvit, the driver nods and you can fall asleep in peace....zzz..zz. No long complicated discussion. No worries of getting lost. I always stay in this area in my countless visits to Bangkok.

I had the opportunity to stay with relatives both in 2014 and 2015 in the Sukhumvit area. Lots of hotels, lots of western oriented pubs, for those times when you want to feel like being back home. But also an abundance of Thai restaurants, massage parlours and the very upscale shopping centres, where the air-conditioning alone is worth walking throughout. The BTS sky train makes staying in Sukhumvit very feasible for further tourist activities, as virtually every major tourist location can be reached by a combination of BTS and subway. And yes, Soi Cowboy is worth a visit, especially at night, for the concentration of vibrant neon colours.

This is the area to stay in as it has a great nightlife and is extremely vibrant. This is where the bars,massage shops and where you will find a couple of Australian Bars. It is where you will find Nana Plaza and Soi Cowboy.

This is the most convienent place to stay in Bangkok if you want a little of everything. It's further from the cultural sites but a cheap taxi away from them. Linked to the city by BTS/MRT everything is a couple stops away.  It's got shopping, street food, nightlife, and much much more. I always stay near Sukhumvit area for the easy linking via skytrain/MRT and Airport link.

I stay on SOI 3 SUKHUMVIT  it is such a vibrant happening area with something for everyone , it s safe and has every thing you could ever need. The  SOI' S on SUKHUMVIT are long each one has its on thing going on .There are night stalls from the start of SOI1/2 up to SOI 15 sometimes further, around  SOI 4/5 after 11PM the stalls pack up and the impromtu bars arrive !  It is forever changing and upgrading "THE BANGKOK POST" reveals regularly  new installations and construction taking place . Sukhumvit is  TRULY  AMAZING THAILAND !

review_title: a busy road at night; not interesting during the day
review_text: It is fine to spend one or two hours walking around the area at night. You will see a lot of people from all over the world. But this is not a very interesting place.
Stayed here for 1 week and the area is really good with all the commodities you need to enjoy your stay.
with shopping mall, building, company, developed area. You can enjoy not many thing there, but there are a bunch of high-end expensive restaurant that you can go, several stars hotels and stuffs.

My hotel is in the Sukumvit area and it was great. Terminal 21 was across the street which is a fun shopping area with also lots of restaurants. Soi Cowboy was close by which was a funny experience in and of itself. It was very close to the skytrain, and lots of other things.

The rooms are clean and tidy , the staff are helpful , and the breakfast is wonderful, loads of choice , the location is a minute from the sky train, so very handy , just b warned, if you have sandels or flip flops on , u won't be allowed on the roof terrace , we had asked downstairs about dress , we were told it was casual , but as we got there , we met five people who were escorted from the roof trc, so we turned around and went else where , other wise , I would have given them , 5/5

review_title: everything you need in one area
review_text: in this area of bangkok you have everything from hotels, shopping, malls, restaraunts, and even one the red light district of bangkok. lots of bussiness everywhere and tons of street vendor trying to sell you anything you might need. the light rail train is a major part of this area and can get you from one end of town to another. at night some of the area become red light area like nana plaza and tons of girls come out to play, but during the day time it's just a great place to shop and take lots of great pics of the area.

The people at Inter-milan make this tailor a gem within the sukhumvit area. The choice of fabric is fantastic and of extremely high quality. The best aspect of Inter-milan Tailor is their commitment to your satisfaction of their product, they are extremely dedicated.

review_title: All the good and bad things are!
review_text: It is where you can find all the good and bad things in Bangkok. In the morning it is basically the good things but during the night, there's plenty of hookers around. The malls are good but in the night market it is where the adventures starts.
You can find all sort of activities by walking along the longest street in Bangkok. Very lively and never sleeps

review_title: Great for tourists. Endless things to do
review_text: Had the pleasure of staying at the Majestic Grande in Sukhumvit in December of 2014 and would go back in a heartbeat. Bustling, busy and very authentic, Sukhumvit offers everything you can imagine plus some which you can't. If you're in Bangkok, I'd highly suggest it as a place to camp for a week or two as it is central to everything. Happy travelling!

review_title: Good district for travellers in a small group
review_text: A nice district for solo travellers or small groups who want to avoid crowds found in Siam.  I usually shop at the Emporium (one of the sister malls as Siam Paragon...), it's smaller and quieter but easier to find what you are looking for in shopping.

During your visit to Bangkok its a must visit area for all types of tourists as the area bubbles with all kinds of activities. It comes to life especially evening when large number of make shift shops come up selling variety of stuff. Only deterrent is gets very crowded as the night progresses but still worth a visit.

We were based here during a 2 week break to Thailand and whilst the city is frenetic almost 24 hours a day, there are quiet area too and we found one of then near the lake, surrounded by greenery from our top (11th)  floor apartment.

We had a great coffee house (Kuppa) and restaurants nearby as well as countless neighbourhood shops to find all that we needed.  An excellent tailor too at Charlie's Designs where Rodger sorted me out with suits and shirts.  We'll come back to this area as it easily connected to the rest of the city by sky train, MRT and of course taxis of all kinds.

Sukhumvit has it all what all needed. Space for all kinds of men. Nice shops and night market. Street will be live till early morning. Its different world all together. Easy access through BTS sky train and MRT at Asok station. Can see heavy traffic at day time. Lot of street food available in all adjacent sois.

and it was never not interesting. The lifestyle is somewhat different to anything I have been use to in the past. Getting around this area is very easy with the sky train stopping every few minutes. There are many great places to see along this road so take your time and do this on a few occasions.

This is a great part of Bangkok and fairly central to other locations. It is very likely and I would recommend to those with an interest in the nightlife, less so to those looking for relaxation. The area has good transport links and many places are within walking distance.

Bangkok was hot and bustling traffic is mad makes m25 on Friday rush hour look like a country lane, but it was an amazing experience and the thai people are very friendly and welcoming. 
A must see is the floating market about an hour from bangkok,the markets are full of stalls and eating stalls atmosphere is buzzing, to avoid the traffic use the skytrain it's quick and easy to use, a great experience can't wait to go and explore more of thailand.

This area is a very busy place. There is lots of traffic but thankfully you can walk almost everywhere. There are also the scooter taxis and tuk tuks which can take you short distances for cheap. Public transportation is also readily available. There are so many things to do that you don't even need to leave the area. Very convenient. Next time I am in Bangkok I will be back in this area.

review_title: Massive traffic jams ruin the whole experience
review_text:
Bangkok is plagued by such perpetually bad traffic that it would literally RUIN your WHOLE holiday experience! I travel extensively, but have NEVER seen a city with such terrible jams!

I wanted to visit one of the "best new restaurants in Bangkok" for lunch as recommended by certain guide located at Sukhumvit.

According to their site, they opened for lunch from 12 noon to 2pm. After I boarded a cab from my hotel at 1130 and was told that the journey would take an hour.

Meow ever, I was caught in such a massive traffic jam under the sweltering heat that by the time I reached the lunch hour was closed!

review_title: Area is a little pricey but convenient
review_text: This is a good central location with easy access to public transport and close to shopping (a lot of shopping).

During the stay in Bangkok, I stayed 2 night in Sukhumvit area and had a great night seeing the attractions around here. In the morning, grab your breakfast from restaurants and cafe nearby, stroll around and you might find a garden. Sukhumvit Garden, very spacious and wide open, there are wooden chairs for people who came there to read a book or a short jog. The air was very fresh and clean. It is open almost every day until evening. Then, at night you can explore Soi area that transformed into night clubs and bar. Soi Cowboy is one of the famous among the plenty of them, be prepare to see pretty hosts on the road and some guys might offered you naked entertainment. Just be sure to keep yourself safe.

If you are planning to visit Bangkok for a few days for sightseeing and shopping, this is probably the best location. It has connections to both the BTS sky train, and also the MRT metro. The airport train line link is also only one stop away on the metro. In Bangkok this is very important as traffic is bad, trains are the best way to get around. Also there are shopping centers, restaurant and street markets all over. I prefer Siam for shopping and exploring but this is better for convenience.

From the streets and alleyways to the malls and bars, there always seems to be something happening. 

I stayed in Soi 18 which was a bit quieter than a few of the other streets we had been down but was within walking distance from all major shopping, bars and nightlife,  with many restaurants and massage parlours along the road and street food and beer available at every turn!

This area is in the center of activity. There are many restaurants, hotels shopping and pool halls. Pool is practically a national recreation. If you are by yourself, one of the pool girls will play with you. You are close to all forms of transportation with easy access. You are within walking distance to the famous Nana Plaza and Cowboy Plaza. There are many street food vendors with local fare. In the evening the street vendors come out and set up booths on the sidewalks. Although this also happens in other areas of Bangkok, this is the largest. You will find everything with these vendors. Just beware of the quality if you buy items of clothing. Everyone is very friendly and courteous. Highly recommended.

Awesome place very hot thou.love the night life, streets were busy with people shopping and enjoying.

interesting place

I stayed a few times in Sukhumvit, on Soi 11. This is really the center of attraction for food, shopping, and nightlife. The sidewalk food is really cheap and tasty. We took all our dinners from the sidewalk vendors. The shopping at Siam Paragon is a couple of kilometers away, easily walked through the Skywalk, on Sukhumvit road.
The hotels in Sukhumvit are not expensive at all, although centrally located.
I highly recommend a stay in the area.
The rumours about the police harrassment were unfounded, as I saw no police there during my whole stay.

If you are looking for a lively and vibrant area to stay then this is it.

My preferred Soi in Sukhumvit is Soi 11 as everything from restaurants, bars, hotels, BTS Monorail, massages, money changers, supermarkets etc are located in this Soi. 

At night street food stalls pop up and at the end of the Soi near Nana BTS are markets are selling a variety of items including clothing etc.

Two hotels Ive stayed at in this Soi are the Presidents Palace and Grand Swiss Hotel. Both are hotels I would be happy to recommend with the newly renovated Grand Swiss being my preferred choice.

At the end of Soi 11 at the opposite end of the street from the BTS is Fraser Suites which has an excellent rooftop Skybar called Above 11 which serves lovely Peruvian Food with fantastic views.

For those looking for adult entertainment then Soi Cowboy and Nana Plaza are a short walk away.

Bkk the best city....sukhumvit I m coming back :D
Had a great experience in Manhaton Hotel 
Soon hitting back to the street of sukhumvit..u will never get bore on these streets if u r looking for nightlife

The heart throb of the city. Has all the elements of a Hollywood pot boiler. There are so many Soi ( streets ) and each having different characteristics and ethnicity. You can find an Arab street, a little India, Koreans and even Japanese. A perfect blend of the globe resides here. 

Apart from that, the place is full of Hotels, malls, restaurants, pubs and night clubs.  Most of the famous tailoring houses of the city are here at Sukhumvit.

The place comes alive during the night. Streets become pubs, hawkers emerge from the back alleys and the atmosphere becomes electric.  This place is not for the faint hearted. The best of bangkok, is at Sukhumvit. Do not dare miss it.
Last time I was here was in 2007. Now 7 years later some places have changed, others are still there. Just enjoying walking through the streets and several site streets (soi's)

Sukumvit road is the main street of Bangkok.The street is full of food outlets cheap and expensive.Shopping cheap and ecpensive.Many hotels in the street.In the night the street turns into bars all along the street.

This area was recommended to us as a hot spot for tourism. It kind of is. This area is full of foreign foods, bars and hotels catered to international travelers to get a taste of home. It is difficult to find any local cuisine without paying a lot for it in this area, but if you have the budget for it, you can eat a food from a different culture every meal.

Sukhumvit, is the mainline that snakes its way through a canyon of glass and steel, its many Soi extend from it like tentacles.  
Sukhumvit, is many things. shopping malls, restaurants, massage parlours and clubs, but scratch past the tourist gloss, there is the underneath of Bangkok and beneath its surface, perhaps here in Sukhumvit, lyes her heart.
I can say this with complete truth as its where I left mine.
  
Above all Sukhumvit is the home to the daughters of Lilith, here she sings her siren song to fools every night. Her song can be heard from the go-go bars of the Cowboy Soi to Nana Plaza, her sweet voice lingers in the night time air above the comfort zone. 

Tonite on Soi 11, she sings Lucky Star, a song by the western madonna. 
Gathered in the courtyard of Rosabieng is a local Thai cover band, theirs is a fusion jazz version, a copy nonetheless not without its flaws. In the chorus they slip there L's and Lucky is now Rucky, my Rucky Star at night is over heard somewhere over the warm gasoline haze and neon lit streets.

The song floats somewhere above Sukhumvit Road, sealing the comfort zone in an invisible gift wrap. My Bangkok lucky hole soul hovers over Soi 11, below I see the beggars lay hidden on the sidewalk outside the Ambassador hotel, on the corner is the tandem scooter salesmen trying to sell off fake Omega sports watches hopelessly in vain. On the opposite side of the street is a slew of Tuk Tuk drivers laughing from their last scheme, waiting to ply their 50 baht round trip magic, their so called "take you anywhere" offer to suckers.  

Then I see Naht, the store manager of the tailors called Manhattans. He leans against a car  outside his storefront. Naht looking ever so pleased with himself, his smile so wide as he speaks into a cell phone, then his smile bursts, and the air is ringed with the laughter of the slightly 'funny' deal.
Nahts smile, his head full of white porcelain teeth crossfades into the white ceiling lights of dark smoky lit club off the Cowboy Soi where a girl, whose name I'll never know, dances lost in the backbeat of a song, she swings from one pole to another, wandering aimlessly, and as an after thought she wonders about Tomorrow. And the bold helvetica typeface of Tomorrow floats away into the night sky, the font lines becoming a projection of stars that burn into bright silver studs and arrange themselves upon a netted crop top worn by a girl singing on the main concourse at Nana BTS. 

The well beaten jungle paths of Sukhumvit are alive tonite,the streets around Soi 11 seem to glow. Night time neon fluorescents are replaced by a series dark blues and machinery greys, painted so closely together, they smudge as streaks that terminate into potholes chiselled into the road surface where glassy puddles hold the reflection of light, and bounce faint highlights upon edges of street architecture before fading into muted blacks of shadow.

Where are you now my Suzy, my sweet allure, my Primavera deluxxxe, my gynoid lover.
Say it, say "I love you", yeah tell me that baby. Tell me all those pretty lies I want to hear. Now say my name.
Call me Ignatz, or Call me Case, No. Call me Jacky Paper, i'm from the Autumn Mists don't you know, no wait,  I'm Corky Laredo,  I'm Clifford Baines, I'm John West and Brady Walsh.
I'm all of these names, these men, we lost boys, from the city of Enoch, on our way to Cythera flying high on an aero zeppelin above Nana.

It's in that burnt sienna skyline where I hover, somewhere over the rainbow of gasoline haze and neon lights.
You're my lucky star,  my Primavera, My Suzy, My Madonna, our patron saint for the daughters of Lillith,
My lucky star, so bright, help me navigate my way home to the western lands,
Here in my dreams I'll still linger on with you, our sweet simulacra love.
But tonight, Tonight, I'll use that, press once and be promised the sweetest of dreams, of an even better Tommorrow.

Sweet dreams from Sukhumvit honey bee, my baby doll, my Miss Nana 2015.

review_title: This is THE Bangkok! Enjoy ethnic town along the Rd.
review_text:
Sukhumvit area is the most busiest, livliest area in Bangkok. It is about BMT Chit Lom to Thong Lo. 
Sukhumvit Rd. is the main road which is full of shopping buidlings, hotels, massage shops, open air food stands and taylor shops. It is fun that you walk along the road and you'll come accross certain ethnic town. For example, around Soi 12 is Korean town and around Nana Sta. is little India where there are many taylors. Soi 33 is Japan town, Soi 3/1 is Arab town. Full of excitement and energy! You can't miss here!

Sukhumvit was a good attraction for us and it is full of life. Some of the major grand malls, street markets, Night life and hotels are at Sukhumvit area. Choosing hotels at this area will be best as most of them are within budget. I was also staying close to this area only and saved a lot of travel time and money too. People who are staying far away from this area have to struggle to reach here on a regular basis since all major attractions i.e. malls and markers are here only. Street food is awesome and quite hygienic here comparative to any other place. 

Main attraction on sukhumvit road is on Road no. 4 and Road no. 3 (i.e. Soi 3 & Soi 4), visiting street markets is worth. 

A must visit place and I'll advise to choose your hotel at this area or nearby only.

I have been bangkok ,for say  over 35 times and allways stayed there from 1 week to as long as 5 weeks,so for people who reads my reviews that is the reason I have experienced in sukumvit
The road is long,and what can one say massage places ,hotels MBK shopping .siam,
Terminal 21, home pro hardware electronis ,tools,ect.
Sukumvit road long and as you walk from Siam end to Asoka pluss there are markets,Indian where
They go,banks,australian visa centre near asoke.
Skytrain right along the road,very busy try not crossing unless by using skytrain steps,
There cheap hotels along ok for short stays ( I don't like them)
So much to see Iff look at the side streets,sleazy bars ,ect.
For any men walking around at get girls offering themselves,some off these set up by police.
I never have experienced only was told ,I am married,
Iff u want to go to places quickly by yourself,get a Motor bike taxi,
Remember allways ask a taxi for turning on the meter,unless deal done before.
Motor bike taxi s usuall 20 baht to 40 baht.
What can I say ,walk the length and you will experience bangkok.
Don't trust foreigners offering anything.,there are many con s
Every part has something different,
Night time a lot off street stalls.

review_title: Center of attraction for all the tourist
review_text: Sukhumvit is the main attraction for most of the tourist. all the major happening is on Sukhumvit area. Like Malls, Hotels, Nightlife etc. There are main hotels in the Sukhumvit and most of them are within budget. Main attraction on sukhumvit road is on Road no. 4 and Road no. 3 (i.e. Soi 3 & Soi 4)

We stayed in a boutique hotel ON8 (see my review) the location is awesome!
This is the life of the party area, shopping, food, nightlife!
WARNING their is an adult area to this street which is a hoot to check out- even if your not into that sort of thing!
Also Thai massage - legit- is very available at about 500baht or 15 US for 2 hours awesome!!!!

review_title: A Long Stretch of Road Serviced by the BTS.
review_text: Sukhumvit actually starts from the intersection of Rachaprasong onward.  This long stretch of road cut through a huge residential area all the way to Pak Nam, where the Chao Phya River meet the Gulf of Thailand.  Therefore the mere mentioning of 'Sukhumvit' can be confusing.  In order to be precise, let limit Sukhumvit to be between Rachaprasong to Prompong.  The distance of few kilometer covers several department stores, hotels, embassies, and many many restaurants.  You can take the BTS from Siam Square Station and get off Prompong which will give you a good idea of the street below.

Sukhumvit is huge, so some sections are bound to be less interesting than others. Shopping on the street pavements begins in the evening and lasts all night. You can get everything from clothes to sex toys, though the quality is debatable and the prices are not super affordable, do not expect super cheap stuff nad expect a lot of bargaining. Prostitutes are everywhere. Massage parlours as well. Walking in the night is a bit difficult with all the stalls and people. Walking in the early morning will get you to many food stalls. Local people buy their breakfast there and it smells really great. If you are brave, you should try, as it looks delicious. You can easily go anywhere you want because the train stations are along this road. An interesting street to walk along even on Sunday, when it is empty... only, you get stopped to many times and have to defend from pushy taxi drivers and tuk tuk drivers and all sorts of locals who claim to have the thing you need.. Most locals will not speak good English (except, of course, those in the real shops, cafes, hotels), but it is possible to get to some kind of understanding.
When the sun goes down the streets come to life. Anything is for sale on these streets. From t shirts to oral jelly. What is that. Exciting to see for sure

review_title: The skytrain will take you right to Sukhumvit
review_text: There are many districts of Bangkok that are just mental! Sukhumvit is definitely one of them. Full of bars, shops, quirky stalls, crazy traffic, there is plenty to see and do. I recommend a visit both during the day and at night so you can see the contrast between day and night life.

If you are an expat needing a dose of western food, culture, and people, this is the place to be.  No matter what time of the day there are offerings of food, clothing, and people watching.  There is a mix of Thai food, the street vendors selling roasted chicken are particularly good, and Thai street food is some of the safest in Asia.

review_title: one of the longest boulevards in the world
review_text:
Sukhumvit Road is not only one of the longest boulevards in Thailand, but one of the longest boulevards in the world. Probably the most popular locality among foreign tourists and expats and home to many luxury hotels , fancy apartments, restaurants , pubs and nightclubs and shopping centers .     The pavement of Sukhumvit itself has become a huge market carrying everything from luggage to fake Rolex watches, and after midnight they turn into roadside bars and pubs. Sukhumvit offers the best dining in Bangkok, from five-star dining to street stands, the variety of choices and tastes are almost overwhelming.
Perennial traffic jams is the main flip side

Good parts and bad parts.

The good -

Location - In the heart of this metropolis and bustling city and is good for access to anywhere in the city with loads of Sky train stations on this road.

Hotels - Many fair priced hotels to choose from with good clean facilities.

Women - Absolutely stunning and beautiful and classy

The Bad -

Prostitutes everywhere - Good or bad depending on how you see it and are.

Westernized - Again hit or miss depending on you, this is a very very modern westernized city, if your looking for local culture this isn't the right part of town.

Locals - Majority i came across are rude and don't speak no English

Taxis - Taxi drivers and Motorbike taxis do not know directions at all unless it is very close to where you are, amount of times the taxis asked me for directions and took me to the wrong places in particular the motorbike taxis not to mention they did not speak any English and again were rude.

Food - Barely any street food down the sois majority of it is fast food chains and restaurants.

Traffic - Absolutely chockablock you cannot move or even cross the road majority of the time for periods of up to 10 minutes.

Good place to get your comfort food or take in a movie
 Lots of western franchises hete anf entertainment. 
Not very traditional and if you miss home you csn hang here

This Are is Well Known For Street Of business. It Therefore, Always Busy All Times. Traffic Jams, Especially Friday. Please Try To Avoid Using Private CarDuring That Day. Besides, The Busy Life, There Are Many Malls, Pubs And Bars. Many Great Hotel For Staying. All is Good Except Traffic. So Bad Ever

More convenient in Sukhumvit , Hotel, nightclub, near shopping mall such as MBK and Terminal 21, health land for massage
BTS, MRT

Sukhumvit is the heart of Bangkok. Full of traffic, it is a long road that passes through the entertainment, commercial and residential centres of Bangkok. Traffic jams on this road seem to have no beginning and no end!

Sukhumvit street is the busiest street in Bangkok. It has plenty of 5 stars hotels, clubs, shops, and the biggest shopping centers. The Sky Train is moving in the median of the street connecting all hotels, shopping malls, and all attractions in the area. When you live in this street you will have access to everything in Bangkok.

review_title: Popular area to stay in Bangkok
review_text:
The madness of the traffic, the business of the streets, it's a street that never sleeps! Ad the area around Sukhumvit road is no exception.

Filled with bars, restaurants and shopping, there's loads to see. If your into girly bars and the sleezier side of Bangkok, then look no further!

For me, I found the sleepy side of things a bit off putting, but the other restaurants and shopping and just the life in the streets overshadowed the darker side.

I love the street side pop up restaurants, this is street food at it's best!

There is also  plenty of shopping to be had at one of the many shopping centres in the area.

Served by both metro and sky rail, it's easy to get to. Head along to Sukhumvit, you'll see Bangkok at it's liveliest!

review_title: Always something to do in Sukhumvit
review_text:
If it is shopping, bars, eating or just people watching, there is always something to do down the Sukhumvit road area.
There is also some very good budget and some more upmarket accommodation in the area to suit all pockets. 
I am a people watcher so can easily amuse myself all day just watching the passing parade of locals and tourists while sipping on a SangSom or one of the many beers on offer.
If you need to travel anywhere in Bangkok from here, it is as easy as jumping on the rail network and you are there in no time as it is very easy to negotiate.
My tip for moving around Sukhumvit is to use all the sky train overpasses for moving up and down the road on foot.

review_title: Never miss this to visit..in Bangkok
review_text: If you are in Bangkok...Sukhumvit is the best place to visit. well commercially developed, all major 5 star hotel, cafe, custom tailors,    everything...but this are is little expensive.

review_title: Sukhumvit is a vibrant area for tourists
review_text: Sukhumvit road is a vibrant mix of shopping, nightlife, restaurants and tourists spots. The whole stretch has many things and is a must for every tourists to visit this area. It has so many good restaurants, lots of places of nightlife to enjoy, huge shopping malls, street shopping and lots of entertainment.

This area is a bit removed (but not too far) from the hustle of downtown Bangkok.  There is plenty to do in the area such as shops and restaurants and the SkyTrain runs literally right down the road.  I recommend the Marriott Suk if you stay in this area.

review_title: Hustle, bustle, Busy Bangkok it's all here in Sukhumvit
review_text: Sukhumvit is one of the longest roads in BKK and the name given to the area along it.  I have stayed riverside and around Siam as well as Sukhumvit, and I keep coming back to Sukhumvit.  Easily access many other parts of BKK using the sky train which follows above the road, avoiding the chaotic traffic.  The most common tourist area BTS stations are Asok, Nana with many hotels clustered around these stations within a 10 minute walk.  From 5 to 3 star, I have stayed in both and if you plan to spend most time out and about, take a 3-3.5 star chain hotel at a cheap price with breakfast included.  Spend the $100 you will save per night on dinner, transport and shopping.

review_title: Best place to stay in Bangkok!
review_text:
If you are looking for an entertaining place to stay while in Bangkok, look no further! We stayed there again this past month while on a 3 day trip to Bangkok and yet again, we were not disappointment! We chose to stay at The Dream hotel which is conveniently located on the heart of Sukhumvit and walking distance to the train stop.

If you are looking for nightlife and adult entertainment, then look no further then this area. Located right in between Soi Cowboy (featured in the movie The Hangover) with all the bars on each side of this narrow iconic street and Nana, the other adult nightlife and entertainment area. This is definitely a must place to stay while in Bangkok if you are looking for nightlife fun!
Centeral part of bangkok you can find a lot of atractions street shopping and shopping malls you must visit this district
plenty of hotels and restaurants. Each of its own class and quality. From indian to italian, all sorts of food available. had a lovely stay here.

In my opinion, Sukhumvit Bangkok is the liveliest street i have visited in Bangkok. it has something for everyone. First of all, reaching on this street is easy with MRT. Next the best of hotels of all ranges, from budget to 5 star, are on this street. This street houses best of restaurants from all the world, you get Indian food, Arab food, contentinental food, and of course Thai food. Street food is also excellent and quite affordable.  the bars are all over.. from exotic to street bars even on cycles.
Nana plaza(at Nana MRT)  & Soi Cowboy(at Asoka MRT) are a must visit... these place rock at night.
there are ok to excellent massage centers at every corner... (however i found ones at Khao-san road better in quality of massage(bus number 3 from Sukhumvit to Khao-San road - 10 bath ticket)
Best of shopping malls- on this street - Lotus, MBK and others.....
The street is awake right from 6am in the morning untill 4am in the night.  it is at it's best between 9pm and 2am, then it starts slowing down a bit.....
the transportation- taxis, tuk-tuks, busses and MRT are available.  however one should avoid travel by taxi or busses between 7pm and 10pm- walking may be faster during these hours. of course MRT is the fastest mode at any time of the day.
i stayed at Hotel Ambasador near Nana MRT station.. a good central place at waking distance from most happening areas...
i would recommend to stay at Sukhumvit during stay at Bangkok and you will not be disapointed.
Nice area to visit enjoyed the different bars and cafés here! And the night markets! Well worth a visit slightly less tourists more locals in this area! Different to khao San road!

It makes me very sad to see that tourism in Thailand is suffering so badly due to the recent coup. 
I  have been living in Bangkok and using Thailand as a base for the last 7 years whilst traveling to various parts of the world for my job. I have never felt more safe and cannot understand why tourists are not coming as much these days.
I think it is bad news that most countries have issued negative travel advisories when the facts say that Thailand is still a very safe holiday destination. Local people and businesses are suffering needlessly.
Please pass the word that there is no reason to stay away.
It's business as usual.

review_title: Thailand food and crafts in one nice package
review_text: The Icon Siam mall is a larger, newer mall in Bangkok.  The highlight, for us, is the huge Sook Siam area.  This gigantic area has food stands and shops selling all manner of things from different regions of Thailand.  It was super fun to see floating markets, food stalls, etc. along the lines of what we saw across the region in one place.  Sook Siam is just great if you are looking for some final items to take home in a well designed area.  Be forewarned--it can get busy.  A nice complement to a visit to Bangkok.  Overall, if you are considering going..just do it!  It is a nice break from the heat and traffic too.

review_title: Nice cultural experience, lovely setting but always very busy and overcrowded.
review_text: SookSiam is a curated cultural destination that brings together the finest treasures from all 77 provinces of Thailand. Here, rare regional cuisines, artisanal handicrafts, and traditional folk arts come together in a harmonious celebration of Thai heritage. You are taken on a journey through the essence of Thailand’s four regions, all within one extraordinary space. Located on the ground floor of ICONSIAM mall. Always really busy. Lovely decor. Nice atmosphere but just way too crowded. A lot of the seating area is communal that is shared between the different food outlets. It isn't very clean. Food is cheap but not the best quality. I like the shopping and atmosphere, but food not so much. It is better to go to an actual restaurant rather than one of the street food stalls.

review_title: Enjoy the food stall in a comfortable space
review_text:
Experience floating markets and food stalls in a hygienic, air-conditioned and comfortable space. Local food stalls such as meat and seafood skewers, pattai, khao mangai, and roti are crowded. Payment is made in cash on the spot.
It had just opened, so I could eat it comfortably.

Bangkok has lots of huge malls. Our favorite is probably the "Siam Paragon" with a great food court and lots of regular restaurants.

This one here is a bit outside of the center, nicely located at the river. Looks great when it gets dark. Also very easy accessible by boat.

We didn't come to shop, but to grab a late lunch. And of course all the food stalls and restaurants are open through here. As most places do.

The food court is really a feast for your eyes. It's made to look like a bustling night market - but indoors. And indeed it looks amazing. The atmosphere is great. And it's a busy place, but fun.

There are plenty of small restaurant with seating. If that's not what you look for, then there are dozens of stalls selling all kinds of thai and street food. Lots of tables to sit down around the stalls. Even a stage where some musicians perform. We were lucky to get some Live music that afternoon.

Any kind of typical Thai food can be found here. Be it smaller snacks like thai sausages and other nibbles or real dishes like Pad Thai or delicous Kra Pao. If you like sweets and desserts then there's lots of choice as well too. You can also take-out some dishes or buy some snacks and products to bring home. There's something for every budget.

We bought some fried chicken, Som Tum and Larb Gai. All dishes were really nicely cooked. Som Tum was crunchy and fresh, the Larb Gai nice and spicy as it should.

It was nice, sitting there enjoying the food and listening to some live music. Even though very busy it was a nice and lively atmosphere.

review_title: All you can eat and shop under one roof.
review_text:
All of Thailand under one glittering roof!
Forget Chatuchak and the humidity and crowds. Forget Central World and its over charging. 
Come across the river to this wonderland where Alipay reigns supreme to cater to the China crowds today.
A very beautiful area in ICONSIAM mall. Here you will find several important cultural areas of Thailand represented, with their traditional food and handicrafts.

SookSiam, part of the larger IconSiam shopping mall complex, offers an overwhelmingly vast array of dining and boutiques representing cultures Thai, Asian, and even global. It’s a perfect place to go if it’s raining or you’d like to escape the heat and traffic of Bangkok. Prices may be higher here than in a more natural market, considering it’s near a luxury mall, but it’s so convenient that cost didn’t matter to me.

review_title: Excellent food court inside the Iconsiam shopping center
review_text:
Sooksiam is located on the ground floor of the beautiful Iconsiam shopping center. 
It is a reconstruction of typical Thai food markets, offering many specialties at prices close to those of real markets. 
We for example took dumplings egg + pork and shrimp + pork and then a mix of Takoyaki: octopus, shrimp and cheese. 
All pretty good.
Find tables to eat on the mezzanine floor, they are usually quieter. 
Recommended especially to see how the Thai setting has been recreated
With 2 beers we spent 430 baht (about 11 euros).

We came for the New Year 2025 Countdown and was surprised as we enter Sooksiam and experienced the magical taste of Thai food.  Lots of food shop offer a wide variety of Thai delicacies and souvenirs.  We enjoyed our Food Trip and tried exotic and non-exotic food.

review_title: A fantastic night market within a shopping mall.
review_text:
If you want to experience all the colors and tastes of Bangkok's night markets without having to stroll through the sleazy, sweaty, noisy and smelly actual night markets - look no further.
This giant market serves as a very modernized night market with all the features of the actual night markets (good food), minus all the negatives aspects of the markets. And it's all air-conditioned.

Located within IconSiam Sook Siam is a themed area that is both fun and great to drop by.  There are good food and souvenir collection for tourists. at competitive pricing!
It was originally conceived as a place to present the cultural heritage of each of the 4 main geographic regions 77 provinces of Thailand, sharing the stories of their unique culture, traditions, arts, handicrafts etc.  Within SookSiam there are over 3,000 stalls so we can imagine how massive and much time one would be engaged here!  Still most tourists enjoyed including ourselves. 
We were here using the ferry service on a Sunday morning.  Just for the F&B we try out Mango Sticky Rice which is a most popular Thai dessert, the one we got has 3 scoops of sticky rice each with different color but not difference in flavor. The mangoes slices are sweet!  There’s also the Moo Ping (Grilled Pork Skewers), tender, juicy, and rightly sweet.  Besides the stalls the props are also well setup, there’s bridges & pavilions and a clear dining area.
We saw that there are as much tourist as locals whom visited the place as us on a Sunday morning.  While the food at SookSiam may not be the best versions of each type it’s still a good introduction to Thai street food for those who are less initiated. 
Not to be missed!

The market is nice but culinary mediocre and comparatively expensive. There are all classics but a lot on a convenience basis. The shopping center itself is great, it offers every conceivable luxury, the rooftop bar upstairs is the smash. Since it is next to our hotel we are often there and could test a lot.
Great food and souvenir collection for tourists. The space simulates an outdoor Bangkok night market. Beautiful layout, mouthwatering food, and clean. I will come back.

The scenery is beautiful and the food is delicious
The zoo is fun
Elephants, giraffes are all cute
I want to come again next time I have the chance!
The hotel is also very comfortable 
Extremely good
Awesome praise!

review_title: A food mall but not authentic.
review_text: If you like to try out different foods of Bangkok, then this is a good place to go. You will find many different foods here. The prices might be higher than what you can find outside since this place is really a shopping mall and not authentic at all.

Pros
Clean and aesthetic overall
Most payment can be done cashless via QR
Mix between local goods and food from different regions
Air Conditioned
HALAL food available and the choice is decent - recommended for muslims

review_title: There is something for all tastes and budgets
review_text: The downstairs is full of food, divided into sections, it has 6 floors and there are everything from new brands to the largest, they even sell cars on the upper floors.

I like it because it feels like a night market. There are so many delicious things to eat and there are restaurants recommended by Baek Jong-won, so you can find them easily.

It was much better than walking outside in the heat.

review_title: Good places to visit in Bangkok, Thailand
review_text: Multicultural and a great place to visit! Authentic local snacks (hot and sour rice noodle soup, crispy pork with Thai sauce), and traditional Thai milk tea in a vat for $95🥤 (Tea aroma ~ it is an authentic Thai milk tea ⋯ delicious ⋯ highly recommended) ⋯Amazing and admirable! NaRaYa (NaRaYa) store has many styles, including the 2024 new amoeba and rose totem hats ~ bright and colorful!

review_title: Sensory overload in the best ways
review_text: A recreated street market that is done so well it feels authentic. It's definitely a sensory overload, the colors, the aromas and the delicious food. It's all wonderful n visually stunning. Loved it all n definitely a must visit

review_title: Street Food inside Icon Siam Shopping Centre
review_text: There are too many dining options to choose from. Every restaurant/stall was busy. All our meals were very good. Hard to not find a decent meal. Service was good and prices were reasonable.

review_title: The interior of the store is decorated with vibrant ornaments.
review_text:
SOOK SIAM
ICON SIAM's ground floor
Decorations inspired by Thai tourist destinations
The entire floor has a concept
The excitement of an adventure
Inspires tourists
Bangkok's leading shopping mall
Souvenir shops and various food shops
A spot you won't get bored of even if you spend the whole day looking around
An experience for everyone. Whatever the weather, the souk has a great atmosphere and lots of street food stalls. It's a good and harmless way to get in before visiting Chinatown.
Hugh variety of food to try, fun wandering around. Food prices were great and food very tasty. Well worth experiencing.

Indoor floating market, good atmosphere, not hot weather, quite a lot of tourists, delicious food, variety, not too expensive.
There are shows that are fun to watch.

review_title: Although it imitates a floating market, the prices are at the same level as a luxury shopping mall.
review_text:
This is a commercial complex that opened in 2018 on the left bank of the Chao Phraya River. There is a pond-like space on the first floor with small boats floating on it, resembling a floating market. Of course, the sales floor is on land, and the prices are the same as those of a high-end commercial facility.
To get there, take a direct boat from Sathorn Pier. Some guidebooks say it's free, but currently the boat fare is 10 baht.

Located on the ground floor of the massive upscale Iconsiam, this place is a food market paradise with the ground floor packed with all kind of good food cuisine to the brim.
We had a hard time trying to find a few food stores to try to avoid overfilling ourselves, but to no avail simply because the choices are endless!
This is an overrated food court. There is no floating market but rather just a design. The food is not good and twice the price vs the food courts of other malls.

This place very awesome, cause u can try all traditional food of Bangkok. You also can find traditional craft Bangkok. In This place also we can find the famous restaurant at Singapore like Boon Tong Kee, Jumbo Seafood ect. 

They also have all famous Brands like LV, Gucci, ect.

I love to shooping here and trying every food.

review_title: A bit artificial, but quite nice
review_text: The sook try to imitate the atmosphere of the night and street markets of Bangkok. It is reachable via hotel shuttle boat or Sathom ICONSIAM shuttle. For those, who do not want to discover a night market or take street food from local sources, this is a good alternative. While we can not comment on the shops located here,  we took a few bites here. Especially the oyster omelet was excellent.

review_title: Signature Thai Food in Indoor Floating Market
review_text: Awesome Indoor Floating Market for beginners to Thai signature street food. Hygienic and safe environment for tourists first time to Bangkok.

We came here for dinner & absolutely loved the vibe from all the locals. 
I would say a must visit place for hawker food in Bangkok.
Clean, air conditioned & very reasonably priced for being inside a 5 star shopping mall.

Amazing array of different cuisines located in themed venues. Looks upmarket and tasteful, not tacky. Yet the prices were very reasonable, some equivalent to street stalls.  A place you must visit when in Bangkok.

Sook Siam is the food level of the IconSiam mall. The latter is a highend mall. Sook Siam is beautifully renovated with many food vendors. This mall can also be accessed by ferry on the Chao Phraya River.

We visited icon siam in 2019 for the first  time and was enthralled...unfortunately  this time in Aug 2022 it reminded  me of congested  smelly railway station...it's  actual essence and beauty lost.....
 Only filled with food kiosks  and nothing  else...is Thailand  only this???...one more thing  it was raining  and the mall staffs managing the hotel  boat pier were ..inefficient  unhelpful  and rude..
Not expected...

review_title: Great Street Food style food court.
review_text:
I visited the ICONSIAM shopping centre specifically for the Sook street food style eatery, which I found to be amazing 👍
The rest of the shopping centre is Lavish.
The free river shuttle runs from Sathorn Pier below Taksin BTS skytrain station.
The activity on the river is also great to see 😊

review_title: Great representation of Thailand and Thai culture
review_text: A showcase of Thai culture. They have water with  boats like on a floating market. They made this a great representation of Thailand and Thai culture. I love spending time here. I especially recommend getting som coconut ice cream, it was delicious.

On a rainy day we decided to take the free ferry from central pier over to Iconsiam Mall. You will find at ground level the Sook. A real attempt at making the Sook with the right atmosphere. Loads to eat very safe lots to buy. We spent over 2 hours just wandering around. Worth a look.

review_title: Safe place to see some tourist culture
review_text: Sample "safe" street food, pick up some handmade souvenirs and just enjoy the mock "indoor floating market". Really nice place to spend an evening with young children. Not necessarily a must see but an easy place to visit with children.

This is inside Iconsiam, interesting, clean and lots of stalls to choose from. We had boat noodles, and some other food, it's not as cheap as a FM but you get AC, clean and you're in a mall, so it's fair.

We visited the mall in December 2019 and the Sooksiam floor was amazing , nothing like this in South Africa.

We had several bowls of coconut icecream that my daughter loved.

We had bubble tea and a few other items available.

The restaurant that stood out for me was in the Isaan region at the main entrance opposite 7/11 .

We had one of the stewed pork dishes and it was fantastic as well as some basil chicken and crispy pork- all good

The rest of Sooksiam was very interesting to visit really worth a visit.We found this more interesting than the rest of the mall.

Enjoy
Lovely space to eat and relax. Ground floor of iconsiam . Old style thai music and atmosphere . Excellent rainy day stop or mid river cruise day .

Stalls n stalls of the best in Iconsiam.
Food centres of all the huge malls are playing catching up now.

From trotters rice, fried chicken/porks, noodles of all types, fruit juices, snacks - sweet n savoury, you can find top quality offerings here. At very reasonable prices compared to Air Con mall Food courts.

It's a must visit if you want try Thai cuisines but don't wanna spend restaurant prices. Highly recommend a visit.
If you are visitors to Thailand and love Thai tastes, don’t miss to get to here. This’s in my opinion the Thai food paradise. Even you’re Thai you’ll live this 25,000 sq.m food plaza

Not sure if they meant souk and misspelt. 😂

At the basement of icon siam and interesting marketplace ambience

review_title: Indoor floating market that you shouldn't miss.
review_text: Sook Siam is an indoor floating market that I enjoy a lot. Away from Bangkok heat. It may not be authentic but fun to visit.

review_title: A kaleidoscope of color, food, and fun
review_text: Sook Siam, affectionately known as Bangkok’s ‘indoor floating market’ is a amazing architectural wonder as well as a fun place to appreciate Thailand’s local cultural heritage, food, and crafts all under one roof in the comfort of a gigantic air conditioned hall.  Human description can hardly do justice to this visual wonderand it’s best experienced in person. Best of all there is no admission fee. While on the surface this might sound a little artificial, there is nothing artificial about the authenticity of the food, snacks and crafts sold here. Vendors were carefully hand picked out of thousands from various floating markets and provinces all over Thailand to set up shop here - in short they are the best of the best. The street food sold here were truly authentic and unparalleled, the crafts were beautiful and pleasing to the eye. Prices of goods were relatively higher than those in actual markets, but not by a lot as the organizers do a great job in keeping them fairly low.  Alsi bring cash (Bhat) as quite a few stalls and vendors do not accept credit cards. Be prepared to spend at least a couple hours here and it should be done in a unhurried manner. Sook Siam should be a priority on every itinerary.

We happened upon this on accident. We did a cocktail cruise that left from the pier in front of the mall this is in. It’s very picturesque and has some cute shops a big food court. But hardly a destination of its own.

Sook Siam is a part of the new shopping centre barely 1 year into operation next to Hilton Millennium: the IconSiam.  This section of the shopping centre has a lot of wonderful local food, snacks, fruits, handcraft products, soaps etc.  Must try the mango sticky rice for 100 bhat.

I visited there many times. It’s part of the Icon Siam and located at ground floor. I love many authentic foodstuffs,restaurants and souvenirs there. All areas were lovely and attractive to all guests. It’s one of the must visit place in Bangkok as well.

review_title: They've got it all for you
review_text:
A must-visit place in Bangkok, personally I felt the Thai festival atmosphere upon entering the place. I enjoyed the concept where they showcase the four regions of Thailand. You can enjoy the thai heritages such as thai food, arts and handicrafts etc. 

I personally enjoyed eating the chicken rice and seafood soup near by the man-made pond. 

I highly recommend to visit SookSiam ( Iconsiam) if you are in Bangkok. two thumbs up!

review_title: Great place to relax and enjoy Asian cuisine
review_text: Mini Asian city on the lower level of a shopping hall. What more could you want? Great place to get away from the heat in bangkok. Prices are high for street food but it's not really Street food. Hygiene standards are far greater and that's what your paying for. Getting there Couldn't be easier, from the BTS take the free ferry to the door.

review_title: Feast for the eyes and the palate
review_text: Entering the place gives you an exciting vibe. It allows one to experience different regions all in one place. Good for tourists who cannot go around entire Thailand. Cool to see the vendors' traditional wear and small boats by the water pond. There is a wide array of food choices, you will need to come back another day to try other food. One can eat street food without having to worry so much. Prices are affordable and there is so much variety. It may be hard to find a table to eat. I hope they could add more tables and seats. My squad enjoyed the all-natural shops selling soaps and shampoos.

Even on a rainy day nothing else to do I would not go back there , we visited Sook Siam by taxi and returned via free boat and BTS  just shops all expensive and a kind of food hall on the ground floor

This place is better than Talad Neon for food by a long shot and just as competitive as the Train night market for food. 

What’s great about this place is you can explore it from 10am and starting eating towards 12pm and still keep exploring after that. You avoid the crowds and can take a picture of the nice things without jostling.

Foods represent all parts of Thailand just about. Plenty of spicy or non spicy at a very reasonable and good price. A bargain for eating in air cond. seats are hard to come by but all over the place so you need to be strategic. 

The kids will enjoy checking out the different things depending on if they have concentration span. We easily spent 4 hours there in the foodcourt sook siam. The rest of the mall we didn’t stop for. 

Catch sky train to Sapan Taksin about 50 Baht from Phaya Thai. Walk down to the Sathorn pier and catch the 8 min free ferry to the Iconsiam building. Sook siam is the same level as the pier entry and is the best way to enter Sook Siam.

review_title: No risk Street Food in Airconditioned comfort.
review_text:
Although ICONSIAM is a massive plaza packed with many unaffordable elite fashion brand, the food court on the ground level is amazingly good value.

Made up to look like a floating market, there are 100's of "street food" vendors all offering just about anything you want in a hyper clean Airconditioned environment.

You could come here daily for months and not try it all.

On the top level, there are also al a carte restaurants, but be prepared to pay double to triple pride for simple fare. 

Certainly one of the best mall food courts I have been to, although there is only minimal table seating available so if you come, grab a table first, then venture out to get your food.

review_title: Gone crazy with fabulous varieties of local foods
review_text: You can get any type of Thai local foods in this foods bazaar. We can’t managed test out everything. Definitely will go back when I visit Bangkok again n You will love it.

review_title: Went with low expectations but was impressed
review_text:
We only really visited out of curiosity and because the free boat shuttle from Sathon pier made it easy to get to. I was expecting a tourist trap or the usual Thai mall food court. It's actually somewhere in between - but it works.

The 'staging' isn't too over the top and they do manage to get away with people selling noodles in boats and others in full traditional dress. A bit odd but even the Thais seem to enjoy the novelty.

What I really liked was the regional focus. Most of the stalls have food from specific parts of Thailand and this is displayed in English alongside the name. A lot of people are so used to seeing a standard core of Thai dishes on all menus that this can be quite educational.

The choice is also huge and while some thing were obviously to be avoided it was quite difficult working out what to eat. We settled for some very good chicken rice (khao man gai), a selection of spicy southern curries over rice, and some mango and sticky rice. All reasonably priced at between 50 and 100 baht each.

The scale, the vision of showcasing all the provinces in this wonderful country called Thailand, it’s just wonderful. So much to see, so much to love.

We spent a large part of our time at Icon Siam at the Sook. Food haven, food heaven. There is something for everyone. 

This is the place to impress your oversea guests. This is the place to be proud of if you are Thai. A new benchmark has been created in this place.

Bravo bravo bravo!

review_title: Amazing.  The street food brought in
review_text:
This place is amazing. It actually is the street food brought inside the mall. An authentic Thai street food. So good and so cheap. 50 Bhat dishes 

It looks like a floating market and there is a huge Japanese section in the entrance.   

Absolutely amazing. Great for a rainy day
Interesting venue to display Thai culture and prices for food/drinks are kept affordable in a luxury mall!!.

review_title: All in one-stop-center for Thai food and souvenirs
review_text: When pressed for time and at lost on how to bargain, this area located on G floor of newly opened Icon Siam offer the best variety for sampling yummy Thai food and buying Thai handicrafts, reportedly from 77 provinces. Shopaholics and foodies would also love exploring the other floors of this impressive and artistic megamall.

If you’re in Icon Siam, make sure to check out Sook Siam because there’s so much food you can try at great price points. There’s all kinds of street food including mango sticky rice for 100 baht and different restaurants as well, all in a traditional Thai set up. Very cute!

review_title: Thai foods from 4 regions of Thailand within a day
review_text: SOOKSIAM is one of my favorite place. You can find a variety of local Thai foods from 4 regions of Thailand within a day. There are plenty of Thai food stalls, which irresistible to stop by. Strongly recommended you guys to drop by there.

review_title: Excellent presentation of clean street food
review_text: Perfect place to enjoy all verity of thai street food in a clean umbrella. highly recommended. UG floor. Don’t skip
SookSiam. The happiness land that have a lot of thing to do on leisure time. You can find a street food and tradition food around Thailand here. I love this place and will back again

review_text: this could be great but the staff there need training on customer services. one person ahead of me and 3 of the staff dealing with the same person. had to wait so long and once I finally got my place, ran out of maps as well.

entity_name: BTS Tourist Information Center Saphan Taksin
review_text:
This BTS station is always packed with people and it is really easy to get lost when you are surrounded my people. Below are some tips to help you go to the right destination without getting lost.:
1. Always follow the signs. if you got losted, you can always ask the staffs there. they are very knowledgeable and will tell you where to go
 2. You will suggest you to buy the rabbit card instead since the rabbit card can always be used no wonder you are. Also, when you leave The palace and and return to the station, it will be packed with people and thsoe people who are using the rabbit card will always be faster.
3. Bring your passport or id card whenever you buy your rabbit card
since they will require you to show it.

Hope these tips will help you on your way

entity_name: BTS Tourist Information Center Saphan Taksin
review_text: The BTS staff at the Saphan Taksin station were very knowledgeable and were able to help me with my train and river travel arrangements. The river is a short walk from the station. Most staff speak/understand English, are friendly and attentive. I thoroughly recommend them !

entity_name: BTS Tourist Information Center Saphan Taksin
review_text: Strategically located in a part of Saphan Taksin BTS station which gets lots of foot traffic, this tourist information office is easily accessible.  And with plenty of visitors intending to get to and from the Chao Phraya River, the office is able to provide loads of ideas of things to see and do, and how to get to various attractions.

entity_name: BTS Tourist Information Center Saphan Taksin
review_text: Stopped by on our way to the ferry station. The staff was very nice and helpful and explained the difference between the ferries and the prices.

entity_name: BTS Tourist Information Center Saphan Taksin
review_text: For using the boat ferries, you can use the BTS Skytrain and get down at the Saphan Taksin station. Its a short walk to the Pier where you can get in the boats. The tourist information center is helpful because there are public feries and there are private river boat tours. So you may be lured into buying a ticket for the private boat tour.

entity_name: BTS Tourist Information Center Saphan Taksin
review_text: Trip for chill for me. I take many photo around hare and eat noodle near BTS Saphan Taksin.It's good, delicious and cheap.
